EDCS-07800511.summary: This epitaph is dedicated to Sacrus, his wife Carssouna, and their son Quintus, with the latter part of the inscription in a mixture of Latin and Old English, likely an early Christian dedication.

EDCS-07800512.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Claudiae Crysidi vixit annos LXXXX heredes ponendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-07800512.translation: To the departed spirits: Claudia Crysidis, lived 90 years; her heirs arranged for this to be placed.
EDCS-07800512.summary: This epitaph honors Claudia Crysidis, who lived to 90 years old, with her heirs responsible for its erection.

EDCS-07800513.summary: This fragmentary inscription appears to be an epitaph for Fortunata, identifying her parent.

EDCS-07800515.text_edition: \n ex decurione alae II Astorum vixit annis LXX\n
EDCS-07800515.translation: Formerly decurion of the Second Ala of Asturians, lived 70 years.
EDCS-07800515.summary: This inscription fragment records the life and former rank of an individual who was a decurion in the Second Ala of Asturians and lived to 70.

EDCS-07800519.translation: The quarter of the HRPo Mercurenses.
EDCS-07800519.summary: This inscription records the name of a neighborhood or quarter, likely associated with the god Mercury, probably dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07800520.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, possibly a dedication or ownership marker, related to someone named Apollonius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07800523.text_edition: \n Deo Marti et Numinibus Augustorum Colasuni Bruccius et Caratius de suo donarunt ad sestertios nummos centum Celatus aerarius fecit et aeramenti libram donavit factam III\n
EDCS-07800523.translation: To the god Mars and the divine powers of the Emperors, Colasuni Bruccius and Caratius gave from their own funds 100 sesterces. Celatus the coppersmith made it and donated a pound of copper, made on the 3rd.
EDCS-07800523.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the god Mars and the Emperors by two individuals, Colasuni Bruccius and Caratius, with details about the craftsman and materials, probably dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07800527.text_edition: \n Deo Marti Braciacae Quintus Sittius Caecilianus praefectus cohortis I Aquitanorum votum solvit\n
EDCS-07800527.translation: To the god Mars Braciacus, Quintus Sittius Caecilianus, prefect of the First Cohort of Aquitanians, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07800527.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow to the god Mars Braciacus by Quintus Sittius Caecilianus, a prefect of a cohort, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07800528.text_edition: \n Cohortis I Frisiavonum centuria Valeri Vitalis\n
EDCS-07800528.translation: The century of the First Cohort of Frisiavones belonging to Valerius Vitalis.
EDCS-07800528.summary: This inscription marks a division within the First Cohort of Frisiavones, specifically the century of Valerius Vitalis, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07800530.text_edition: \n Deae Arnomecte Aelius Motio votum solvit laetus libens merito\n
EDCS-07800530.translation: To the goddess Arnomecta, Aelius Motio willingly, gladly, and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07800530.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow to the goddess Arnomecta by Aelius Motio, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07800540.text_edition: \n Tiberius Claudius Tirintius eques cohortis Thracum annorum LVII stipendiorum XX hic situs est\n
EDCS-07800540.translation: Tiberius Claudius Tirintius, a cavalryman of the Thracian cohort, aged 57, with 20 years of service, is buried here.
EDCS-07800540.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Tiberius Claudius Tirintius, a cavalryman of the Thracian cohort, recording his age and length of service, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07800541.text_edition: \n Titus Flaminius Titi Pollia Faventia annorum XXXXV stipendiorum XXII miles legionis XIIII geminae militavi aquilifer nunc hic sum hoc legite et felices vita plus minus este di uva vini et aqua prohibent ubi Tartara Ditis vivite dum sidus vitae dat tempus honeste\n
EDCS-07800541.translation: Titus Flaminius, son of Titus, of the Pollia tribe, from Faventia, aged 45, with 22 years of service, served as a standard-bearer with an eagle in the 14th Legion Gemina; now I am here. Read this, and may you be fortunate in life. Live well while life's star gives time for honor, for the gods forbid drunkenness with wine and water where the Underworld of Dis lies.
EDCS-07800541.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Titus Flaminius, a standard-bearer (aquilifer) of the 14th Legion Gemina, including his age, service years, origin, and a philosophical epitaph, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07800542.text_edition: \n Gaius Mannius Gai filius Pollia Secundus Pollentia miles legionis XX annorum LII stipendiorum XXXI beneficiarius legati praetorii hic situs est\n
EDCS-07800542.translation: Gaius Mannius Secundus, son of Gaius, of the Pollia tribe, from Pollentia, a soldier of the 20th Legion, aged 52, with 31 years of service, a subordinate (beneficiarius) of the praetorian legate, is buried here.
EDCS-07800542.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Gaius Mannius Secundus, a soldier and beneficiarius of a legate in the 20th Legion, recording his age, service years, origin, and place of burial, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07800543.text_edition: \n Marcus Petronius Luci filius Menenia Vicetia nnorum XXXVIII miles legionis XIIII Geminae militavit nnos XVIII signifer fuit hic situs est\n
EDCS-07800543.translation: Marcus Petronius, son of Lucius, of the Menenia tribe, from Vicetia, aged 38, served 18 years as a standard-bearer (signifer) in the 14th Legion Gemina; he is buried here.
EDCS-07800543.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Marcus Petronius, a standard-bearer (signifer) of the 14th Legion Gemina, recording his age, service years, origin, and place of burial, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07800544.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Placida annorum LV curam agente coniuge anno XXX Dis Manibus Deuccus annorum XV curam agente fratre\n
EDCS-07800544.translation: To the Divine Manes. Placidia, aged 55, with her husband acting as guardian. To the Divine Manes. Deuccus, aged 15, with his brother acting as guardian.
EDCS-07800544.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Placidia and Deuccus, recording their ages and the guardians responsible for them, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07800546.summary: This is a very brief funerary inscription, possibly for someone named 'esune', dedicated to the Divine Manes.

EDCS-07800547.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription with very little legible text, making it impossible to determine its content or purpose.

EDCS-07800548.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription containing parts of names, but its original context and purpose are lost.

EDCS-07800553.translation: Bellicus dedicated this altar to the god Trida.
EDCS-07800553.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription recording Bellicus's offering of an altar to the god Trida.

EDCS-07800554.text_edition: \n Deo Marti Nodonti Flavius Blandinus armatura votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-07800554.translation: Flavius Blandinus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to the god Mars Nodontus.
EDCS-07800554.summary: This is a votive inscription in which Flavius Blandinus fulfilled a vow to the god Mars Nodontus.

EDCS-07800555.text_edition: \n D<i=E>vo Nodenti Silvianus an<e=I>llum perdedit d<i=E>m<i=E>diam partem donavit Nodenti inter quibus nomen Seniciani <non illis=NOLLIS> pe<r=T>mittas sanitatem donec perferat usque templum Nodentis Rediviva \n
EDCS-07800555.translation: Silvianus lost his ring and dedicated half of it to Nodens, among whom the name of Senicianus is not permitted to have health until he brings it to the temple of Nodens Rediviva.
EDCS-07800555.summary: This complex inscription records Silvianus dedicating half of a lost ring to the god Nodens, with a curse invoked against Senicianus until a vow is fulfilled at the temple of Nodens Rediviva.

EDCS-07800556.text_edition: \n Pectillus votum quod promissit deo Nudente Marti dedit\n
EDCS-07800556.translation: Pectillus gave to the god Nudentus Mars the vow he had promised.
EDCS-07800556.summary: This is a votive inscription where Pectillus fulfilled a promised vow to the god Nudentus Mars.

EDCS-07800588.text_edition: \n Deo Marti Leno sive Ocelo Vellauno et numini Augusti Marcus Nonius Romanus ob immunitatem collegnii sic donum de suo dedit Glabrione et Homullo consulibus X Kalendas Septembres\n
EDCS-07800588.translation: Marcus Nonius Romanus gave this gift from his own resources to the god Mars Leno, also called Ocelus Vellaunus, and to the divine will of Augustus, because of the immunity of the guild, with Glabrio and Homullus as consuls, on the 10th day before the Kalends of September.
EDCS-07800588.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription by Marcus Nonius Romanus to Mars Leno/Ocelus Vellaunus and the Emperor's divinity, motivated by guild immunity, dated by consuls and a specific date in August.

EDCS-07800589.text_edition: \n Deo Marti Ocelo Aelius Augustinus optio votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-07800589.translation: Aelius Augustinus, optio, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to the god Mars Ocelus.
EDCS-07800589.summary: This is a votive inscription in which Aelius Augustinus, an optio, fulfilled a vow to the god Mars Ocelus.

EDCS-07800590.text_edition: \n legato legionis II Augustae proconsuli provinciae Narbonensis legato Augusti pro praetore provinciae Lugdunensis ex decreto ordinis res publica civitatis Silurum\n
EDCS-07800590.translation: As legate of the Second Augustan Legion, proconsul of the province of Narbonensis, legate of the Emperor with praetorian power of the province of Lugdunensis, by decree of the town council, for the republic of the citizens of Silurum.
EDCS-07800590.summary: This inscription records an honorific monument dedicated to a high-ranking official by the community of the Silures, likely during the late 1st or early 2nd century AD.

EDCS-07800595.text_edition: \n Titus Flavius Postumius Varus vir clarissimus legatus templum Dianae restituit\n
EDCS-07800595.translation: Titus Flavius Postumius Varus, a most distinguished man, as legate, restored the temple of Diana.
EDCS-07800595.summary: This inscription records the restoration of the temple of Diana by Titus Flavius Postumius Varus, a prominent figure serving as a legate, likely in the 2nd century AD.

EDCS-07800596.text_edition: \n Deae Fortunae Iulius Bassus praefectus castrorum\n
EDCS-07800596.translation: To the goddess Fortuna, Julius Bassus, prefect of the camp.
EDCS-07800596.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the goddess Fortuna by Julius Bassus, a camp prefect, likely from the Roman army during the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-07800597.text_edition: \n Fortune et Bono Evento Cornelius Castus et Iulia Belismicus coniuges posuerunt\n
EDCS-07800597.translation: To Fortune and Good Fortune, Cornelius Castus and Iulia Belismicus, husband and wife, erected this.
EDCS-07800597.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the gods Fortune and Good Fortune by a married couple, Cornelius Castus and Iulia Belismicus, probably in the 2nd century AD.

EDCS-07800599.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno Fronto Aemilianus Calpurnius Rufilianus legatus Augustorum monitu\n
EDCS-07800599.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus, Frontus Aemilianus Calpurnius Rufinianus, legate of the Emperors, by their instruction.
EDCS-07800599.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Dolichenus by Frontus Aemilianus Calpurnius Rufinianus, an imperial legate, likely in the late 2nd or early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07800600.text_edition: \n Deo Mercurio Cur dono dedit Severus posuit\n
EDCS-07800600.translation: To the god Mercury, Severus erected this, and gave it as a gift.
EDCS-07800600.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the god Mercury by Severus, who set up the monument and donated it, likely in the 2nd century AD.

EDCS-07800601.text_edition: \n Invicto Mithrae s Iustus legionis II Augustae bene merenti fecit\n
EDCS-07800601.translation: To the Invincible Mithras, Iustus of the Second Augustan Legion made this for his well-deserving god.
EDCS-07800601.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the god Mithras by Iustus, a soldier of the Second Augustan Legion, likely in the 2nd century AD.

EDCS-07800603.text_edition: \n Saluti Reginae Publius Sallienius Publi filius Maecia Thalamus Hadria praefectus legionis II Augustae cum filiis suis Ampeiano et Luciliano dono dedit\n
EDCS-07800603.translation: For the safety of our Emperors, Publius Saliienus Publi son Maeicia Thalamus Hadria, prefect of the Second Augustan Legion, dedicated this with his sons Ampeianus and Lucilianus.
EDCS-07800603.summary: This inscription records a dedication made for the safety of the emperors by Publius Saliienus Hadria, a prefect of the Second Augustan Legion, along with his sons, likely during the Severan period (early 3rd century AD).

EDCS-07800605.text_edition: \n pro salute Augustorum Nostrorum Severi et Antonini et Getae Caesaris Publius Saliienus Publi filius Maeicia Thalamus Hadria praefectus legionis II Augustae cum Ampeiano et Luciliano\n
EDCS-07800605.translation: For the safety of our Emperors Severus and Antoninus and Caesar Geta, Publius Saliienus Publi son Maeicia Thalamus Hadria, prefect of the Second Augustan Legion, with Ampeianus and Lucilianus.
EDCS-07800605.summary: This inscription records a dedication for the safety of Emperors Severus, Antoninus (Caracalla), and Geta, made by Publius Saliienus Hadria, prefect of the Second Augustan Legion, and his sons, dating to the Severan period (early 3rd century AD).

EDCS-07800606.text_edition: \n Numinibus Augustorum Genio legionis II Augustae in honorem aquilae primus pilus dono dedit dedicatum VIIII Kalendas Octobres Peregrino et Aemiliano consulibus curante Urso actario legionis eiusdem\n
EDCS-07800606.translation: To the divine powers of the Emperors, to the genius of the Second Augustan Legion, in honor of the eagle, the primus pilus donated this; dedicated on the 23rd day before the Kalends of October, when Peregrinus and Aemilianus were consuls, under the care of Ursus, a staff officer of the same legion.
EDCS-07800606.summary: This inscription records a dedication by a primus pilus (chief centurion) of the Second Augustan Legion to the imperial divinities and the legion's genius, with the date specified by consuls and a staff officer's supervision, likely in the early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07800609.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari divi Nervae filio Nervae Traiano Augusto Germanico pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate patri patriae consuli III legio II Augusta\n
EDCS-07800609.translation: To Emperor Caesar Nerva Trajan Augustus Germanicus, son of the deified Nerva, pontifex maximus, holder of tribunician power, father of the fatherland, consul for the third time, by Legio II Augusta.
EDCS-07800609.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Trajan, likely made by Legio II Augusta around the time of his third consulship (102 CE).

EDCS-07800611.translation: Fulfilled the vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-07800611.summary: This brief inscription records the fulfillment of a vow, with no specific dedicant or recipient identified.

EDCS-07800613.text_edition: \n Imperatores Valerianus et Gallienus Augusti et Valerianus nobilissimus Caesar cohorti VII centurias a solo restituerunt per Desticium Iubam virum clarissimum legatum Augustorum pro praetore et Vitulasium Laetinianum legatum legionis II Augustae curante Domitio Potentino praefecto legionis eiusdem\n
EDCS-07800613.translation: Emperors Valerian and Gallienus Augusti and Valerian nobilissimus Caesar rebuilt the cohort VII centuria from the ground up, through Desticius Iuba, vir clarissimus, imperial legate with praetorian authority, and Vitulasian Laetinianus, legate of Legio II Augusta, under the supervision of Domitius Potentinus, prefect of the same legion.
EDCS-07800613.summary: This inscription records the rebuilding of a centuria by Cohors VII, commissioned by Emperors Valerian and Gallienus and overseen by imperial officials around the time of their joint rule (c. 253-260 CE).

EDCS-07800614.text_edition: \n isus Claudius Aemilius Quintus Iulius Haterianus legatus Augusti pro praetore provinciae Ciliciae\n
EDCS-07800614.translation: Claudius Aemilius Quintus Iulius Haterianus, imperial legate with praetorian authority of the province of Cilicia.
EDCS-07800614.summary: This is likely an official's titulature, identifying Claudius Aemilius Quintus Iulius Haterianus as the imperial legate pro praetore of Cilicia, probably from the 3rd century CE.

EDCS-07800616.translation: Centuria of the II Cohort, belonging to Valerius Flaccus.
EDCS-07800616.summary: This inscription records ownership or dedication of a centuria of Cohors II to Valerius Flaccus.

EDCS-07800619.translation: Centuria of the V Cohort, belonging to Paetinus.
EDCS-07800619.summary: This inscription records ownership or dedication of a centuria of Cohors V to Paetinus.

EDCS-07800620.text_edition: \n Cohortis VI hastati prioris centuria Roesi Moderati\n
EDCS-07800620.translation: Centuria of the VI Cohort, belonging to Roesus Moderatus, the 'hastatus prior'.
EDCS-07800620.summary: This inscription records ownership or dedication of a specific centuria within Cohors VI to Roesus Moderatus.

EDCS-07800623.summary: This inscription records ownership or dedication of a centuria to Claudius Cupitus.

EDCS-07800625.summary: This inscription records ownership or dedication of a centuria to Gaius Iulius Caecinianus.

EDCS-07800630.summary: This inscription records ownership or dedication of a centuria to Valerius Maximus.

EDCS-07800631.translation: Of the eighth cohort, under the centuria of Valerius Maxsimus.
EDCS-07800631.summary: This inscription appears to record a military unit affiliation or a division within the army, possibly related to a tomb or monument.

EDCS-07800634.summary: This is a brief epitaph identifying an individual named Primus, who held the military rank of tesserarius, possibly indicating a tomb marker.

EDCS-07800635.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelius Herculanus aeques vixit annos XXVIII coniux faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-07800635.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aurelius Herculanus, an equestrian, lived 28 years. His wife arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-07800635.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aurelius Herculanus, an equestrian who died at 28, commissioned by his wife.

EDCS-07800636.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Titus Flavius Candidus Ulpia Traiana miles legionis II Augustae stipendiorum VII annorum XXVII frater curavit\n
EDCS-07800636.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Titus Flavius Candidus, a soldier of the Second Augusta Legion, with 7 years of service, aged 27. His brother arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-07800636.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Titus Flavius Candidus, a soldier of the Second Augusta Legion who died at 27 after 7 years of service, commissioned by his brother.

EDCS-07800637.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Titus Flavius Natalis veteranus vixit annos LXV curatum per Flavium Ingenuinum et Flavium Flavinum filios et Flaviam Veldiccam coniugem\n
EDCS-07800637.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Titus Flavius Natalis, a veteran, lived 65 years. Care was taken by his sons, Flavius Ingenuinus and Flavius Flavinus, and his wife, Flavia Veldicca.
EDCS-07800637.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Titus Flavius Natalis, a veteran who lived to 65, commissioned by his children and wife.

EDCS-07800638.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Gai Iuli Decumini veterani legionis II Augustae annorum XXXXV coniunx faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-07800638.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Gaius Julius Decuminus, a veteran of the Second Augusta Legion, aged 45. His wife arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-07800638.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Julius Decuminus, a veteran of the Second Augusta Legion who died at 45, commissioned by his wife.

EDCS-07800639.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Iulius Iulianus miles legionis II Augustae stipendiorum XVIII nnum XL hic situs est curam agente Amanda coniuge\n
EDCS-07800639.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Julius Julianus, a soldier of the Second Augusta Legion, with 18 years of service, aged 40, lies here. His wife Amanda took care of this.
EDCS-07800639.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Julius Julianus, a soldier of the Second Augusta Legion who died at 40 after 18 years of service, commissioned by his wife.

EDCS-07800640.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Quinti Iuli Severi Dinia veterani legionis II Augustae coniux faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-07800640.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Quintus Julius Severus Dinia, a veteran of the Second Augusta Legion. His wife arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-07800640.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Julius Severus Dinia, a veteran of the Second Augusta Legion, commissioned by his wife.

EDCS-07800641.text_edition: \n IV L Iulius Carius filius vixit nnos XXV optio annos II faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-07800641.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Julius Carus, son, lived 25 years, optio. He arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-07800641.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Julius Carus, an optio who died at 25, who commissioned his own monument.

EDCS-07800642.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Iulius Valens veteranus legionis II Augustae vixit annis C Iulia Secundina coniunx et Iulius Martinus filius faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-07800642.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Julius Valens, a veteran of the Second Augusta Legion, lived 100 years. His wife Iulia Secundina and his son Iulius Martinus arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-07800642.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Julius Valens, a veteran of the Second Augusta Legion who lived to 100, commissioned by his wife and son.

EDCS-07800644.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Gaius Valerius Gai filius Galeria Victor Lugduni signifer legionis II Augustae stipendiorum XVII nnum XLV curam agente Annio Perpetuo herede\n
EDCS-07800644.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gaius Valerius, son of Gaius, of the Galeria tribe, Victor from Lugdunum, standard-bearer of the Legio II Augusta, with 17 years of service, aged 45, with Annio Perpetuo acting as administrator as heir.
EDCS-07800644.summary: This is a tombstone for Gaius Valerius, a standard-bearer in Legio II Augusta, who died at age 45, with his heir managing the tomb.

EDCS-07800645.text_edition: \n Vindutius legionis II Augustae nnum XLV\n
EDCS-07800645.translation: Vindutius of the Legio II Augusta, aged 45.
EDCS-07800645.summary: This is a tombstone for Vindutius, a soldier of Legio II Augusta, who died at age 45.

EDCS-07800648.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Tadia Vallaunius vixit nnos LXV et Tadius Exuperatus filius vixit nnos XXXVII defunctus expeditione Germanica Tadia Exuperata filia matri et fratri piissima secus tumulum patris posuit\n
EDCS-07800648.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Tadia Vallaunius lived 65 years and Tadius Exuperatus, her son, lived 37 years, having died on campaign in Germania. Tadia Exuperata, his daughter, most devotedly placed this by the tomb of her mother and brother.
EDCS-07800648.summary: This is a tombstone commemorating Tadia Vallaunius and her son Tadius Exuperatus, who died in military service, erected by his devoted daughter.

EDCS-07800649.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Vitali vixit annos XII\n
EDCS-07800649.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Vitalis lived 12 years.
EDCS-07800649.summary: This is a tombstone for Vitalis, a child who died at age 12.

EDCS-07800650.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caesoria Corotica vixit annos XLVIII Renatus coniux eius et Munatius et Lestinus et Leontius fecerunt fili eius\n
EDCS-07800650.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Caesoria Corotica lived 48 years. Renatus, her husband, and Munatius, and Lestinus, and Leontius, her sons, made this.
EDCS-07800650.summary: This tombstone honors Caesoria Corotica, who died at age 48, and was made by her husband and sons.

EDCS-07800651.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Iuliae Nundinae vixit annos XXX Agrius Cimarus coniunx piissimus faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-07800651.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Iulia Nundina lived 30 years. Agrius Cimarus, her most devoted husband, saw to its construction.
EDCS-07800651.summary: This tombstone commemorates Iulia Nundina, who died at age 30, and was erected by her devoted husband.

EDCS-07800652.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus et memoriae Iuliae Secundinae matri piissime vixit annis LXXV Gaius Iulius Martinus filius faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-07800652.translation: To the spirits of the departed and for the memory of Iulia Secundina, my most devoted mother, who lived 75 years. Gaius Iulius Martinus, her son, saw to its construction.
EDCS-07800652.summary: This tombstone honors Iulia Secundina, who died at age 75, and was erected by her devoted son.

EDCS-07800653.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Iulie Senice vicsit annos LX\n
EDCS-07800653.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Iulia Senice lived 60 years.
EDCS-07800653.summary: This is a tombstone for Iulia Senice, who died at age 60.

EDCS-07800654.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Iulia Veneria annorum XXXII Iulius Alesander coniunx pientissimus et Iulius Belicianus filius monimentum faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-07800654.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Iulia Veneria, aged 32. Iulius Alesander, her most devoted husband, and Iulius Belicianus, her son, saw to the construction of this monument.
EDCS-07800654.summary: This tombstone honors Iulia Veneria, who died at age 32, and was erected by her devoted husband and son.

EDCS-07800655.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Iulia iunda vixit annos XXXV\n
EDCS-07800655.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Iulia Iunda lived 35 years.
EDCS-07800655.summary: This is a tombstone for Iulia Iunda, who died at age 35.

EDCS-07800656.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Iulia Iberna vixsit annos XVI messes XI faciendum curavit Flavia Flavina mater\n
EDCS-07800656.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Iulia Iberna lived 16 years and 11 months. Her mother, Flavia Flavina, saw to the making of this monument.
EDCS-07800656.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulia Iberna, erected by her mother Flavia Flavina, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07800661.text_edition: \n Iuccittia vixit annos LXXX curam egit I\n
EDCS-07800661.translation: Iuccittia lived 80 years. She took care of [---].
EDCS-07800661.summary: A fragmentary funerary epitaph for Iuccittia, recording her age and an incomplete description of her responsibilities, likely Roman Imperial.

EDCS-07800670.summary: A very fragmentary inscription mentioning the name Quintus Iulius, possibly part of a dedication or epitaph, Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07800676.text_edition: \n vixit annos III quinque Secundus fecit legionis II Augustae faciendum curavit a mater filio fecit P M\n
EDCS-07800676.translation: Secundus made this for his son. He lived 3 years and 5 months. His mother had it made. With full rites.
EDCS-07800676.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young boy, erected by his mother on behalf of his father Secundus, possibly associated with the Legio II Augusta, Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07800684.text_edition: \n vixi AL - N --- IVI --- coniunx eius hic situs est\n
EDCS-07800684.translation: I lived [---] years. His wife is buried here.
EDCS-07800684.summary: A fragmentary epitaph stating the deceased lived a certain number of years and that his wife is buried with him, Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07800685.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Valeri Primi et fili equitis alae NER optionis heres faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-07800685.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Valerius Primus, son of [---], an eques of the ala [---], an optio, his heir saw to the making of this monument.
EDCS-07800685.summary: A funerary epitaph for Valerius Primus, a cavalry officer (eques, optio) in an auxiliary unit, erected by his heir, likely Roman Imperial.

EDCS-07800689.summary: A brief inscription recording a centuria and a name, Marcus Ennius Primus, possibly related to military organization or property division, Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07800695.summary: An inscription recording a centuria (a division of land or a unit) named Marci and a measurement of 46 feet, likely related to land surveying or military demarcation, Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07800696.summary: An inscription identifying a centuria named Valeri Flavini, possibly related to land division or ownership, Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07800698.text_edition: \n Iulius Gaveronis filius fecit miles cohortis I Nerviorum\n
EDCS-07800698.translation: Iulius Gaveronis, son of [---], made this. Soldier of the Cohors I Nerviorum.
EDCS-07800698.summary: An inscription by Iulius Gaveronis, a soldier in the I Cohort of Nervii, recording his dedication or activity, likely Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07800701.summary: This inscription marks a land division or boundary belonging to the century of Iulius Mansuetus.

EDCS-07800702.summary: This inscription likely records a measurement of land, 39 feet, within the area of the century of Mansuetus.

EDCS-07800703.translation: The century of Iulius Perpetuus, 21 feet.
EDCS-07800703.summary: This inscription indicates a land measurement of 21 feet within the century of Iulius Perpetuus.

EDCS-07800704.translation: The century of Iulius Perpetuus, 39 feet.
EDCS-07800704.summary: This inscription notes a land measurement of 39 feet belonging to the century of Iulius Perpetuus.

EDCS-07800705.summary: This inscription records a land measurement of 20 feet associated with the century of Perpetuus.

EDCS-07800706.summary: This inscription marks a land division of 21 feet within the century of Perpetuus.

EDCS-07800707.summary: This inscription indicates a land measurement of 22 feet in the century of Perpetuus.

EDCS-07800708.summary: This fragmentary inscription refers to the century of Marcuss, with missing information regarding its size or purpose.

EDCS-07800709.text_edition: \n Deae Minervae Aurelius Sabinianus actarius votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-07800709.translation: Aurelius Sabinianus, the record keeper, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to the goddess Minerva.
EDCS-07800709.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the goddess Minerva by Aurelius Sabinianus, who held the position of 'actarius', likely in the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-07800721.translation: The century of Rufus Sabinus, GNATLV...
EDCS-07800721.summary: This inscription pertains to the century of Rufus Sabinus and includes an incomplete word, likely indicating a measurement or description of the land.

EDCS-07800725.text_edition: \n Fortunae Reduci Aesculapio et Saluti eius liberti et familia Titi Pomponi Titi fili Galeria Mamiliani Rufi Antistiani Funisulani Vettoniani legati Augusti dederunt dedicaverunt\n
EDCS-07800725.translation: To Fortuna Redux, Aesculapius, and her health, the freedmen and household of Titus Pomponius Mamilianus Rufus Antistianus Vettonianus, son of Titus, of the Galeria tribe, legate of Augustus, gave and dedicated.
EDCS-07800725.summary: This inscription records a dedication by freedmen and household to deities Fortuna Redux, Aesculapius, and Salus, likely during the career of the legate Titus Pomponius Mamilianus Rufus Antistianus Vettonianus.

EDCS-07800727.translation: To the genius of the Aurelian century, Julius Quintilianus.
EDCS-07800727.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the genius of a specific century, the Aurelian century, by Julius Quintilianus.

EDCS-07800728.text_edition: \n Genio sancto centurie sic Aelius Claudianus optio votum solvit\n
EDCS-07800728.translation: To the sacred genius of the century, Aelius Claudianus, optio, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07800728.summary: This inscription records an optio named Aelius Claudianus fulfilling a vow to the sacred genius of his century.

EDCS-07800730.text_edition: \n Pro salute Dominorum Nostrorum Invictissimorum Augustorum Genio loci Flavius Longus tribunus militum legionis XX Valeriae victricis et Longinus filius eius domo Samosata votum solverunt\n
EDCS-07800730.translation: For the safety of our Lords, the Invictissimorum Augustorum, Flavius Longus, tribune of the soldiers of the Twentieth Legion Valeria Victrix, and his son Longinus, from Samosata, fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-07800730.summary: This inscription records Flavius Longus, a military tribune of the XX Valeria Victrix legion, and his son Longinus fulfilling a vow for the safety of the Emperors.

EDCS-07800731.text_edition: \n Genio signiferorum legionis XX Valeriae victricis Titus Flavius Valerianus collegis donum dedit\n
EDCS-07800731.translation: To the genius of the signiferi of the Twentieth Legion Valeria Victrix, Titus Flavius Valerianus gave a gift to his colleagues.
EDCS-07800731.summary: This inscription records Titus Flavius Valerianus, likely a signifer himself, presenting a gift to his colleagues representing the genius of the signiferi of the XX Valeria Victrix legion.

EDCS-07800732.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Tanaro Lucius Elufrius Galeria Praesens Clunia princeps legionis XX Valeriae victricis Commodo et Laterano consulibus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-07800732.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Tanaro, Lucius Elufrius Galeria Praesens, princeps of the Twentieth Legion Valeria Victrix, in the consulship of Commodus and Lateranus, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07800732.summary: This inscription records Lucius Elufrius Galeria Praesens, a princeps in the XX Valeria Victrix legion, fulfilling a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Tanaro during the consulship of Commodus and Lateranus.

EDCS-07800734.summary: This fragmentary inscription records a dedication to the god Mars Conservator.

EDCS-07800737.text_edition: \n Deae Minervae Furius Fortunatus magister primus votum solvit\n
EDCS-07800737.translation: To the goddess Minerva, Furius Fortunatus, first magister, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07800737.summary: This inscription records Furius Fortunatus, holding the position of first magister, fulfilling a vow to the goddess Minerva.

EDCS-07800754.translation: Of the century of Atti Celer.
EDCS-07800754.summary: This inscription likely marks the territory or belongs to the century of a centurion named Atti Celer.

EDCS-07800755.translation: Of the century of Titus Flavius Cicatricula.
EDCS-07800755.summary: This inscription likely marks the territory or belongs to the century of a centurion named Titus Flavius Cicatricula.

EDCS-07800756.summary: This inscription identifies the century belonging to Quintus Maximus, likely marking a military unit's division.

EDCS-07800757.translation: The century of Quintus Terentius N.
EDCS-07800757.summary: This inscription identifies the century belonging to Quintus Terentius N., likely marking a military unit's division.

EDCS-07800758.text_edition: \n Gaius Calventius Gai filius Claudia Celer Apro miles legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis centuria Vibi Clementis \n
EDCS-07800758.translation: Gaius Calventius, son of Gaius, of the Claudia tribe, Celer Apro, a soldier of the Second Adiutrix Pia Fidelis legion, in the century of Vibius Clemens.
EDCS-07800758.summary: This inscription records Gaius Calventius, a soldier of the Second Adiutrix legion, identifying his tribe, cognomen, and centurion.

EDCS-07800759.text_edition: \n Gaius Iuventius Gai Claudia Capito Apro miles legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis centuria Iuli Clementis nnorum XL stipendiorum XVII\n
EDCS-07800759.translation: Gaius Iuventius, son of Gaius, of the Claudia tribe, Capito Apro, a soldier of the Second Adiutrix Pia Fidelis legion, in the century of Iulius Clemens, aged 40, with 17 years of service.
EDCS-07800759.summary: This inscription records Gaius Iuventius, a soldier of the Second Adiutrix legion, specifying his age, service length, tribe, and centurion.

EDCS-07800760.text_edition: \n Lucius Terentius Claudia Fuscus Apro miles legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis \n
EDCS-07800760.translation: Lucius Terentius, of the Claudia tribe, Fuscus Apro, a soldier of the Second Adiutrix Pia Fidelis legion.
EDCS-07800760.summary: This inscription identifies Lucius Terentius, a soldier of the Second Adiutrix legion, noting his tribe and cognomen.

EDCS-07800761.text_edition: \n Gaio Valerio Crispo veterano ex legione II adiutrice pia fideli\n
EDCS-07800761.translation: To Gaius Valerius Crispus, a veteran from the Second Adiutrix Pia Fidelis legion.
EDCS-07800761.summary: This is a dedication to Gaius Valerius Crispus, a veteran of the Second Adiutrix legion.

EDCS-07800762.text_edition: \n Quintus Valerius Quinti filius Claudia Fronto Celea miles legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis annorum L stipendiorum XXV\n
EDCS-07800762.translation: Quintus Valerius, son of Quintus, of the Claudia tribe, Fronto Celea, a soldier of the Second Adiutrix Pia Fidelis legion, aged 50, with 25 years of service.
EDCS-07800762.summary: This inscription records Quintus Valerius, a soldier of the Second Adiutrix legion, detailing his age, service length, tribe, and cognomen.

EDCS-07800763.text_edition: \n Lucius Valerius Luci filius Claudia Seneca Savaria miles legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis \n
EDCS-07800763.translation: Lucius Valerius, son of Lucius, of the Claudia tribe, Seneca Savaria, a soldier of the Second Adiutrix Pia Fidelis legion.
EDCS-07800763.summary: This inscription identifies Lucius Valerius, a soldier of the Second Adiutrix legion, noting his tribe, cognomen, and place of origin.

EDCS-07800764.text_edition: \n inus eques legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis centuria Petroni Fidi stipendiorum IXI annorum XXV hic sepultus est\n
EDCS-07800764.translation: [...inus], an equestrian of the Second Adiutrix Pia Fidelis legion, in the century of Petronius Fidus, with 9 years of service, aged 25, is buried here.
EDCS-07800764.summary: This funerary inscription records an equestrian soldier of the Second Adiutrix legion, providing his service length, age, and centurion.

EDCS-07800765.text_edition: \n Voltimesis Pudens Gai filius Sergia Augusta eques legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis annorum XXXII stipendiorum XIII hic situs est\n
EDCS-07800765.translation: Voltimesis Pudens, son of Gaius, of the Sergia tribe, Augusta, an equestrian of the Second Adiutrix Pia Fidelis legion, aged 32, with 13 years of service, is laid here.
EDCS-07800765.summary: This funerary inscription records Voltimesis Pudens, an equestrian soldier of the Second Adiutrix legion, noting his age, service length, tribe, and cognomen.

EDCS-07800769.text_edition: \n Sextus Epidius Luci filius Tromentina Pudens Aequo \n
EDCS-07800769.translation: Sextus Epidius Pudens, son of Lucius, of the Tromentina tribe, to Aequus.
EDCS-07800769.summary: This is a dedication by Sextus Epidius Pudens to Aequus, likely on a tombstone or monument.

EDCS-07800770.text_edition: \n Lucius Annius Luci filius Tromentina Marcellus Veiis\n
EDCS-07800770.translation: Lucius Annius Marcellus, son of Lucius, of the Tromentina tribe, from Veii.
EDCS-07800770.summary: This inscription records the name and tribal affiliation of Lucius Annius Marcellus, possibly for funerary or civic purposes.

EDCS-07800771.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Gabinius Felix miles legionis II Augustae Antoninianae vixsit sic annis XXXX heres ponendum curavit\n
EDCS-07800771.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gabinius Felix, a soldier of the II Augusta Antoniniana legion, lived for 40 years; his heir arranged for this to be set up.
EDCS-07800771.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gabinius Felix, a soldier of the II Augusta Antoniniana legion, who died at the age of 40.

EDCS-07800772.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Anni Cestius miles legionis XX Valeriae victricis annorum XIII \n
EDCS-07800772.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Cestius, a soldier of the XX Valeria Victrix legion, aged 13.
EDCS-07800772.summary: This funerary inscription records the name and young age of Cestius, a soldier of the XX Valeria Victrix legion.

EDCS-07800773.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marcus Aurelius Alexander praefectus castrorum legionis XX Valeriae victricis natione Syrus Osroenus vixit annos LXXII c yces et S\n
EDCS-07800773.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Aurelius Alexander, camp prefect of the XX Valeria Victrix legion, a Syris of Osrhoene by birth, lived for 72 years...
EDCS-07800773.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Marcus Aurelius Alexander, a high-ranking camp prefect of the XX Valeria Victrix legion, who died at age 72.

EDCS-07800774.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marcus Aurelius Nepos |(centurio) legionis XX Valeriae victricis coniux pientissima faciendum curavit vixit annis L sub ascia dedicatum\n
EDCS-07800774.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Aurelius Nepos, centurion of the XX Valeria Victrix legion, a most devoted wife arranged for this to be made. She lived 50 years, dedicated under the ax.
EDCS-07800774.summary: This funerary inscription commemorates Marcus Aurelius Nepos, a centurion of the XX Valeria Victrix legion, and notes that it was erected by his wife.

EDCS-07800775.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caecilius Avitus Emerita Augusta optio legionis XX Valeriae victricis stipendiorum XV vixit annos XXXIIII heres faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-07800775.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caecilius Avitus, from Emerita Augusta, an optio in the XX Valeria Victrix legion with 15 years of service, lived for 34 years; his heir arranged for this to be set up.
EDCS-07800775.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Caecilius Avitus, an optio in the XX Valeria Victrix legion with 15 years of service, who died at age 34.

EDCS-07800776.text_edition: \n Lucius Caiatius Luci filius Galeria Sextinus Lugduno miles legionis XX Valeriae victricis \n
EDCS-07800776.translation: Lucius Caiatius Sextinus, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, from Lugdunum, a soldier of the XX Valeria Victrix legion.
EDCS-07800776.summary: This inscription identifies Lucius Caiatius Sextinus, a soldier of the XX Valeria Victrix legion, noting his origin from Lugdunum.

EDCS-07800777.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Gaius Cestius Teurnicus annorum XXX miles legionis XX Valeriae victricis stipendiorum X heres faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-07800777.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Cestius Teurnicus, aged 30, a soldier of the XX Valeria Victrix legion with 10 years of service; his heir arranged for this to be set up.
EDCS-07800777.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Gaius Cestius Teurnicus, a soldier of the XX Valeria Victrix legion, who died at age 30 after 10 years of service.

EDCS-07800778.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Lucius Ecimius Bellicianus Vitalis veteranus legionis XX Valeriae victricis hic sepelitus\n
EDCS-07800778.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Ecimius Bellicianus Vitalis, a veteran of the XX Valeria Victrix legion, is buried here.
EDCS-07800778.summary: This funerary inscription marks the burial place of Lucius Ecimius Bellicianus Vitalis, a veteran of the XX Valeria Victrix legion.

EDCS-07800780.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Furi Maximi militis legionis XX Valeriae victricis stipendiorum XXII heres faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-07800780.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. His heir had this made for Furius Maximus, a soldier of the Twentieth Legion Valerius Victrix, with twenty-two years of service.
EDCS-07800780.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated to Furius Maximus, a soldier of the Twentieth Legion Valerius Victrix, by his heir.

EDCS-07800781.text_edition: \n Gaius Iulius Gai Claudia Quartus Celeia miles legionis XX Valeriae victricis centuria Titi Flavi Pro annorum XXX stipendiorum VII Rusius Mo N\n
EDCS-07800781.translation: Gaius Iulius Quartus, son of Gaius and Claudia, of Celeia, soldier of the Twentieth Legion Valerius Victrix, in the century of Titus Flavius Pro, aged thirty years, with seven years of service.
EDCS-07800781.summary: This funerary inscription records Gaius Iulius Quartus, a soldier of the Twentieth Legion Valerius Victrix, who died at the age of 30 after 7 years of service.

EDCS-07800782.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Gaius Iulius Severus eques legionis XX Valeriae victricis vixit annos XXXX \n
EDCS-07800782.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Gaius Iulius Severus, an equestrian soldier of the Twentieth Legion Valerius Victrix, lived 40 years.
EDCS-07800782.summary: This funerary inscription records Gaius Iulius Severus, an equestrian soldier of the Twentieth Legion Valerius Victrix, who died at the age of 40.

EDCS-07800783.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Lucius Licinius Luci Teretina Valens Arelate veteranus legionis XX Valeriae victricis annorum VL heres faciundum f curavit hic situs est\n
EDCS-07800783.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius Licinius Valens, son of Lucius, of the Teretina tribe, from Arles, a veteran of the Twentieth Legion Valerius Victrix, aged 45 years, his heir had this made. He is buried here.
EDCS-07800783.summary: This funerary monument records Lucius Licinius Valens, a veteran of the Twentieth Legion Valerius Victrix, who died at the age of 45.

EDCS-07800784.text_edition: \n Gaius Lovesius Papiria Cadarus Emerita miles legionis XX Valeriae victricis annorum XXV stipendiorum IIX Frontinius Aquilo heres faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-07800784.translation: Gaius Lovesius Cadarus, son of Papirius, of Emerita, soldier of the Twentieth Legion Valerius Victrix, aged twenty-five years, with eight years of service. Frontinius Aquilo, his heir, had this made.
EDCS-07800784.summary: This funerary inscription records Gaius Lovesius Cadarus, a soldier of the Twentieth Legion Valerius Victrix, who died at the age of 25 after 8 years of service, and was commemorated by his heir.

EDCS-07800785.text_edition: \n Quintus Postumius Quinti filius Papiria Solus Emerita miles legionis XX primi pili nnorum XXXV stipendiorum XXI hic situs est\n
EDCS-07800785.translation: Quintus Postumius Solus, son of Quintus, of the Papiria tribe, from Emerita, a soldier of the Twentieth Legion, primipilaris, aged thirty-five years, with twenty-one years of service. He is buried here.
EDCS-07800785.summary: This funerary inscription records Quintus Postumius Solus, a primipilaris soldier of the Twentieth Legion, who died at the age of 35 after 21 years of service.

EDCS-07800786.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Publio Rustio Fabia Crescenti Brixia militi legionis XX Valeriae victricis annorum XXX stipendiorum X Groma heres faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-07800786.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Groma had this made for Publius Rustius Crescenti, of Brixia, soldier of the Twentieth Legion Valerius Victrix, aged thirty years, with ten years of service.
EDCS-07800786.summary: This funerary monument is dedicated to Publius Rustius Crescenti, a soldier of the Twentieth Legion Valerius Victrix, by his heir Groma.

EDCS-07800787.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marcus Sextius Marci filius Claudia Bellicus Claudia Celeia annorum XX stipendiorum\n
EDCS-07800787.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Sextius Bellicus, son of Marcus, aged twenty years, with [years] of service.
EDCS-07800787.summary: This fragmentary funerary inscription records Marcus Sextius Bellicus, who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-07800788.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Titinius Felix beneficiarius legati legionis XX Valeriae victricis militavit annos XXII vixit annos XLV Iulia Similina coniux et heres \n
EDCS-07800788.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Titinius Felix, a beneficiarius (staff officer) to the legate of the Twentieth Legion Valerius Victrix, served for twenty-two years and lived for forty-five years. His wife and heir, Iulia Similina.
EDCS-07800788.summary: This funerary monument records Titinius Felix, a staff officer for the legate of the Twentieth Legion Valerius Victrix, who died at the age of 45 after 22 years of service, and was commemorated by his wife and heir.

EDCS-07800789.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marcus Ulpius Marci filius Ulpia Ianuarius Traiana stipendiorum XVIIII annorum XXXVII heres faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-07800789.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. His heir had this made for Marcus Ulpius Ianuarius, son of Marcus, of Trajana, aged thirty-seven years, with nineteen years of service.
EDCS-07800789.summary: This funerary inscription records Marcus Ulpius Ianuarius, who died at the age of 37 after 19 years of service, and was commemorated by his heir.

EDCS-07800790.text_edition: \n Cocceiae Irene coniugi castissimae et sanctissimae vixit annos XXX mensem Gaius Valerius Iustus actarius legionis XX posuit\n
EDCS-07800790.translation: To Cocceia Irene, his most chaste and most holy wife, who lived 30 years. Gaius Valerius Iustus, a record-keeper of the 20th Legion, erected this.
EDCS-07800790.summary: A funerary monument set up by Gaius Valerius Iustus for his wife Cocceia Irene, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-07800791.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Quintus Vibius Secundus Anniensis Cremona miles legionis XX Valeriae victricis centuria Octaviani \n
EDCS-07800791.translation: To the Divine Shades. Quintus Vibius Secundus of Aniensis, from Cremona, a soldier of the 20th Valeria Victrix Legion, of the centuria of Octavianus.
EDCS-07800791.summary: A funerary inscription for Quintus Vibius Secundus, a soldier of the 20th Legion, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-07800792.text_edition: \n equo publico |(centurio) legionum V Mac<e=I>donicae et VIII Augustae et II Augustae et XX Valeriae Victricis vixit annis LXI Aristio libertus heres faciendum curavit \n
EDCS-07800792.translation: Having achieved the equestrian rank, he served as centurion in the 5th Macedonian, 8th Augustan, 2nd Augustan, and 20th Valeria Victrix Legions. He lived 61 years. Aristio, his freedman and heir, saw to its construction.
EDCS-07800792.summary: A funerary inscription for a centurion who served in four legions, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-07800793.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus stus g- - -sig(nifer) legionis XX Valeriae victricis stipendiorum nnum heres curavit\n
EDCS-07800793.translation: To the Divine Shades. [Name lost]... standard-bearer of the 20th Valeria Victrix Legion, [number] years of service. His heir saw to its construction.
EDCS-07800793.summary: A fragmentary funerary inscription for a standard-bearer of the 20th Legion, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-07800794.text_edition: \n Celeia miles legionis XX Valeriae victricis nnrum XL stipendiorum XX heres faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-07800794.translation: Celeia, a soldier of the 20th Valeria Victrix Legion, served 20 years, lived 40 years. His heir saw to its construction.
EDCS-07800794.summary: A funerary inscription for Celeia, a soldier of the 20th Legion, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-07800795.text_edition: \n Palatina ens Oia legionis XX Valeriae victricis militavit\n
EDCS-07800795.translation: Palatina [name lost] Oia of the 20th Valeria Victrix Legion served.
EDCS-07800795.summary: A fragmentary inscription recording military service in the 20th Legion, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-07800800.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Anni Felicis veterani vixit annos LXXX \n
EDCS-07800800.translation: To the Divine Shades. Annius Felix, a veteran, lived 80 years.
EDCS-07800800.summary: A funerary inscription for Annius Felix, a veteran, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-07800801.text_edition: \n Lucius Antestius Luci Sergia Sabinus Corduba \n
EDCS-07800801.translation: Lucius Antestius Lucius, son of Sergia Sabina, from Corduba.
EDCS-07800801.summary: An inscription identifying a man named Lucius Antestius, likely a Roman citizen from Corduba, possibly funerary or honorific, from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-07800802.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marco Apronio Marci filio Fabia ecio Beryto S\n
EDCS-07800802.translation: To the Divine Shades. To Marcus Apronius, son of Marcus, of the Fabia tribe, from Berytus.
EDCS-07800802.summary: A funerary inscription for Marcus Apronius, son of Marcus, of the Fabia tribe, from Berytus, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-07800804.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelius Diogenes imaginifer M \n
EDCS-07800804.translation: To the Divine Shades. Aurelius Diogenes, standard-bearer.
EDCS-07800804.summary: A fragmentary funerary inscription for Aurelius Diogenes, an imaginifer (standard-bearer), likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-07800805.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aureli Luci equitis heres faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-07800805.translation: To the spirits of the departed. The heir of Aurelius Lucius, a knight, had this made.
EDCS-07800805.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aurelius Lucius, an eques, with his heir commissioning the monument, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-07800806.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caecilius Donatus Bessus natione militavit annos XXVI vixit annos XXXX\n
EDCS-07800806.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Caecilius Donatus Bessus, by nation, served as a soldier for 26 years and lived for 40 years.
EDCS-07800806.summary: This funerary inscription records the military service and lifespan of Caecilius Donatus Bessus, a soldier, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-07800807.text_edition: \n Lucius Camulius Luci filius Stellatina Albanus Taurinus \n
EDCS-07800807.translation: Lucius Camulius, son of Lucius, of the Stellatina tribe, Albanus Taurinus.
EDCS-07800807.summary: This inscription simply records the name and tribal affiliation of Lucius Camulius Albanus Taurinus, possibly marking a tomb or a dedication, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-07800808.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Decimi Capieni Urbici Voltinia nna signiferi stipendiorum XXIIII nnum XLIIII heres faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-07800808.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Decimus Capienus Urbicus, of the Voltinia tribe, a standard-bearer (signifer) with 24 years of service, lived for 44 years; his heir had this made.
EDCS-07800808.summary: This funerary epitaph records the military service and lifespan of Decimus Capienus Urbicus, a signifer, with his heir commissioning the monument, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-07800809.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Cassius Secundus missus honesta missione vixit annos LXXX us coniugi\n
EDCS-07800809.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Cassius Secundus, discharged with an honorable dismissal, lived for 80 years... to his spouse.
EDCS-07800809.summary: This funerary inscription records the honorable discharge and lifespan of Cassius Secundus, likely a soldier, and is dedicated to his spouse, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-07800810.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marcus Cluvius Marci (filius) Aniensi Valentius Foro Iuli \n
EDCS-07800810.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Marcus Cluvius Valentius, son of Marcus, of the Aniensis tribe, from Forum Iulii.
EDCS-07800810.summary: This funerary inscription records the name, tribal affiliation, and origin of Marcus Cluvius Valentius, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-07800811.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Quintus Cornelius.
EDCS-07800811.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph dedicating the monument to Quintus Cornelius, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-07800812.summary: This inscription records the name and filiation of Quintus Cornelius, likely marking a tomb or a property, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-07800813.translation: Quintus Cornelius, son of Quintus, of the Aniensis tribe.
EDCS-07800813.summary: This inscription records the name, filiation, and tribal affiliation of Quintus Cornelius, likely marking a tomb or a property, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-07800814.text_edition: \n Quintus Domitius Quinti filius Claudia Optatus Viruno \n
EDCS-07800814.translation: Quintus Domitius Optatus, son of Quintus, of the Claudia tribe, from Virunum.
EDCS-07800814.summary: This inscription records the name, filiation, tribal affiliation, and origin of Quintus Domitius Optatus, likely marking a tomb or a dedication, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-07800815.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Gai Iuli Marullini beneficiari tuni vixit annis XXXXV heres faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-07800815.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Gaius Julius Marullinus, a beneficiarius (aide) of Tuni, lived for 45 years. His heir took care of the erection of this monument.
EDCS-07800815.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Julius Marullinus, who died at 45, with his heir commissioning the monument.

EDCS-07800816.text_edition: \n Quintus Iunius November vixit annos LXX Iulius Liberalis\n
EDCS-07800816.translation: Quintus Junius November lived for 70 years. Julius Liberalis [erected this].
EDCS-07800816.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Junius November, who died at 70, with Julius Liberalis noted as the dedicator.

EDCS-07800817.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Gaius Licinius veteranus vixit annos LXXX curam agente Aelio Candido\n
EDCS-07800817.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Gaius Licinius, a veteran, lived for 80 years. Aelius Candidus oversaw the work.
EDCS-07800817.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Licinius, a veteran who died at 80, with Aelius Candidus overseeing its construction.

EDCS-07800818.text_edition: \n Quintus Longinius Pomentina Laetus Luco stipendiorum XV centuria Corneli Severi \n
EDCS-07800818.translation: Quintus Longinius of the Pomentina tribe, Laetus, with 15 years of service in the century of Cornelius Severus.
EDCS-07800818.summary: This inscription records Quintus Longinius Laetus, a soldier who served 15 years in the century of Cornelius Severus.

EDCS-07800819.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Gai Publili Sergia Mi\n
EDCS-07800819.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Gaius Publilius of the Sergia tribe, Mi[lvius?].
EDCS-07800819.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph for Gaius Publilius of the Sergia tribe.

EDCS-07800820.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Lucio Festinio Probo filio vixit annos II dies XXVIIII Lucius Sempronius Probianus pater faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-07800820.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Lucius Festinius Probus, son, who lived for 2 years and 29 days. Lucius Sempronius Probianus, his father, took care of the erection of this monument.
EDCS-07800820.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Festinius Probus, who died at the age of 2 years and 29 days, commissioned by his father.

EDCS-07800821.text_edition: \n Sextus Sexti filius Fabia Brixia Simil\n
EDCS-07800821.translation: Sextus, son of Sextus, of the Fabia tribe, from Brixia, Simil[is?].
EDCS-07800821.summary: This is a fragmentary epitaph for Sextus, son of Sextus, possibly named Similis, from Brixia.

EDCS-07800823.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Gai Valeri Gai Claudia militis \n
EDCS-07800823.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Gaius Valerius, son of Gaius, a soldier of the Claudia tribe.
EDCS-07800823.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Valerius, son of Gaius, a soldier belonging to the Claudia tribe.

EDCS-07800824.text_edition: \n Marcus Valerius Marci filius Claudia Martialis \n
EDCS-07800824.translation: Marcus Valerius Martialis, son of Marcus, of the Claudia tribe.
EDCS-07800824.summary: This inscription records Marcus Valerius Martialis, son of Marcus, belonging to the Claudia tribe.

EDCS-07800825.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Lucius Valerius Luci Salaria Pud III \n
EDCS-07800825.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Lucius Valerius, son of Lucius, of the Salaria tribe, Pudens III [decades?].
EDCS-07800825.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph for Lucius Valerius, son of Lucius, of the Salaria tribe.

EDCS-07800826.translation: To Voconia Nigrina, from Gaius Valerius Victor.
EDCS-07800826.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Gaius Valerius Victor to Voconia Nigrina, likely a tombstone.

EDCS-07800827.text_edition: \n optionis ad spem ordinis centuria Lucili Ingenui qui naufragio perit situs est\n
EDCS-07800827.translation: Of the optio, in the hope of promotion in the century of Lucilius Ingenuus, who perished in a shipwreck, he is buried here.
EDCS-07800827.summary: This inscription honors an optio who died in a shipwreck, possibly marking his burial place or commemorating his service.

EDCS-07800829.text_edition: \n Gai filius Claudia s Savaria XXX\n
EDCS-07800829.translation: Gaius, son of [---], Claudia Savaria, aged 30.
EDCS-07800829.summary: This is a personal inscription recording the name, parentage, tribal affiliation, and age of an individual named Gaius.

EDCS-07800832.translation: To the spirits of the departed, from Gaius.
EDCS-07800832.summary: This is a simple dedication to the Manes (departed spirits) by Gaius, likely a tombstone inscription.

EDCS-07800835.summary: This fragmentary inscription mentions the tribe Galeria and the status of being married, likely part of a funerary or commemorative text.

EDCS-07800837.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus beri vixit annos LXX ex testamento Tiberia heres faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-07800837.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Berus lived 70 years. From his will, Tiberia, his heir, saw to its construction.
EDCS-07800837.summary: This funerary inscription records the age of Berus, who lived to 70, and states that his heir Tiberia commissioned the monument according to his will.

EDCS-07800840.text_edition: \n annorum XXVI turma VILIX frater fecit\n
EDCS-07800840.translation: Aged 26. His brother, Vilix, made this.
EDCS-07800840.summary: This inscription records the age of an individual and states that his brother, Vilix, erected the monument.

EDCS-07800841.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Flavi Callimorphi vixit annis XXXXII et Serapioni vixit nnis III mensibus VI Thesaeus fratri et filio faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-07800841.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Flavi Callimorphi lived 42 years, and Serapio lived 3 years and 6 months. Thesaeus, his brother and son, saw to its construction.
EDCS-07800841.summary: This funerary inscription commemorates Flavi Callimorphi, who lived 42 years, and his son Serapio, who lived 3 years and 6 months, with the monument commissioned by Thesaeus.

EDCS-07800842.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Etacontio liberto bene merenti Gaius Asurius Fortis patronus eius posuit\n
EDCS-07800842.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for Etacontius, a well-deserving freedman. Gaius Asurius Fortis, his patron, erected this.
EDCS-07800842.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated by the patron Gaius Asurius Fortis to his freedman Etacontius.

EDCS-07800843.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Atilianus et Antiatilianus annorum X Protus annorum XII Pompeius Optatus dominus faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-07800843.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Atilianus and Antiatilianus, aged 10. Pompeius Optatus, their master, saw to its construction.
EDCS-07800843.summary: This funerary inscription honors Atilianus and Antiatilianus, both aged 10, with the monument commissioned by their master, Pompeius Optatus.

EDCS-07800845.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Curatia Dinysia vixit annos XXXX heres faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-07800845.translation: To the Divine Shades. Curatia Dinysia lived for 40 years; her heir took care to have this made.
EDCS-07800845.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Curatia Dinysia, who lived to be 40 years old, with the monument erected by her heir, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07800846.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Fesonie Severiane vixit nnos XXV SI OII NIOCAR \n
EDCS-07800846.translation: To the Divine Shades. Fesonia Severiana lived for 25 years. [---]
EDCS-07800846.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Fesonia Severiana, who lived to be 25 years old, with a partially preserved text, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07800847.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Domitia Saturnina, who lived to be 41 years old, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07800848.translation: To the Divine Shades. Flavia Saturnina.
EDCS-07800848.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Flavia Saturnina, likely from the Roman Imperial period, possibly with additional text now lost.

EDCS-07800849.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Restitae vixit annos VII et Martiae vixit annos III parentes \n
EDCS-07800849.translation: To the Divine Shades. Restitae lived for 7 years and Martiae lived for 3 years. [Her] parents [made this].
EDCS-07800849.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for two young girls, Restitae (7 years old) and Martiae (3 years old), with the monument erected by their parents, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07800852.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus PR IM AE NA cus curante Iulio Secundo herede\n
EDCS-07800852.translation: To the Divine Shades. Primaena. Her heir, Iulius Secundus, took care to have this made.
EDCS-07800852.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Primaena, with her heir, Iulius Secundus, responsible for its construction, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07800857.text_edition: \n Deabus Matribus Ollototis Iulius Secundus et Aelia Augustina\n
EDCS-07800857.translation: To the Mother Goddesses Ollototis, Iulius Secundus and Aelia Augustina [dedicated this].
EDCS-07800857.summary: This is a dedication to the deities known as the Mother Goddesses Ollototis by Iulius Secundus and Aelia Augustina, likely from the Roman Imperial period, possibly in the provinces.

EDCS-07800858.text_edition: \n Fortunae Conservatrici Lucius Senecianius Martius |(centurio) legionis VI victricis\n
EDCS-07800858.translation: To Fortuna the Preserver, Lucius Senecianius Martius, centurion of the VI Victrix legion, [dedicated this].
EDCS-07800858.summary: This is a dedication to the goddess Fortuna Conservatrix by Lucius Senecianius Martius, a centurion in the VI Victrix legion, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07800865.text_edition: \n Victoriae legionis VI victricis Valerius Rufus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-07800865.translation: To Victoria, Lucius Valerius Rufus, [of the] VI Victrix legion, fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-07800865.summary: This is a votive inscription to the goddess Victoria by Valerius Rufus of the VI Victrix legion, indicating the fulfillment of a vow, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07800866.text_edition: \n Deo sancto Apollini Mapono pro salute Domini Nostri et numeri equitum Sarmatarum nnacensium Gordiani Aelius Antoninus |(centurio) legionis VI victricis domo Melitenis praepositus et praefectus votum solvit libens merito dedicatum pridie Kalendas Septembres imperatore domino nostro Gordiano Augusto II et Ponpeiano consulibus\n
EDCS-07800866.translation: To the holy god Apollo Maponus, Aelius Antoninus, centurion of the VI Victrix legion, from Melitene, prefect and commander, fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly for the safety of Our Lord and the troop of Sarmatian equites of Gordian origin, dedicated on the day before the Kalends of September, when our lord Gordian Augustus II and Pompeianus were consuls.
EDCS-07800866.summary: This is a complex votive inscription to Apollo Maponus by Aelius Antoninus, a centurion and prefect of the VI Victrix legion, for the safety of the emperor Gordian III and his troops, precisely dated to 31 August AD 241.

EDCS-07800867.text_edition: \n Pacifero Marti ELEGA VRBA posuit ex voto\n
EDCS-07800867.translation: Elega Urba set up this monument for Mars the Bringer of Peace, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-07800867.summary: This is a votive dedication to Mars made by Elega Urba.

EDCS-07800869.text_edition: \n Deabus Matribus Marcus Ingenuius Asiaticus decurio alae II Asturum votum solvit libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-07800869.translation: Marcus Ingenuius Asiaticus, a decurion of the Second Wing of Asturians, willingly, joyfully, and deservedly fulfilled his vow to the Mother Goddesses.
EDCS-07800869.summary: This records the fulfillment of a vow by Marcus Ingenuius Asiaticus, a decurion, to the Deae Matres.

EDCS-07800871.text_edition: \n centurio legionis XX Valeriae victricis cum suis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-07800871.translation: A centurion of the Twentieth Legion Valeria Victrix, with his dependants, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07800871.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by a centurion of the Twentieth Legion, along with his family.

EDCS-07800873.text_edition: \n Pro Salute et Victoria invicti imperatoris Marci Aureli Severi Antonini Pii Felicis Augusti et Iuliae Augustae matris Domini Nostri et castrorum Semproni\n
EDCS-07800873.translation: For the health and victory of the unconquered Emperor Marcus Aurelius Severus Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, and of Julia Augusta, mother of Our Lord, and of the camp; Sempronius [---]
EDCS-07800873.summary: This incomplete inscription is a dedication for the health and victory of Emperor Caracalla, Julia Domna, and possibly the camp, with Sempronius as the dedicator.

EDCS-07800877.text_edition: \n His terris tegitur Aelia Matrona quondam vixit annos XXVIII menses II dies VIII et Marcus Iulius Maximus filius vixit annos VI menses III dies XX et Campania Dubitatae mater vixit annos L Iulius Maximus singularis consularis alae Sarmatarum coniux coniugi incomparabili et filio patri pientissimo et socaere tenacissime memoriam posuit\n
EDCS-07800877.translation: Aelia Matrona, who lived 28 years, 2 months, and 8 days, and Marcus Iulius Maximus, her son, who lived 6 years, 3 months, and 20 days, and Campania Dubitatae, the mother, who lived 50 years, lie buried in this land; Iulius Maximus, a singularis of the Wing of Sarmatians, erected this memorial to his incomparably beloved wife, his most devoted father, and his tenacious mother-in-law.
EDCS-07800877.summary: This is a funerary monument commemorating Aelia Matrona, her son Marcus Iulius Maximus, her mother Campania Dubitatae, and erected by her husband Iulius Maximus.

EDCS-07800878.translation: To the spirits of the departed: a decurion of the Wing of Sarmatians.
EDCS-07800878.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the Dis Manes by a decurion of the Wing of Sarmatians.

EDCS-07800879.translation: [---]aiia took care; [---]ius, son of a decurion.
EDCS-07800879.summary: This incomplete inscription likely records a person named Naiia and mentions the son of a decurion, possibly related to a funerary or commemorative context.

EDCS-07800880.text_edition: \n pater Saturninus I O V L\n
EDCS-07800880.summary: This inscription, containing the abbreviation IOVL (Iuppiter Optimus Maximus Votis Libens Solvit), likely records a votive fulfillment by Saturninus.

EDCS-07800882.text_edition: \n Flavius us voto suscepto merito posuit\n
EDCS-07800882.translation: Flavius, from a vow undertaken, deservedly, set this up.
EDCS-07800882.summary: This is a votive inscription by Flavius.

EDCS-07800883.text_edition: \n Deo Ialono Contrebi sanctissimo Iulius Ianuarius emeritus ex decurione votum solvit\n
EDCS-07800883.translation: To the most holy god Ialonus Contrebius, Iulius Ianuarius, a retired decurion, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07800883.summary: This is a votive dedication to the god Ialonus Contrebius by Iulius Ianuarius, a retired decurion.

EDCS-07800884.text_edition: \n Deo Marti Sabinus praepositus et milites numeri Barcariorum sub cura eiius posverunt\n
EDCS-07800884.translation: To the god Mars, Sabinus the prefect and the soldiers of the numerical unit of the Barcaarii under his command erected this.
EDCS-07800884.summary: This is a military dedication to the god Mars by a prefect and soldiers, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-07800885.text_edition: \n Deo sancto Marti Cocidio Vibenius Lucius beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-07800885.translation: Vibenius Lucius, a consular officer's assistant, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to the holy god Marti Cocidio.
EDCS-07800885.summary: This is a private votive inscription to the god Marti Cocidio by a soldier, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-07800888.text_edition: \n ob balineum refectum et basilicam vetustate conlabsum sic a solo restitutam equitibus alae Sebussianae Postumianae sub Octavio Sabino viro clarissimo praeside nostro curante Flavio Ammausio praefecto equitum dedicata XI Kalendas Septembres Censore II et Lepido II consulibus\n
EDCS-07800888.translation: For the bathhouse having been repaired and the basilica, which had collapsed from age, having been restored from the ground up, the knights of the Sebussian Pustumian cavalry, under the most distinguished Octavius Sabinus, our governor, with Flavius Ammausius, the prefect of the cavalry, overseeing, dedicated this on the eleventh day before the Kalends of September, when Censor and Lepidus were consuls for the second time.
EDCS-07800888.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a repaired bathhouse and basilica by the knights of a cavalry unit, under the supervision of a governor and prefect, likely dating to the late Roman period.

EDCS-07800889.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Lucius Iulius Apollinaris Trever annorum XXX eques alae Augustae hic situs est\n
EDCS-07800889.translation: To the spirits of the departed, Lucius Iulius Apollinaris of Trier, aged thirty, a cavalryman of the Augusta cavalry unit, lies here.
EDCS-07800889.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for a cavalryman named Lucius Iulius Apollinaris, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-07800891.text_edition: \n Servandus coloniae Claudiae Augustae Agrippinensium ad forum \n
EDCS-07800891.translation: Servandus, of the colony of Claudia Augusta Agrippinensium, near the forum.
EDCS-07800891.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that likely refers to an individual named Servandus in the colony of Cologne (Colonia Claudia Ara Agrippinensium).

EDCS-07800894.text_edition: \n Numinibus Augusti nostri et Genio COLGF Apollinis Bellinus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-07800894.translation: To the divine spirits of our Emperor and the Genius of the colony of Apollo, Bellinus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07800894.summary: This is a votive inscription to the imperial cult and the Genius of a colony by an individual named Bellinus, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-07800895.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum et perpetuae securitati Aureli Pusinni civis vixit annos LIIII militavit annos XXXVI Aureliae Eubiae coniugis eiius vixit annos XXXVII Aurelius Propinquus filio karissimo\n
EDCS-07800895.translation: To the spirits of the departed and for eternal security: Aurelia Pusinna, a citizen, lived 54 years, served 36 years. To Aurelia Eubia, his wife, who lived 37 years. To Aurelius Propinquus, his dearest son.
EDCS-07800895.summary: This is a multi-generational tombstone inscription commemorating Aurelius Propinquus, his wife Aurelia Eubia, and likely his son, detailing their ages and military service, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-07800896.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription that appears to be part of a dedication or funerary text.

EDCS-07800899.text_edition: \n Deo Marti Nodonti Aurelius cinus sigillum\n
EDCS-07800899.translation: To the god Marti Nodonti, Aurelius Cinus [dedicated this] sigillum.
EDCS-07800899.summary: This is a votive dedication of a small statue (sigillum) to the god Marti Nodonti by Aurelius Cinus, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-07800900.text_edition: \n Lucianus Deo Marti Nodonti coll<eg=IC>(ae) Aprili Viatoris votum solvit \n
EDCS-07800900.translation: Lucianus fulfilled his vow to the god Marti Nodonti, [in the century] of Aprilius Viator.
EDCS-07800900.summary: This is a votive inscription by Lucianus to the god Marti Nodonti, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-07800901.text_edition: \n Matribus Marcus Nantonius Orbiotalus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-07800901.translation: Marcus Nantonius Orbiotalus gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to the Mothers.
EDCS-07800901.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicating a vow to the Matres by Marcus Nantonius Orbiotalus.

EDCS-07800902.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Cintusmus miles cohortis IIII Gallorum posuit Melisus\n
EDCS-07800902.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Melisus placed this for Cintusmus, a soldier of the 4th Cohort of Gauls.
EDCS-07800902.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Cintusmus, a soldier of the 4th Cohort of Gauls, erected by Melisus.

EDCS-07800903.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Croto Vindicis emerito cohortis IIII Gallorum annorum XXXX monimentum fecit Flavia Peregrina coniunx pientissima marito pientissimo titulum posuit\n
EDCS-07800903.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Flavia Peregrina, a most devoted wife, erected this monument for her most devoted husband, Croto, a veteran of the 4th Cohort of Gauls, aged 40.
EDCS-07800903.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Croto, a veteran of the 4th Cohort of Gauls, erected by his wife Flavia Peregrina.

EDCS-07800904.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Verecunda Rufi filia cives Dobunna nnum XXXV Excingus coniux coniugi karissimae posuit de suo\n
EDCS-07800904.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Excingus placed this for his dearest wife, Verecunda, daughter of Rufus, a citizen of Dobunna, aged 35, at his own expense.
EDCS-07800904.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Verecunda, daughter of Rufus, erected by her husband Excingus.

EDCS-07800906.text_edition: \n Deo Breganti et Numini Augusti Titus Aurelius Quintus dono dedit pecunia et sumptu suo\n
EDCS-07800906.translation: Titus Aurelius Quintus gave this gift to the god Bregantos and the divine will of Augustus, with his own money and at his own expense.
EDCS-07800906.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription by Titus Aurelius Quintus to the god Bregantos and the imperial numen.

EDCS-07800907.text_edition: \n Fortunae sacrum Gaius Antonius Modestus legionis VI victricis piae fidelis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-07800907.translation: To Fortuna. Gaius Antonius Modestus of the 6th Legion Victrix, pious and faithful, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07800907.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicating a vow to Fortuna by Gaius Antonius Modestus of the 6th Legion Victrix.

EDCS-07800910.text_edition: \n Deae Victoriae Brigantiae et Numinibus Augustorum Titus Aurelius Aurelianus dedit dedicavit pro se et suis se magistro sacrorum Antonino III et Geta II consulibus\n
EDCS-07800910.translation: Titus Aurelius Aurelianus gave and dedicated this to the goddess Victory Brigantia and the divine spirits of the Augusti, for himself and his family, while he was master of the sacred rites under the consulship of Antoninus and Geta.
EDCS-07800910.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to the goddess Victory Brigantia and the imperial numen by Titus Aurelius Aurelianus, dating to the consulship of Antoninus and Geta.

EDCS-07800911.text_edition: \n Deae Victoriae Brigantiae aram dedicavit Aurelius Senopianus\n
EDCS-07800911.translation: Aurelius Senopianus dedicated an altar to the goddess Victory Brigantia.
EDCS-07800911.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription by Aurelius Senopianus for an altar to the goddess Victory Brigantia.
