EDCS-07801317.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, Gracilus, having been ordained in Upper Germany in the 22nd Aurelian Legion, arranged for this to be made for his wife.
EDCS-07801317.summary: Gracilus dedicates a memorial to his deceased wife.

EDCS-07801320.text_edition: \n Fortunae sanctae Marcus Valerius Fulvianus praefectus equitum votum solvit libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-07801320.translation: To holy Fortune, Marcus Valerius Fulvianus, prefect of the cavalry, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07801320.summary: Marcus Valerius Fulvianus, a cavalry prefect, dedicates a vow to the goddess Fortune.

EDCS-07801321.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo et Matribus Ollototis sive Transmarinis Pomponius Donatus beneficiarius consularis pro salute sua et suorum votum solvit libens animo\n
EDCS-07801321.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the overseas Mother Goddesses Ollototae, Pomponius Donatus, a staff officer of the consul, fulfilled his vow for his own health and that of his family, gladly and willingly.
EDCS-07801321.summary: Pomponius Donatus dedicates a vow to Jupiter and the Mother Goddesses Ollototae for his health and his family's.

EDCS-07801322.text_edition: \n Deabus Matribus Ollototis Tiberius Claudius Quintianus beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-07801322.translation: To the Mother Goddesses Ollototae, Tiberius Claudius Quintianus, a staff officer of the consul, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07801322.summary: Tiberius Claudius Quintianus dedicates a vow to the Mother Goddesses Ollototae.

EDCS-07801324.text_edition: \n Matribus sacrum Gemellus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-07801324.translation: Gemellus gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to the Mother Goddesses.
EDCS-07801324.summary: Gemellus dedicates a vow to the Mother Goddesses.

EDCS-07801326.text_edition: \n Sulevis ala Vettonum CANN votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-07801326.translation: To Sulevia of the ala of the Vettones, CANN gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07801326.summary: CANN dedicates a vow to the goddess Sulevia.

EDCS-07801327.text_edition: \n mandus ex cuneo Frisiorum Vinoviensium votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-07801327.translation: Mandus of the Frisians from Vinovium willingly and deservedly paid his vow.
EDCS-07801327.summary: This is a votive inscription by Mandus, identified as belonging to the Frisians of Vinovium, likely from the Roman military.

EDCS-07801330.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Nemonius Montanus decurio vixit nnos XL Nemonius Sanctus frater et coheredes ex testamento fecerunt\n
EDCS-07801330.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Nemonius Montanus, a decurion, lived for 40 years. Nemonius Sanctus, his brother, and co-heirs erected this from the will.
EDCS-07801330.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Nemonius Montanus, a decurion, erected by his brother and co-heirs.

EDCS-07801331.translation: Julius Victorinus of the century of Pu...
EDCS-07801331.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely commemorating Julius Victorinus, a soldier.

EDCS-07801332.text_edition: \n Numinibus Augustorum et Silvano Invicto sacrum Gaius Tetius Veturius Micianus pr(a)efectus alae Sebosiannae ob aprum eximiae formae captum quem multi antecessores eius praedari non potuerunt voto suscepto libens posuit\n
EDCS-07801332.translation: Sacred to the Divine Powers of the Emperors and to Silvanus the Invincible. Gaius Tetius Veturius Micianus, prefect of the Sebosian ala, willingly set this up with a vow, for capturing a boar of exceptional form, which many of his predecessors were unable to hunt.
EDCS-07801332.summary: This is a dedication to the Emperors' divine powers and Silvanus by Gaius Tetius Veturius Micianus, a cavalry prefect, to commemorate a successful boar hunt.

EDCS-07801333.text_edition: \n Deo Silvano Aurelius Quirinus praefectus fecit\n
EDCS-07801333.translation: Aurelius Quirinus, prefect, made this for the god Silvanus.
EDCS-07801333.summary: This is a dedication to the god Silvanus by Aurelius Quirinus, a prefect.

EDCS-07801334.text_edition: \n Deo Apollini Tertius votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-07801334.translation: Tertius willingly and deservedly paid his vow to the god Apollo.
EDCS-07801334.summary: This is a votive inscription by Tertius to the god Apollo.

EDCS-07801336.text_edition: \n Deo Marti Condati Valerius Probianus pro se et suis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-07801336.translation: Valerius Probianus willingly and deservedly paid his vow to the god Mars Condates for himself and his family.
EDCS-07801336.summary: This is a votive inscription by Valerius Probianus to the god Mars Condates, for himself and his family.

EDCS-07801337.translation: Votum solvit [for the god] Vitiris Duihno.
EDCS-07801337.summary: This is a votive inscription to the god Vitiris Duihno.

EDCS-07801338.text_edition: \n Daeabus Vitiribus Vitalis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-07801338.translation: Vitalis willingly and deservedly paid his vow to the goddesses Vitiris.
EDCS-07801338.summary: This is a votive inscription by Vitalis to the goddesses Vitiris.

EDCS-07801343.text_edition: \n Deo Esculapio Publius Viboleius Secundus aram dono dedit\n
EDCS-07801343.translation: Publius Viboleius Secundus gave this altar as a gift to the god Aesculapius.
EDCS-07801343.summary: This is a dedication of an altar to the god Aesculapius by Publius Viboleius Secundus.

EDCS-07801344.text_edition: \n Deae Brigantiae sacrum Congenniccus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-07801344.translation: Sacred to the goddess Brigantia. Congenniccus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07801344.summary: This is a dedication to the goddess Brigantia by Congenniccus, who willingly fulfilled his vow, likely dating to the Roman period in Britain.

EDCS-07801346.text_edition: \n Marti Alatori Gaius Vinicius Celsus pro se et votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-07801346.translation: To Mars the Winged. Gaius Vinicius Celsus fulfilled his vow for himself and (others) willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-07801346.summary: This is a dedication to Mars the Winged by Gaius Vinicius Celsus, who fulfilled his vow, likely a military dedication from the Roman period.

EDCS-07801348.translation: Julius Verax, centurion of the VI Legion.
EDCS-07801348.summary: This is a military inscription identifying Julius Verax as a centurion of the VI Legion, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07801350.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar divi Severi nepos divi Magni Antonini filius Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate pater patriae consul aquam usibus militum cohortis V Gallorum induxit curante Mario Valeriano legato eius pro praetore\n
EDCS-07801350.translation: Emperor Caesar, grandson of the deified Severus, son of the deified Antoninus, Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus, pontifex maximus, holder of tribunician power, father of the fatherland, consul, brought water for the use of the soldiers of the 5th Cohort of Gauls, under the supervision of his legate, proconsul, Marius Valerianus.
EDCS-07801350.summary: This imperial rescript records Emperor Alexander Severus inaugurating a water supply for the 5th Cohort of Gauls, supervised by his legate Marius Valerianus, dating to his reign (222-235 AD).

EDCS-07801352.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Audus vixit nns VIIII menses VIIII Lucius Arruntius Salvianus filio bene (merenti) piissimo\n
EDCS-07801352.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Audus lived 9 years, 9 months. Lucius Arruntius Salvianus (dedicated this) to his most dutiful son, well-deserving.
EDCS-07801352.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Audus, who died at the age of 9 years and 9 months, dedicated by his father Lucius Arruntius Salvianus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07801353.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld, from Iul[ius...]
EDCS-07801353.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription beginning a dedication to the Spirits of the Underworld, likely part of a tombstone, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07801354.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Victoris natione Maurum annorum XX libertus Numeriani equitis alae I Asturum qui piantissime prosequutus est\n
EDCS-07801354.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Victoris, a Moor by nationality, aged 20, freedman of Numerianus, a cavalryman of the I Wing of Asturians, who very dutifully accompanied (him).
EDCS-07801354.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Victoris, a Moor and freedman of Numerianus, a cavalryman of the I Wing of Asturians, who died at age 20, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07801355.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Regina liberta et coniuge Barates Palmyrenus natione Catvallauna annorum XXX RGYN\'\' BT HRY BR T\'\' HBL\n
EDCS-07801355.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Regina, freedwoman and wife of Barates of Palmyra, of the Catuvellaunian people, aged 30. (Greek text follows).
EDCS-07801355.summary: This is a bilingual tombstone inscription for Regina, freedwoman and wife of Barates of Palmyra, who was aged 30, likely from Roman Britain and dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07801357.summary: This is a very brief inscription identifying Proculus as a prefect, likely military or administrative, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07801363.text_edition: \n Fortunae Augustae sacrum Publius Aelius Atticus praefectus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-07801363.translation: Sacred to Fortuna Augusta. Publius Aelius Atticus, prefect, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07801363.summary: This is a dedication to Fortuna Augusta by Publius Aelius Atticus, a prefect, who willingly fulfilled his vow, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07801365.text_edition: \n Genio praetori Claudius Epaphroditus Claudianus tribunus cohortis I Lingonum votum libens posuit merito\n
EDCS-07801365.translation: To the Genius of the praetor, Claudius Epaphroditus Claudianus, tribune of the First Cohort of Lingones, gladly dedicated this offering in recognition of merit.
EDCS-07801365.summary: A military dedication to the Genius of a praetor, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-07801367.text_edition: \n Marti Augusto Auffidius Aufidianus dono dedit\n
EDCS-07801367.translation: Auffidius Aufidianus gave this as a gift to Mars Augustus.
EDCS-07801367.summary: A private dedication to the god Mars Augustus, probably dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-07801368.text_edition: \n Deo Marti Ascernus posuit aram votum solvit\n
EDCS-07801368.translation: Ascernus dedicated this altar to the god Mars and paid his vow.
EDCS-07801368.summary: A dedication to the god Mars by Ascernus, who also fulfilled a vow, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-07801369.translation: To the god Mars Caurus, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-07801369.summary: A dedication to the god Mars Caurus, recording the fulfillment of a vow, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-07801373.text_edition: \n Numini Augusti et Genio cohortis I Fidae Vardullorum civium Romanorum equitatae sub Antistio Advento legato Augusti pro praetore Flavius Titianus tribunus de suo dedit\n
EDCS-07801373.translation: To the divine power of Augustus and the Genius of the First Cohort of Fidae Vardullorum, Roman citizens, equestrians, under Antistius Adventus, Legate of the Emperor Pro Praetore, Flavius Titianus, tribune, dedicated from his own funds.
EDCS-07801373.summary: A military dedication by a tribune of the First Cohort of Fidae Vardullorum to the divine power of Augustus and the cohort's Genius, during the governorship of Antistius Adventus, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-07801374.text_edition: \n Reginae votum Misio votum libens solvit\n
EDCS-07801374.translation: Misio gladly paid her vow to the Queen.
EDCS-07801374.summary: A private dedication by Misio to a female deity referred to as 'Regina', recording the payment of a vow, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-07801375.text_edition: \n Deo Silvano Marcus Didius Provincialis beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-07801375.translation: Marcus Didius Provincialis, a beneficiarius consularis, gladly and happily paid his vow to the god Silvanus, in recognition of merit.
EDCS-07801375.summary: A dedication to the god Silvanus by Marcus Didius Provincialis, a special soldier (beneficiarius consularis), likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-07801376.text_edition: \n Deae Victorie voto suscepto Ulpius posuit\n
EDCS-07801376.translation: Ulpius set this up, having undertaken a vow to the goddess Victory.
EDCS-07801376.summary: A dedication to the goddess Victory by Ulpius, who fulfilled a vow, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-07801378.text_edition: \n Deo Vitiri VNTHAV princeps posuit pro se et suis\n
EDCS-07801378.translation: VNTHAV, a princeps, set this up for himself and his family to the god Vitiris.
EDCS-07801378.summary: A dedication to the god Vitiris by a man named VNTHAV, who held the rank of princeps, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-07801381.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar Marcus Antonius Gordianus Pius Felix Augustus balneum cum basilica a solo instruxit per Egnatium Lucilianum legatum Augusti pro praetore curante Marco Aurelio Quirino praefecto cohortis I Lingonum Gordianae\n
EDCS-07801381.translation: Emperor Caesar Marcus Antonius Gordianus Pius Felix Augustus constructed the bathhouse with its basilica from the ground up, through Egnatius Lucilianus, Legate of the Emperor Pro Praetore, with Marcus Aurelius Quirinus, Prefect of the First Cohort of Gordian Lingones, supervising.
EDCS-07801381.summary: An imperial building inscription recording Emperor Gordian III's construction of a bathhouse and basilica, supervised by a legatus Augusti pro praetore and a cohort prefect, likely dating to the 240s CE.

EDCS-07801382.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar Marcus Antonius Gordianus Pius Felix Augustus principia et armamentaria conlapsa restituit per Maecilium Fuscum legatum Augusti pro praetore curante Marco Aurelio Quirino praefecto cohortis I Lingonum Gordianae\n
EDCS-07801382.translation: Emperor Caesar Marcus Antonius Gordianus Pius Felix Augustus restored the collapsed headquarters building and armories, through Maecilius Fuscus, imperial legate with praetorian authority, with Marcus Aurelius Quirinus, prefect of the I Gordian Cohort of Lingones, supervising.
EDCS-07801382.summary: This inscription records the restoration of military buildings by Emperor Gordian III, carried out by his legate and a cohort prefect, likely during the mid-3rd century CE.

EDCS-07801385.translation: Of the Eighth Cohort, under the centuria of Oppius Proculus.
EDCS-07801385.summary: This is a military marker indicating unit and subordinate command within a cohort, likely Roman.

EDCS-07801389.text_edition: \n Genio IV Aurelius praefectus votum libens solvit\n
EDCS-07801389.translation: To the Genius, Aurelius, the prefect, willingly paid his vow.
EDCS-07801389.summary: This dedication records a vow paid by Aurelius, a prefect, to a local Genius, possibly in the 3rd century CE.

EDCS-07801392.text_edition: \n Deo Vernostono Cocidio Viri<l=I>is Germanus votum solvit libens \n
EDCS-07801392.translation: To the god Vernostonus Cocidius, Virilis Germanus willingly paid his vow.
EDCS-07801392.summary: This dedication records a vow paid by Virilis Germanus to the Romano-British deity Vernostonus Cocidius.

EDCS-07801393.translation: To the god Vitirus, Maximus paid his vow.
EDCS-07801393.summary: This inscription records a vow paid to the god Vitirus by Maximus.

EDCS-07801401.summary: This is a military marker indicating command within a Roman cohort.

EDCS-07801402.translation: Under the centuria of Varsidius Justus.
EDCS-07801402.summary: This is a military marker indicating command within a Roman cohort.

EDCS-07801404.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Dubitati militis X P ROB\n
EDCS-07801404.translation: To the spirits of the departed, Dubitatus, a soldier of the Tenth Legion, P [---] R O B [---]
EDCS-07801404.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a soldier named Dubitatus of the Tenth Legion, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-07801410.text_edition: \n Apollini Mapono Quintus Terentius Quinti filius Oufentina Firmus Saena praefectus castrorum legionis VI victricis piae fidelis dedit dedicavit\n
EDCS-07801410.translation: To Apollo Maponus, Quintus Terentius Firmus, son of Quintus, of the Oufentina tribe, camp prefect of the Sixth Legion Victrix Pia Fidelis, gave and dedicated.
EDCS-07801410.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a gift to Apollo Maponus by Quintus Terentius Firmus, a camp prefect of the Sixth Legion, likely in the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-07801411.translation: To Apollo Maponus, Calpurnius, the tribune, dedicated.
EDCS-07801411.summary: This dedication records a gift to Apollo Maponus made by a tribune named Calpurnius.

EDCS-07801413.text_edition: \n Deo Arecurio Apollinaris Cassi votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-07801413.translation: Apollinaris Cassi willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to the god Arecurius.
EDCS-07801413.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Apollinaris Cassi to the god Arecurius.

EDCS-07801416.translation: NIII erected an altar to Diana.
EDCS-07801416.summary: This inscription records the erection of an altar to Diana by NIII.

EDCS-07801420.text_edition: \n Iovi aeterno Dolicheno et Caelesti Brigantiae et Saluti Caius Iulius Apolinaris |(centurio) legionis VI iussu dei\n
EDCS-07801420.translation: Gaius Julius Apolinaris, a centurion of the VI Legion, [erected this] by order of the god to the eternal Jupiter Dolichenus, and to Caelestis Brigantia, and to Salus.
EDCS-07801420.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Gaius Julius Apolinaris, a centurion of the VI Legion, to Jupiter Dolichenus, Caelestis Brigantia, and Salus, by divine command.

EDCS-07801426.text_edition: \n Soli Invicto vexillatio legionis VI victricis piae fidelis fecit sub cura Sexti Calpurni Agricolae legati Augusti pro praetore\n
EDCS-07801426.translation: The vexillation of the VI Victrix Legion, pious and faithful, erected this to Sol Invictus under the supervision of Sextus Calpurnius Agricola, imperial legate with praetorian authority.
EDCS-07801426.summary: This inscription records the erection of a monument to Sol Invictus by a detachment of the VI Victrix Legion, supervised by the legate Sextus Calpurnius Agricola.

EDCS-07801427.text_edition: \n Victoriae Augustae Lucius Iulius Iulianus legatus \n
EDCS-07801427.translation: Lucius Julius Julianus, legate, [dedicated this] to Victory Augusta.
EDCS-07801427.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Victory Augusta by Lucius Julius Julianus, a legate.

EDCS-07801430.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, likely recording a dedication or transfer of property related to Viterius.

EDCS-07801431.text_edition: \n LEG A Quintus Calpurnius Concessinius praefectus equitum caesa Corionototarum manu praesentissimi numinis dei votum solvit\n
EDCS-07801431.translation: Quintus Calpurnius Concessinius, prefect of cavalry, willingly fulfilled his vow to the god, whose most present divine power protected [him] when the Corionototae were killed.
EDCS-07801431.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Quintus Calpurnius Concessinius, a cavalry prefect, after a victory over the Corionototae.

EDCS-07801432.text_edition: \n sit norus praepositus curam agens horreorum tempore expeditionis felicissimae nnae votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-07801432.translation: [...]us, praepositus, while in charge of the granaries during a most fortunate expedition, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to [the goddess] Nnae.
EDCS-07801432.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by a praepositus in charge of granaries during a military expedition.

EDCS-07801436.text_edition: \n Imperatori Tito Aelio An<t=I>onino Augusto Pio II consuli sub cura Quinti Lollii Urbici legati Augusti pro praetore legio II Augusta fecit \n
EDCS-07801436.translation: The II Augusta Legion erected this to Emperor Titus Aelius Antoninus Augustus Pius, in his second consulship, under the supervision of Quintus Lollius Urbicus, imperial legate with praetorian authority.
EDCS-07801436.summary: This inscription records the erection of a monument by the II Augusta Legion to Emperor Antoninus Pius during his second consulship, under the supervision of legate Quintus Lollius Urbicus.

EDCS-07801442.text_edition: \n Imperatoribus Caesaribus Marco Aurelio Antonino et \n
EDCS-07801442.translation: To the Emperors Caesars Marcus Aurelius Antoninus and [...]
EDCS-07801442.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription beginning a dedication to the Emperors Marcus Aurelius Antoninus and another Caesar.

EDCS-07801450.text_edition: \n Instante Flavio Hygino legionis VI victricis\n
EDCS-07801450.translation: During the tenure of Flavius Hyginus of the Sixth Legion Victrix.
EDCS-07801450.summary: This inscription records the period of service of Flavius Hyginus, a member of the Sixth Legion Victrix, likely from the 2nd century AD.

EDCS-07801460.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Barathes Palmorenus sic vexillarius vixit annos LXVIII\n
EDCS-07801460.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Barathes Palmorenus, a standard-bearer, lived for sixty-eight years.
EDCS-07801460.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Barathes Palmorenus, a vexillarius (standard-bearer), who died at the age of 68.

EDCS-07801461.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Flavinus eques alae Petrianae signifer turma Candidi annorum XXV stipendiorum VII hic situs\n
EDCS-07801461.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Flavinus, a cavalryman of the ala Petriana, standard-bearer of the troop of Candidus, lived for twenty-five years and served for seven years, is buried here.
EDCS-07801461.summary: This funerary inscription records the life and service of Flavinus, a cavalryman and signifer (standard-bearer) in the ala Petriana, who died at 25 after 7 years of service.

EDCS-07801463.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Iulius Primus coniugi carissimae ponendum curavit\n
EDCS-07801463.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Julius Primus erected this for his dearest wife.
EDCS-07801463.summary: This funerary inscription records Julius Primus dedicating a monument to his beloved wife.

EDCS-07801464.text_edition: \n Lucio Valerio Iusto militi legionis VI Egnatius Dyonisius et Surius Iustus heredes faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-07801464.translation: Egnatius Dyonisius and Surius Iustus, the heirs, had this made for Lucius Valerius Justus, a soldier of the Sixth Legion.
EDCS-07801464.summary: This monument was erected by the heirs of Lucius Valerius Justus, a soldier in the Sixth Legion.

EDCS-07801469.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Ahtehe filiae Nobilis vixit annis V\n
EDCS-07801469.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Ahtehe, daughter of Nobilis, lived for five years.
EDCS-07801469.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Ahtehe, daughter of Nobilis, who died at the age of five.

EDCS-07801470.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Sudrenus Ertole nomine Vellibia felicissime vixit annis IIII diebus LX\n
EDCS-07801470.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Sudrenus, son of Ertole, named Vellibia, lived most happily for four years and sixty days.
EDCS-07801470.summary: This funerary inscription records the life of Sudrenus, identified by the name Vellibia, who lived for four years and sixty days.

EDCS-07801471.text_edition: \n Iulia Materna annorum VI Iulius Marcellinus filiae carissimae\n
EDCS-07801471.translation: Iulia Materna, aged six. Julius Marcellinus for his dearest daughter.
EDCS-07801471.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Julius Marcellinus to his daughter Iulia Materna, who died at the age of six.

EDCS-07801473.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Mo[---]
EDCS-07801473.summary: This is a fragment of a funerary inscription, starting with the dedication 'Dis Manibus' and a partial name, 'Mo[---]'.

EDCS-07801474.summary: This is a very fragmented inscription, likely containing names such as '[---]llonius' and 'Lu[---]cius'.

EDCS-07900002.text_edition: \n Deo Herculi Gaius Vitellius Atticianus |(centurio) legionis VI victricis piae fidelis\n
EDCS-07900002.translation: To the god Hercules, Gaius Vitellius Atticianus, centurion of the VI Victrix Pia Fidelis legion.
EDCS-07900002.summary: A dedication to the god Hercules by a centurion of the VI Victrix legion, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-07900003.translation: To the goddesses Minerva and Victory-Hercules.
EDCS-07900003.summary: A dedication to the goddesses Minerva and Victory-Hercules, possibly on a shared altar or shrine, date unknown.

EDCS-07900005.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari divi Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti Arabici Adiabenici Parthici Maximi filio divi Antonini Pii Germanici Sarmatici nepoti divi Antonini Pii pronepoti divi Hadriani abnepoti divi Traiani Parthici et divi Nervae adnepoti Marco Aurelio Antonino Pio Felici Augusto Parthico Maximo Britannico Maximo pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate XVI Imperatori II consuli IIII patri patriae pro pietate ac devotione communi curante Gaio Iulio Marco legato Augusti pro praetore cohors II Nerviorum civium Romanorum posuit devota numini maiestatique eius\n
EDCS-07900005.translation: To the Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus, grandson of the deified Antoninus Pius Germanicus Sarmaticus, great-grandson of the deified Antoninus Pius, descendant of the deified Hadrian, great-great-grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus and the deified Nerva, Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus Parthicus Maximus Britannicus Maximus, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 16th time, Imperator for the second time, Consul for the fourth time, father of the country, for the common piety and devotion, administered by Gaius Julius Marcus, imperial legate with praetorian authority, the II Nerviorum cohors of Roman citizens dedicated this, devoted to his divinity and majesty.
EDCS-07900005.summary: A dedication by the II Nerviorum cohort to Emperor Caracalla, recorded during his 16th tribunician power and 4th consulship, approximately 213 CE.

EDCS-07900011.text_edition: \n Dis cultoribus huius loci Iulius Victor tribunus\n
EDCS-07900011.translation: To the gods, worshipers of this place, Julius Victor, tribune.
EDCS-07900011.summary: A dedication to the local gods by a military tribune named Julius Victor, date uncertain.

EDCS-07900012.text_edition: \n Deae Dianae sacrum Aelia Timo posuit votum solvens laeta libens merito\n
EDCS-07900012.translation: Sacred to the goddess Diana. Aelia Timo set this up, gladly, willingly, and deservedly fulfilling her vow.
EDCS-07900012.summary: A votive dedication to the goddess Diana by Aelia Timo, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-07900013.text_edition: \n Fortunae sacrum Gaius Valerius Longinus tribunus Fortunae sacrum Gaius Valerius Longinus tribunus\n
EDCS-07900013.translation: Sacred to Fortune. Gaius Valerius Longinus, tribune. Sacred to Fortune. Gaius Valerius Longinus, tribune.
EDCS-07900013.summary: A repetitive dedication to the goddess Fortune by Gaius Valerius Longinus, a tribune, date unknown.

EDCS-07900014.text_edition: \n Fortunae Augustae Aelia Proculina votum solvit\n
EDCS-07900014.translation: To Fortune the Augusta, Aelia Proculina fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-07900014.summary: A votive dedication to the goddess Fortune the Augusta by Aelia Proculina, date unknown.

EDCS-07900015.text_edition: \n Fortunae Reduci Iulius Severinus tribunus explicito balineo votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-07900015.translation: To Fortune the Rescuer, Julius Severinus, tribune, after the bathhouse was opened, fulfilled his vow gladly, willingly, and deservedly.
EDCS-07900015.summary: A dedication to Fortune the Rescuer by Julius Severinus, a tribune, possibly related to the inauguration of a bathhouse, date unknown.

EDCS-07900016.text_edition: \n Deo Herculi Iulius Paullus tribunus votum solvit\n
EDCS-07900016.translation: To the god Hercules, Julius Paullus, tribune, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07900016.summary: A votive dedication to the god Hercules by Julius Paullus, a tribune, date unknown.

EDCS-07900019.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo vexillatio Gaesatorum Raetorum quorum curam agit Aemilius Aemilianus tribunus cohortis I Vangionum\n
EDCS-07900019.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, by the standard-bearer detachment of the Raeti Gaesati, whose care is undertaken by Aemilius Aemilianus, tribune of the I Vangionum cohort.
EDCS-07900019.summary: A dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, concerning a detachment of auxiliary troops (Gaesati Raetorum) under the command of a tribune from the I Vangionum cohort, date uncertain.

EDCS-07900020.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo vexillatio Gaesatorum Raetorum quorum curam agit Iulius Victor tribunus cohortis I Vangionum\n
EDCS-07900020.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, the detachment of Raetian javelin-throwers, whose commander is Julius Victor, tribune of the I Cohort of Vangiones.
EDCS-07900020.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter by a military detachment of Raetian javelin-throwers under the command of a tribune, likely from the 1st to 3rd century CE.

EDCS-07900023.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolocheno Gaius Iulius Publilius Pius tribunus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-07900023.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, and Dolochenus, Gaius Julius Publilius Pius, tribune, gladly and deservedly paid his vow.
EDCS-07900023.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Jupiter and Dolochenus by Gaius Julius Publilius Pius, a tribune, likely from the 1st to 3rd century CE.

EDCS-07900024.text_edition: \n Marti Victori Iulius Publilius Pius tribunus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-07900024.translation: To Mars the Victorious, Julius Publilius Pius, tribune, gladly and deservedly paid his vow.
EDCS-07900024.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Mars the Victorious by Julius Publilius Pius, a tribune, likely from the 1st to 3rd century CE.

EDCS-07900026.text_edition: \n Marti Victori nius Pr tribun libertus ex voto solvit libens animo\n
EDCS-07900026.translation: To Mars the Victorious, [---]nius Pr, a freedman, fulfilled his vow with a willing heart.
EDCS-07900026.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Mars the Victorious by a freedman, likely from the 1st to 3rd century CE.

EDCS-07900027.text_edition: \n Matribus Tramarinis Iulius Victor votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-07900027.translation: To the Transmarine Mothers, Julius Victor paid his vow gladly and deservedly.
EDCS-07900027.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to the Transmarine Mothers by Julius Victor, likely from the 1st to 3rd century CE.

EDCS-07900028.text_edition: \n Deo Mogonito Cad et numini domini nostri Augusti Marcus Gavius Secundinus beneficiarius consularis Habitanci prima statione pro se et suis posuit\n
EDCS-07900028.translation: To the god Mogonitus, and to the divine spirit of our lord Augustus, Marcus Gavius Secundinus, an auxiliary soldier serving as a beneficiarius of a consular legate, placed this for himself and his family at the first station.
EDCS-07900028.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the god Mogonitus and the deified emperor Augustus by Marcus Gavius Secundinus, a military official, likely from the 1st to 3rd century CE.

EDCS-07900029.text_edition: \n Deo Moguno Cad Inventus dono votum solvit\n
EDCS-07900029.translation: To the god Mogunus, Cad Inventus paid his vow as a gift.
EDCS-07900029.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to the god Mogunus by Cad Inventus, likely from the 1st to 3rd century CE.

EDCS-07900031.text_edition: \n Somnio praemonitus miles hanc ponere iussit aram quae Fabio nupta est Nymphis venerandis\n
EDCS-07900031.translation: Warned by a dream, a soldier ordered this altar to be set up, which was dedicated to the venerable Nymphs and to his wife Fabia.
EDCS-07900031.summary: This inscription records the erection of an altar dedicated to the Nymphs and the wife of a soldier, prompted by a dream, likely from the 1st to 3rd century CE.

EDCS-07900032.text_edition: \n pro salute Arrunti Paulini Theodotus libertus\n
EDCS-07900032.translation: For the health of Arruntius Paulinus, Theodotus, his freedman.
EDCS-07900032.summary: This inscription records a dedication for the well-being of Arruntius Paulinus by his freedman, likely from the 1st to 3rd century CE.

EDCS-07900034.text_edition: \n cohors I Vangionum equitata milliaria cui praeest Marcus Peregrinius Super tribunus\n
EDCS-07900034.translation: The I Cohort of Vangiones, an equestrian unit of a thousand men, which is commanded by Marcus Peregrinius Super, tribune.
EDCS-07900034.summary: This inscription records the commander of the I Cohort of Vangiones, an equestrian unit, likely from the 1st to 3rd century CE.

EDCS-07900037.text_edition: \n Imperatoribus Caesaribus Lucio Septimio Severo Pio Pertinaci Arabico Adiabenico Parthico Maximo consuli III et Marco Aurelio Antonino Pio consuli II Augustis et Publio Septimio Getae nobilissimo Caesari portam cum muris vetustate dilapsis iussu Alfeni Senecionis viri clarissimi consularis curante Oclatinio Advento procuratore Augustorum nostrorum cohors I Vangionum |(milliaria) equitata cum Aemilio Salviano tribuno suo a solo restituit\n
EDCS-07900037.translation: Under Emperors Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus, consul for the third time, and Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Augustus, consul for the second time, and the most noble Caesar Publius Septimius Geta, the First Cohort of Vangiones, a milliary unit with cavalry, with its tribune Aemilius Salvianus, rebuilt the gate and the walls, which had fallen into ruin through age, by order of Alfennius Senecio, a man of consular rank, under the supervision of Oclatinus Adventus, procurator of our Augusti.
EDCS-07900037.summary: This is a military inscription recording the rebuilding of a gate and walls by the First Cohort of Vangiones under the emperors Septimius Severus, Caracalla, and Geta, dating to their joint consulships between 205 and 208 AD.

EDCS-07900040.text_edition: \n imperatoris Caesaris Marci Aureli Antonini Pii Augusti Marcus Messorius Diligens tribunus sacrum\n
EDCS-07900040.translation: Marcus Messorius Diligens, tribune, dedicated this sacred offering by order of Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Augustus.
EDCS-07900040.summary: This is a dedication by a military tribune, Marcus Messorius Diligens, under Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius (Caracalla), likely dating to his reign (198-217 AD).

EDCS-07900044.text_edition: \n Cohors I Vangionum fecit curante Iulio Paullo tribuno\n
EDCS-07900044.translation: The First Cohort of Vangiones built this, under the supervision of Julius Paulus, tribune.
EDCS-07900044.summary: This is a building inscription by the First Cohort of Vangiones, mentioning their tribune Julius Paulus, likely from the late 2nd or early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07900049.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Aemilianus, aged 10.
EDCS-07900049.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Aemilianus, who died at the age of 10.

EDCS-07900050.text_edition: \n Iulius Victor signifer vixit annos LV\n
EDCS-07900050.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Julius Victor, a standard-bearer, who died at the age of 55.

EDCS-07900051.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Satrius Honoratus vixit annis V mensibus VIII\n
EDCS-07900051.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Satrius Honoratus, aged 5 years and 8 months.
EDCS-07900051.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Satrius Honoratus, who died at the age of 5 years and 8 months.

EDCS-07900052.text_edition: \n miles cohortis IIII Gallorum stipendiorum XIIII defunctus vixsit annis XXXXIIII\n
EDCS-07900052.translation: A soldier of the Fourth Cohort of Gauls, with 14 years of service, died aged 42 years.
EDCS-07900052.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for a soldier of the Fourth Cohort of Gauls, who died after 14 years of service at the age of 42.

EDCS-07900053.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Aureliae Lupulae matri piissimae Dionysius Fortunatus filius sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-07900053.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. To Aurelia Lupula, a most dutiful mother. Dionysius Fortunatus, her son. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-07900053.summary: This is a tombstone inscription set up by a son, Dionysius Fortunatus, for his devoted mother, Aurelia Lupula.

EDCS-07900054.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Aurelia Quartilla vixit annis XIII mensibus I V diebus XXII Aurelius Quartinus posuit filiae suae\n
EDCS-07900054.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Aurelia Quartilla lived 13 years, 1 month, and 22 days. Aurelius Quartinus set this up for his daughter.
EDCS-07900054.summary: This is a tombstone inscription set up by Aurelius Quartinus for his daughter, Aurelia Quartilla, who died at the age of 13 years, 1 month, and 22 days.

EDCS-07900055.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Iuliona ni filia vixit annos XVI menses XI dies XIIII\n
EDCS-07900055.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Iuliona, daughter of [---], lived 16 years, 11 months, and 14 days.
EDCS-07900055.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Iuliona, who died at the age of 16 years, 11 months, and 14 days.

EDCS-07900057.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Blescius Diovicus filiae suae vixsit annum I et dies XXI\n
EDCS-07900057.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Blescius Diovicus lived one year and twenty-one days for his daughter.
EDCS-07900057.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Blescius Diovicus, likely a child, set up by a parent.

EDCS-07900060.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Claus lived.
EDCS-07900060.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph recording the name Claus and noting they lived.

EDCS-07900061.text_edition: \n filia Coerani vixit annos XVIII sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-07900061.translation: Daughter of Coeranus, she lived eighteen years. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-07900061.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the name of a daughter of Coeranus and her age at death.

EDCS-07900062.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Uxian Mufo lived.
EDCS-07900062.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph recording the name Uxian Mufo and noting they lived.

EDCS-07900065.text_edition: \n Genio domini nostri et Signorum cohortis I Vardullorum et numeri Exploratorum Bremeniensium Gordiani Egnatius Lucilianus legatus Augusti pro praetore curante Cassio Sabiniano tribuno\n
EDCS-07900065.translation: To the Genius of our Lord Emperor and the Standards of the 1st Cohort of Vardullians and the group of Gordian Explorers, Ignatius Lucilianus, legate of the emperor with praetorian power, dedicated it, with Cassius Sabinianus, tribune, overseeing the work.
EDCS-07900065.summary: This inscription records the dedication of an altar or monument to the emperor's genius and military standards by a legate and overseen by a tribune, likely from the 2nd century AD based on the title 'Gordiani'.

EDCS-07900066.text_edition: \n Genio et Signis cohortis I Fidae Vardullorum civium Romanorum equitatae Titus Licinius Valerianus tribunus\n
EDCS-07900066.translation: To the Genius and Standards of the 1st Cohort of Vardullians, Loyal, Roman citizens, equipped for cavalry, Titus Licinius Valerianus, tribune, dedicated it.
EDCS-07900066.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a monument to the genius and standards of a specific Roman cohort by its tribune, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-07900068.text_edition: \n Deo Matuno pro salute Marci Aureli bono generis humani imperante Gaius Iulius Marcus legatus Augusti pro praetore posuit ac dedicavit curam agente Caecilio Optato tribuno\n
EDCS-07900068.translation: To the god Matunus, for the safety of Marcus Aurelius, of good stock of humanity, while Gaius Julius Marcus, legate of the emperor with praetorian power, ruled, Gaius Caecilius Optatus, tribune, placed and dedicated it, with Caecilius Optatus, tribune, supervising the work.
EDCS-07900068.summary: This inscription records the dedication of an altar to the god Matunus for the emperor's well-being by a legate and overseen by a tribune, likely dating to the late 2nd century AD given the mention of Marcus Aurelius as emperor.

EDCS-07900069.text_edition: \n Deae Minervae Iulius Carantus singularis consularis\n
EDCS-07900069.translation: To the goddess Minerva, Julius Carantus, a singular former consular.
EDCS-07900069.summary: This is a dedication to the goddess Minerva by Julius Carantus, identified as a former singular official, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-07900070.text_edition: \n Deae sanctae Minervae Flavius Severinus tribunus aram dedit\n
EDCS-07900070.translation: To the holy goddess Minerva, Flavius Severinus, tribune, gave an altar.
EDCS-07900070.summary: This inscription records a tribune named Flavius Severinus dedicating an altar to the goddess Minerva.

EDCS-07900071.text_edition: \n Deae Minervae et Genio collegi Caecilius Optatus tribunus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-07900071.translation: To the goddess Minerva and the Genius of the college, Caecilius Optatus, tribune, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07900071.summary: This inscription records a tribune named Caecilius Optatus fulfilling a vow to Minerva and the genius of a college.

EDCS-07900072.text_edition: \n Dis Moguntibus Iulius Firminus decurio fecit\n
EDCS-07900072.translation: Julius Firminus, decurion, made this for the gods Moguntes.
EDCS-07900072.summary: This is a dedication to the gods Moguntes by Julius Firminus, a decurion.

EDCS-07900073.text_edition: \n Deae Romae sacrum duplicarii numeri exploratorum Bremeniensium aram instituerunt natali eius curante Caepione Charitino tribuno votum solventes libentes merito\n
EDCS-07900073.translation: The duplicarii of the scouting unit at Bremen erected this altar to the goddess Roma, fulfilling their vow with joy and deservingness on her birthday, under the supervision of Tribune Caepio Charitinus.
EDCS-07900073.summary: This altar was dedicated to the goddess Roma by soldiers of the scouting unit at Bremen, fulfilling a vow under Tribune Caepio Charitinus.

EDCS-07900074.text_edition: \n Silvano Pantheo pro salute Rufini tribuni et Lucillae eius Eutychus libertus cum suis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-07900074.translation: Eutychus, freedman, with his dependants, joyfully and deservingly fulfilled his vow to Silvanus Pantheus for the safety of Tribune Rufinus and his wife Lucilla.
EDCS-07900074.summary: This is a vow fulfillment for the safety of Tribune Rufinus and Lucilla, made by his freedman Eutychus.

EDCS-07900075.text_edition: \n Deo Invicto et Soli socio sacrum pro salute et incolumitate imperatoris Caesaris Marci Aureli Antonini Pii Felicis Augusti Lucius Caecilius Optatus tribunus cohortis I Vardullorum cum consecraneis votum Deo a solo extructum\n
EDCS-07900075.translation: Sacred to the Unconquered God and Sol, his companion. For the safety and well-being of the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, Lucius Caecilius Optatus, tribune of the I Vardullorum cohort, with his fellow soldiers, fulfilled a vow to the god, having built from the ground up.
EDCS-07900075.summary: This dedication to Sol Invictus and Sol by Lucius Caecilius Optatus, tribune of the I Vardullorum cohort, is for the emperor's safety and well-being.

EDCS-07900076.text_edition: \n Victoriae et Paci Iulius Melanio tribunus Imperatore Volusiano et Publicola sic consulibus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-07900076.translation: To Victoria and Pax. Tribune Julius Melanio fulfilled his vow with joy and deservingly, when Volusianus and Publicola were consuls.
EDCS-07900076.summary: Tribune Julius Melanio dedicated this to Victoria and Pax during the consulship of Volusianus and Publicola.

EDCS-07900079.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Tito Aelio Hadriano Antonino Augusto Pio patri patriae sub Quinto Lollio Urbico legato Augusti pro praetore cohors I Lingonum equitata fecit\n
EDCS-07900079.translation: The I Lingonum equitata cohort made this for Emperor Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, father of the country, under Quintus Lollius Urbicus, imperial legate with proconsular authority.
EDCS-07900079.summary: This monument was erected by the I Lingonum equitata cohort for Emperor Antoninus Pius, under the governor Quintus Lollius Urbicus.

EDCS-07900082.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Severo Antonino Pio Felici Augusto Parthico Maximo Britannico Maximo Germanico Maximo pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate XVIIII Imperatori III consuli IIII proconsuli patri patriae cohors I Fida Vardullorum civium Romanorum equitata Antoniniana fecit sub cura [] [] legati Augusti pro praetore\n
EDCS-07900082.translation: The I Fida Vardullorum civium Romanorum equitata Antoniniana cohort made this for Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, Parthicus Maximus, Britannicus Maximus, Germanicus Maximus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the 19th year, consul for the 3rd time, governor for the 4th time, father of the country, under the supervision of [ ] [ ], imperial legate with proconsular authority.
EDCS-07900082.summary: This is a dedication by the I Fida Vardullorum equitata Antoniniana cohort for Emperor Caracalla, likely dating to his later reign based on his titles.

EDCS-07900083.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Antonino Pio Felici Augusto tribunicia potestate III consuli III proconsuli patri patriae ballistarium a solo cohors I fida Vardullorum Antoniniana sub cura Tiberi Claudi Paulini legati Augusti pro praetore fecit instante Publio Aelio Erasino tribuno\n
EDCS-07900083.translation: The I fida Vardullorum Antoniniana cohort made this ballistarium from the ground up for Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, with tribunician power for the 3rd year, consul for the 3rd time, governor, father of the country, under the supervision of Tiberius Claudius Paulinus, imperial legate with proconsular authority, with Publius Aelius Erasinus, tribune, overseeing.
EDCS-07900083.summary: This ballistarium was built by the I Fida Vardullorum Antoniniana cohort for Emperor Caracalla, under Legate Tiberius Claudius Paulinus and supervised by Tribune Publius Aelius Erasinus.

EDCS-07900089.summary: This names Publius Aelius Erasinus as a tribune, likely a standalone dedication or part of a larger inscription.

EDCS-07900091.text_edition: \n S HIIIIIII cohortis I Vardulorum praefecto cohortis I Augustae Lusitanorum item cohortis I Breucorum subcuratori viae Flaminiae et alimentorum subcuratori operum publicorum Iulia Lucilla clarissima femina marito bene merenti vixit annos XLVIII menses VI dies XXV\n
EDCS-07900091.translation: Iulia Lucilla, most distinguished lady, lived for 48 years, 6 months, and 25 days, for her well-deserving husband, prefect of the I cohort of Vardullorum, prefect of the I Augustan cohort of Lusitanorum, also curator of the I cohort of Breucorum, subcurator of the Flaminian Way and of food supplies, subcurator of public works.
EDCS-07900091.summary: This is a funerary epigram for Iulia Lucilla, recording her age and listing her husband's various military and administrative titles.

EDCS-07900093.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Felicionis liberti vixit annis XX\n
EDCS-07900093.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Felicio, freedman, lived 20 years.
EDCS-07900093.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a freedman named Felicio, who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-07900094.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Hermagora alumno Honoratus tribunus\n
EDCS-07900094.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Hermagora, a ward, honored by Honoratus, tribune.
EDCS-07900094.summary: This is a funerary inscription honoring Hermagora, a ward, by Honoratus, a tribune.

EDCS-07900095.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Gaius Iulius Gai filius Flavia Ingenuus miles legionis VI victricis piae fidelis\n
EDCS-07900095.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Gaius Julius Ingenuus, son of Gaius Julius, a soldier of the VI Victrix legion, pious and loyal.
EDCS-07900095.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Julius Ingenuus, a soldier of the VI Victrix legion.

EDCS-07900102.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo cohors IIII Lingonum equitata cui attendit Iulius Honoratus (|centurio) legionis II Augustae votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-07900102.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, the 4th cohort of Lingones, cavalry, under the command of Julius Honoratus, centurion of the II Augusta legion, happily and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07900102.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by the 4th cohort of Lingones, cavalry, under the command of Julius Honoratus, a centurion of the II Augusta legion, to Jupiter.

EDCS-07900103.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Aelius Rufus praefectus cohortis IIII Lingonum\n
EDCS-07900103.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest. Aelius Rufus, prefect of the 4th cohort of Lingones.
EDCS-07900103.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter by Aelius Rufus, prefect of the 4th cohort of Lingones.

EDCS-07900105.text_edition: \n Didius Severus praefectus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-07900105.translation: Didius Severus, prefect, happily and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07900105.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by Didius Severus, a prefect.

EDCS-07900108.text_edition: \n typum cum basi et templum fecit Gaius Iulius Maximinus |(centurio) legionis VI Victricis ex voto\n
EDCS-07900108.translation: Gaius Julius Maximinus, centurion of the VI Victrix legion, made the statue with its base and a temple, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-07900108.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a statue, base, and temple by Gaius Julius Maximinus, a centurion of the VI Victrix legion, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-07900113.translation: Of the cohort, the century of Varius Celer.
EDCS-07900113.summary: This inscription likely marks a boundary or dedication related to the century of Varius Celer within a cohort.

EDCS-07900114.translation: Of the cohort, the century of Sentius Priscus.
EDCS-07900114.summary: This inscription likely marks a boundary or dedication related to the century of Sentius Priscus within a cohort.

EDCS-07900115.translation: Of the cohort, the century of Iustinus Secundus.
EDCS-07900115.summary: This inscription likely marks a boundary or dedication related to the century of Iustinus Secundus within a cohort.

EDCS-07900118.text_edition: \n centuria Iuli Numisiani Ulpius Canalius et Lucius Goutius\n
EDCS-07900118.translation: Ulpius Canalius and Lucius Goutius of the century of Iulius Numisianus.
EDCS-07900118.summary: This inscription records the dedication or marking by members of a Roman military century, likely associated with Iulius Numisianus, possibly related to a boundary or construction project.

EDCS-07900121.text_edition: \n Deabus Matribus Tramarinis Patriis Aurelius Iuvenalis sacrum\n
EDCS-07900121.translation: Aurelius Iuvenalis dedicates this sacred offering to the Deae Matres Tramarinae Patriae.
EDCS-07900121.summary: This is a religious dedication made by Aurelius Iuvenalis to the "Tramarine", "Patrician" Mother Goddesses, likely a local cult.

EDCS-07900124.translation: Gaius Valerius to the god Silvanus.
EDCS-07900124.summary: This is a simple dedication to the god Silvanus by Gaius Valerius, likely an individual offering.

EDCS-07900125.text_edition: \n Imperatori Antonino Augusto Pio patri patriae vexillatio legionis II Augustae et legionis XX Valeriae victricis contributi exercitibus Germaniis duobus sub Iulio Vero legato Augusti pro praetore\n
EDCS-07900125.translation: To the Emperor Antoninus Augustus Pius, father of the fatherland, from the contributing soldiers of the II Augusta and XX Valeria Victrix legions, two armies from Germania, under Julius Verus, imperial legate with praetorian power.
EDCS-07900125.summary: This inscription is a dedication by two legions, II Augusta and XX Valeria Victrix, to Emperor Antoninus Pius, likely commemorating military operations or a building project in Germania during the prefecture of Julius Verus.

EDCS-07900130.text_edition: \n Deo Antenocitico et Numinibus Augustorum Aelius Vibius legionis XX Valeriae victricis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-07900130.translation: Aelius Vibius of the XX Valeria Victrix legion willingly and deservedly paid his vow to the god Antenociticus and the Numinia of the Emperors.
EDCS-07900130.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by Aelius Vibius of the XX Valeria Victrix legion to the god Antenociticus and the imperial divinities, likely from the 2nd century AD.

EDCS-07900131.text_edition: \n Deo Antenocitico sacrum cohors I Vangionum quibus praeest cius Cassianus praefectus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-07900131.translation: Sacred to the god Antenociticus: Cohors I Vangionum, commanded by its prefect Cassianus, willingly and deservedly paid its vow.
EDCS-07900131.summary: This is a religious dedication by the First Cohort of Vangiones, led by Prefect Cassianus, to the god Antenociticus, likely from the 2nd century AD.

EDCS-07900132.text_edition: \n Deo Anocitico iudiciis Optimorum Maximorumque Imperatorum Nostrorum sub Ulpio Marcello consulari Tineius Longus in prefectura equitum lato clavo exornatus et quaestor designatus\n
EDCS-07900132.translation: To the god Anociticus, by the judgments of our Best and Greatest Emperors, Tineius Longus, honored with the broad stripe and designated quaestor, in his prefecture of cavalry, under Ulpius Marcellus as consular.
EDCS-07900132.summary: This inscription records Tineius Longus, a military official and aspiring senator, dedicating an offering to the god Anociticus under the consular authority of Ulpius Marcellus, likely in the late 2nd or early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07900133.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno et Numinibus Augustorum pro salute imperatoris Caesaris Titi Aeli Hadriani Antonini Augusti Pii patris patriae et legionis II Augustae Marcus Liburnius Fronto legionis eiusdem votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-07900133.translation: Marcus Liburnius Fronto of the same legion willingly and deservedly paid his vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus and the Numinia of the Emperors for the safety of Emperor Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, father of the fatherland, and of the II Augusta legion.
EDCS-07900133.summary: This inscription records Marcus Liburnius Fronto of the II Augusta legion making a votive offering to Jupiter Dolichenus and the imperial divinities for the safety of Emperor Antoninus Pius and his legion, likely from the mid-2nd century AD.

EDCS-07900135.translation: Lenuanus paid his vow to the god Mars.
EDCS-07900135.summary: This is a simple votive inscription to the god Mars by an individual named Lenuanus.

EDCS-07900136.text_edition: \n Deo Marti Victori Vindex votum solvit\n
EDCS-07900136.translation: Vindex paid his vow to the victorious god Mars.
EDCS-07900136.summary: This is a votive inscription made by Vindex to Mars Victor, the victorious aspect of the god Mars.

EDCS-07900137.text_edition: \n Matriribus Campestribus et Genio alae primae Hispanorum Asturum Gordianae Titus Agrippa praefectus templum a solo restituit\n
EDCS-07900137.translation: To the Campestrian Mothers and the Genius of the First Wing of Asturians of Hispania Gordiana, Titus Agrippa, prefect, restored the temple from the ground up.
EDCS-07900137.summary: This inscription records the restoration of a temple by Titus Agrippa, prefect of the ala I Hispanorum Asturum Gordiana, likely during the reign of the Gordians.

EDCS-07900140.text_edition: \n Victoriae Auggustorum Alfeno Senecione consulari felix ala I Astorum M praefecto\n
EDCS-07900140.translation: To Victoria Augustorum, from Alfeno Senecione, consular, happy, from the first wing of Asturians, with the prefect M.
EDCS-07900140.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Victoria Augustorum by Alfeno Senecione, a consular official, and members of the ala I Astorum, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07900143.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Traiano Hadriano Augusto Aulo Platorio Nepote legato Augusti pro praetore vexillatio classis Britannicae\n
EDCS-07900143.translation: For the Emperor Caesar Trajan Hadrian Augustus, from Aulus Platorius Nepos, imperial legate pro praetore, and the detachment of the British fleet.
EDCS-07900143.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Aulus Platorius Nepos, a Roman governor, and a detachment of the British fleet to Emperor Hadrian, likely from the mid-2nd century AD.

EDCS-07900153.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Decimus Iulius Quinti filius Candidus cohortis primae Vangionum annorum XXXX\n
EDCS-07900153.translation: Dedicated to the Spirits of the Departed. Decimus Julius Candidus, son of Quintus, of the First Cohort of Vangiones, aged 40.
EDCS-07900153.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Decimus Julius Candidus, a soldier of the cohors I Vangionum, who died at the age of 40, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07900154.translation: Dedicated to the Spirits of the Departed. To Publius Sermullius Martialis.
EDCS-07900154.summary: This is a simple tombstone inscription dedicating a memorial to Publius Sermullius Martialis.

EDCS-07900159.summary: This inscription likely marks a boundary or property belonging to the century (military unit or administrative division) of Iulius Rufus.

EDCS-07900160.summary: This inscription likely marks a boundary or property belonging to the century (military unit or administrative division) of Iulius Rufus.

EDCS-07900164.summary: This inscription likely marks a boundary or property belonging to the century (military unit or administrative division) of Iulius.

EDCS-07900165.summary: This inscription likely marks a boundary or property belonging to the century (military unit or administrative division) of Valerius Flavus.

EDCS-07900166.summary: This inscription likely marks a boundary or property belonging to the century (military unit or administrative division) of Valerius Flavus.

EDCS-07900167.summary: This inscription records the name of a centurial unit, the century of Proculus.

EDCS-07900168.text_edition: \n Cohortis I Dacorum centuria Aeli Didae\n
EDCS-07900168.translation: The century of Aelius Didas of the First Cohort of Dacians.
EDCS-07900168.summary: This inscription records the name of a centurial unit within the First Cohort of Dacians, the century of Aelius Didas.

EDCS-07900171.summary: This inscription records the name of a centurial unit, the century of Avidus Rufus.

EDCS-07900202.summary: This inscription appears to record the name of a person, Claudius, possibly with his father's name partially preserved.

EDCS-07900203.text_edition: \n Deo Invicto Mytrae Publius Aelius Titullus praefectus votum solvit libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-07900203.translation: To the Unconquered God Mithras, Publius Aelius Titullus, prefect, willingly, joyfully, and deservedly paid his vow.
EDCS-07900203.summary: This inscription records a vow paid by Publius Aelius Titullus, a prefect, to the god Mithras.

EDCS-07900204.text_edition: \n Deo Soli Invicto Tiberius Claudius Decimus Cornelius Antonius praefectus templum restituit\n
EDCS-07900204.translation: To the Unconquered Sun God Sol, Tiberius Claudius Decimus Cornelius Antonius, prefect, restored the temple.
EDCS-07900204.summary: This inscription records the restoration of a temple to the god Sol by Tiberius Claudius Decimus Cornelius Antonius, a prefect.

EDCS-07900205.text_edition: \n Soli Apollini Aniceto Mithrae Aponius Rogatianus \n
EDCS-07900205.translation: To Sol Apollo Anicetus Mithras, Aponius Rogatianus.
EDCS-07900205.summary: This inscription records the dedication of something to the gods Sol Apollo Anicetus and Mithras by Aponius Rogatianus.

EDCS-07900206.text_edition: \n Deo Lucius Sentius Castus |(centurio) legionis VI dono posuit\n
EDCS-07900206.translation: Lucius Sentius Castus, centurion of the Sixth Legion, placed this as a gift to the god.
EDCS-07900206.summary: This inscription records a gift made by Lucius Sentius Castus, a centurion of the Sixth Legion, to a deity.

EDCS-07900208.translation: The century of Pedi Quintus of the Fourth Cohort.
EDCS-07900208.summary: This inscription records the name of a centurial unit within the Fourth Cohort, the century of Pedi Quintus.

EDCS-07900210.summary: This inscription records the name of a centurial unit, the century of Arrius.

EDCS-07900217.translation: The century of Flavius Latinus of the Eighth Cohort.
EDCS-07900217.summary: This is a military inscription identifying the century of Flavius Latinus within the Eighth Cohort, likely found on a tombstone or military equipment.

EDCS-07900218.summary: This military inscription identifies the century of Hostilius Lupus, probably on a tombstone or military equipment.

EDCS-07900220.summary: This is a military inscription identifying the century of Vocontius Co[--], likely on a tombstone or military equipment.

EDCS-07900224.summary: This military inscription identifies the century of Turrianus Priscus, likely on a tombstone or military equipment.

EDCS-07900228.translation: Vilidedius, the heir, set this up to the Divine Shades.
EDCS-07900228.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording that Vilidedius erected a monument to a deceased person.

EDCS-07900229.text_edition: \n Matribus templum cum ara vexillatio cohortis I Vardullorum instante Publio D V votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-07900229.translation: A detachment of the First Cohort of Vardulli, with Publius D. V. presiding, willingly and deservedly fulfilled a vow to build a temple with an altar to the Mothers.
EDCS-07900229.summary: This is a dedication of a temple and altar to the Matres (Mothers) by a military unit, the First Cohort of Vardulli, during the tenure of Publius D. V.

EDCS-07900234.summary: This inscription likely refers to a divine omen, possibly a lightning strike, interpreted as a sign from the gods.

EDCS-07900241.text_edition: \n rmat civis Norici annorum XXX Messorius Magnus frater eius duplicarius aliae Sabinianae faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-07900241.translation: His brother Messorius Magnus, a duplicarius of the Alia Sabiniana, had this made for Noricus, a citizen, aged thirty.
EDCS-07900241.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Noricus, a citizen aged thirty, and stating that his brother, Messorius Magnus, a cavalryman, commissioned the monument.

EDCS-07900243.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aureliae Victorinae Aurelius Victor pater filiae\n
EDCS-07900243.translation: Aurelius Victor, father, for his daughter Aurelia Victorina, to the Divine Shades.
EDCS-07900243.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording that Aurelius Victor set up a monument for his daughter, Aurelia Victorina.

EDCS-07900247.translation: The century of Stabilius Solon of the Sixth Cohort.
EDCS-07900247.summary: This military inscription identifies the century of Stabilius Solon within the Sixth Cohort, likely on a tombstone or military equipment.

EDCS-07900248.translation: The century of Caecilius Clemens of the Eighth Cohort.
EDCS-07900248.summary: This inscription records a military unit, specifically a century within the Eighth Cohort.

EDCS-07900249.translation: The century of Volusianus of the Third Cohort.
EDCS-07900249.summary: This inscription records a military unit, specifically a century within the Third Cohort.

EDCS-07900250.summary: This inscription likely records a boundary stone or marker belonging to Flavius Carantinus.

EDCS-07900252.translation: The century of Paulus Aper of the Ninth Cohort.
EDCS-07900252.summary: This inscription records a military unit, specifically a century within the Ninth Cohort.

EDCS-07900253.text_edition: \n Turma Luci A Fani per vallum pedes CXIII turma Luci A Fani\n
EDCS-07900253.translation: The troop of Lucius, son of Aefanus, 113 paces along the rampart; the troop of Lucius, son of Aefanus.
EDCS-07900253.summary: This inscription records a measurement related to military construction, likely marking a section of the rampart belonging to the troop of Lucius Aefanus.

EDCS-07900255.summary: This inscription records a military unit, specifically a century belonging to Pompeius Rufus.

EDCS-07900256.translation: To the Good Goddess, Queen, Celestial.
EDCS-07900256.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the Good Goddess (Bona Dea), an important female deity in Roman religion.

EDCS-07900257.text_edition: \n Deae Fortunae Conservatrici Venenus Germanus libens merito\n
EDCS-07900257.translation: To the Goddess Fortuna the Preserver, Venenus Germanus willingly, as deserved.
EDCS-07900257.summary: This inscription is a votive offering to the goddess Fortuna for her protective qualities, made by Venenus Germanus.

EDCS-07900260.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno pro salute Augustorum Nostrorum Galerius Verecundus \n
EDCS-07900260.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest Dolichenus, for the safety of Our Emperors, Galerius Verecundus.
EDCS-07900260.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Dolichenus for the well-being of the emperors, made by Galerius Verecundus.

EDCS-07900262.translation: To the Goddess Ratio, [he/she] willingly fulfilled [his/her] vow.
EDCS-07900262.summary: This inscription is a votive offering to the goddess Ratio (Reason or Equity), where the vow was willingly fulfilled.

EDCS-07900263.text_edition: \n Deo sancto Vitiri Tertulus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-07900263.translation: Tertulus gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to the holy god Vitir.
EDCS-07900263.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to the god Vitir by Tertulus.

EDCS-07900266.text_edition: \n SVCNV SVOTVT deo Votri votum solvit\n
EDCS-07900266.translation: Svcnv fulfilled his vow to the god Votri.
EDCS-07900266.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to the god Votri by Svcnv.

EDCS-07900267.text_edition: \n Marcus Aurelius Ianuarius emeritus pro se et suis omnibus votum solvit libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-07900267.translation: Marcus Aurelius Ianuarius, a veteran, gladly, joyfully, and deservedly fulfilled his vow for himself and all his family.
EDCS-07900267.summary: This inscription records a veteran named Marcus Aurelius Ianuarius fulfilling a vow for his family.

EDCS-07900271.text_edition: \n Aqua adducta alae II Asturum sub Ulpio Marcello legato Augusti pro praetore\n
EDCS-07900271.translation: Water brought for the 2nd Wing of Astures, under Ulpius Marcellus, Imperial legate with praetorian power.
EDCS-07900271.summary: This inscription records an aqueduct project for the 2nd Wing of Astures during the governorship of Ulpius Marcellus.

EDCS-07900278.translation: Under the supervision of Aelius Longinus, prefect of cavalry.
EDCS-07900278.summary: This inscription records the supervision of a project by Aelius Longinus, a cavalry prefect.

EDCS-07900281.translation: From the century of Bassus of the 1st Cohort.
EDCS-07900281.summary: This inscription identifies a military unit, the century of Bassus from the 1st Cohort.

EDCS-07900282.translation: From the century of Flavius Civilis of the Cohort.
EDCS-07900282.summary: This inscription identifies a military unit, the century of Flavius Civilis.

EDCS-07900283.translation: From the century of Caecilius Proculus of the 5th Cohort.
EDCS-07900283.summary: This inscription identifies a military unit, the century of Caecilius Proculus from the 5th Cohort.

EDCS-07900284.translation: From the century of Caecilius Proculus of the 5th Cohort.
EDCS-07900284.summary: This inscription identifies a military unit, the century of Caecilius Proculus from the 5th Cohort.

EDCS-07900285.translation: From the century of Hortaeus Maximus.
EDCS-07900285.summary: This inscription identifies a military unit, the century of Hortaeus Maximus.

EDCS-07900287.summary: This is a military inscription, likely a boundary marker or unit designation, for the century of Similis.

EDCS-07900288.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aventino curatori alae II Asturum stipendiorum XV Aelius Gemellus decurio heres faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-07900288.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Aelius Gemellus, decurion and heir, arranged for this to be made for Aventinus, curator of the 2nd Wing of Astures, after 15 years of service.
EDCS-07900288.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Aventinus, a curator of the Astures cavalry wing, by his heir and decurion Aelius Gemellus, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-07900290.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Fabie Honorate Fabius Honoratus tribunus cohortis I Vangionum et Aurelia Eglectiane fecerunt filie dulcissime\n
EDCS-07900290.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Fabius Honoratus, tribune of the 1st Cohort of Vangiones, and Aurelia Eglectiane made this for their dearest daughter, Fabia Honorata.
EDCS-07900290.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Fabia Honorata by her parents, Fabius Honoratus, a tribune, and Aurelia Eglectiane, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-07900291.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Urse sorori Iulie coniugi Canioni filio Lurio Germanus\n
EDCS-07900291.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Germanus [erected this] for Ursa, his sister, and for Iulia, his wife, and for Lurio, his son.
EDCS-07900291.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Germanus to his sister Ursa, his wife Iulia, and his son Lurio, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-07900297.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that begins with the word 'stone' and the name Iulius, possibly part of a larger dedication or epitaph.

EDCS-07900306.translation: The century of Aelius Aelianus of the 9th Cohort.
EDCS-07900306.summary: This is a military inscription indicating the century of Aelius Aelianus within the 9th Cohort.

EDCS-07900307.translation: The century of Lousius Suavis of the 6th Cohort.
EDCS-07900307.summary: This is a military inscription indicating the century of Lousius Suavis within the 6th Cohort.

EDCS-07900308.translation: The century of Matellus Ursi of the 10th Cohort.
EDCS-07900308.summary: This is a military inscription indicating the century of Matellus Ursi within the 10th Cohort.

EDCS-07900320.summary: This is a military inscription, likely a boundary marker or unit designation, for the century of Adauctus Pudentis.

EDCS-07900321.summary: This is a military inscription, likely a boundary marker or unit designation, for the century of Antonius Felix.

EDCS-07900322.translation: Of the first century of Cohors X, belonging to Iulius Commidius.
EDCS-07900322.summary: This inscription records a military unit designation, specifically the first century of Cohors X, linked to an individual named Iulius Commidius.

EDCS-07900323.summary: This inscription records a military unit designation, specifically a century named after Hellenis.

EDCS-07900324.summary: This inscription records a military unit designation, specifically a century named after Nason.

EDCS-07900327.summary: This inscription records a military unit designation, specifically a century named after Romulus (or possibly a similar name like Romulus).

EDCS-07900329.translation: To the god Belleticaurus, from Lunaris.
EDCS-07900329.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the god Belleticaurus by an individual named Lunaris.

EDCS-07900330.text_edition: \n Deae Conventinae Bellicus votum solvens libens merito posuit\n
EDCS-07900330.translation: Bellicus willingly and deservedly set up this votive offering to the goddess Conventina.
EDCS-07900330.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by Bellicus to the goddess Conventina.

EDCS-07900331.text_edition: \n Deae Convetinae votum rettulit Mausaeus optio cohortis primae Frixiavonum\n
EDCS-07900331.translation: Mausaeus, optio of the first cohort of the Frixiavi, returned his vow to the goddess Conventina.
EDCS-07900331.summary: This inscription records a vow returned by Mausaeus, an optio of the first cohort of the Frixiavi, to the goddess Conventina.

EDCS-07900332.text_edition: \n Deae Coventine cohorti I Cubernorum Aurelius Campester votum posuit laeto animo\n
EDCS-07900332.translation: Aurelius Campester set up this votive offering with a joyful heart to the goddess Coventine for Cohors I of the Cuberni.
EDCS-07900332.summary: This inscription records a votive offering by Aurelius Campester for Cohors I of the Cuberni, dedicated to the goddess Coventine.

EDCS-07900333.translation: To the goddess Coventine, from Aurelius Crotus the German.
EDCS-07900333.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the goddess Coventine by Aurelius Crotus, identified as German.

EDCS-07900334.text_edition: \n Deae Nimfae Coventine Maduhus Germanus posuit pro se et suis votum solvens libens merito\n
EDCS-07900334.translation: Maduhus the German set up this votive offering, willingly and deservedly fulfilling his vow for himself and his family, to the goddess Nympha Coventine.
EDCS-07900334.summary: This inscription records a votive offering by Maduhus, a German, to the goddess Nympha Coventine, for himself and his family.

EDCS-07900335.text_edition: \n Nimphae Coventinae tianus decurio SLEV merito\n
EDCS-07900335.translation: To the Nymph Coventina, TIANUS, decurion, [dedicated this] deservedly.
EDCS-07900335.summary: This is a dedication to the nymph Coventina by TIANUS, a decurion, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07900336.text_edition: \n Deae Coventinae Vinomathus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-07900336.translation: VINOMATHUS gladly and deservedly paid his vow to the goddess Coventina.
EDCS-07900336.summary: This is a votive offering to the goddess Coventina by VINOMATHUS, who gladly fulfilled his vow, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07900337.text_edition: \n Deae Coventine Panus miles cohortis TTOIN votum libes animo reddidit et posiuit\n
EDCS-07900337.translation: PANUS, a soldier of the TTOIN cohort, gladly and with a willing spirit paid his vow and set this up to the goddess Coventina.
EDCS-07900337.summary: This inscription records a soldier named PANUS of the TTOIN cohort fulfilling a vow to the goddess Coventina, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07900338.summary: This inscription simply records the names Gabinius, son of Felicis, and Saturninus, possibly as owners or dedicators of a monument, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07900339.text_edition: \n Covetinae Augustae votum manibus suis Saturninus fecit Gabinius\n
EDCS-07900339.translation: Saturninus Gabinius made and set this up with his own hands to Coventina Augusta.
EDCS-07900339.summary: This is a dedication to Coventina Augusta by Saturninus Gabinius, who constructed the monument himself, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07900340.text_edition: \n Deae Covetine Crotus votum libens solvi pro mea salute\n
EDCS-07900340.translation: CROTUS gladly paid his vow for my health to the goddess Coventina.
EDCS-07900340.summary: This is a votive inscription by CROTUS to the goddess Coventina for his own health, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07900341.text_edition: \n Deae sanctae Covontine Vincentius pro salute sua voto libens laetus merito dedicavit\n
EDCS-07900341.translation: Vincentius gladly, happily, and deservedly dedicated this to the holy goddess Coventina for his own health.
EDCS-07900341.summary: This is a dedication to the goddess Coventina by Vincentius for his own health, who offered it gladly and deservedly, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07900342.text_edition: \n Deae Covventinae Titus D Cosconianus praefectus cohortis I Batavorum libens merito\n
EDCS-07900342.translation: Titus D. Cosconianus, prefect of the First Cohort of Batavians, [dedicated this] gladly and deservedly to the goddess Coventina.
EDCS-07900342.summary: This is a dedication to the goddess Coventina by Titus D. Cosconianus, prefect of the First Cohort of Batavians, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07900343.text_edition: \n Covventinae Aelius Tertius prefectus cohortis I Batavorum votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-07900343.translation: Aelius Tertius, prefect of the First Cohort of Batavians, gladly and deservedly paid his vow to Coventina.
EDCS-07900343.summary: This inscription records Aelius Tertius, prefect of the First Cohort of Batavians, fulfilling a vow to Coventina, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07900344.text_edition: \n Fortunae cohors I Batavorum cui praeest Marcus Flaccinius Marcellus praefectus\n
EDCS-07900344.translation: To Fortuna, the First Cohort of Batavians, commanded by Marcus Flaccinius Marcellus, prefect.
EDCS-07900344.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the goddess Fortuna by the First Cohort of Batavians, under the command of their prefect Marcus Flaccinius Marcellus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07900345.text_edition: \n Deae Fortunae Vitalis fecit libens merito\n
EDCS-07900345.translation: To the goddess Fortuna Vitalis, he made [this offering] willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-07900345.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicating an offering to the goddess Fortuna Vitalis, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-07900346.text_edition: \n Genio huius loci Texandri et Suvevae vexillarii cohortis II Nerviorum\n
EDCS-07900346.translation: To the genius of this place, Texandus and Suveva, standard-bearers of the Second Cohort of Nervians.
EDCS-07900346.summary: This inscription records the dedication of an offering to the genius loci by two standard-bearers of the Second Cohort of Nervians, likely from the Roman army.

EDCS-07900347.text_edition: \n Deae Matri Deum Tranquila Severa pro se et suis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-07900347.translation: Tranquilla Severa fulfilled her vow willingly and deservedly to the Mother of the Gods for herself and her family.
EDCS-07900347.summary: This is a votive inscription from Tranquilla Severa to the Mother of the Gods, offered for herself and her family, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-07900350.text_edition: \n Minervae Quintus architectus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-07900350.translation: Quintus the architect fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly to Minerva.
EDCS-07900350.summary: This votive inscription records Quintus, an architect, fulfilling a vow to the goddess Minerva, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-07900351.text_edition: \n Die Minerve Venico pro salute posuit sumptu suo\n
EDCS-07900351.translation: Venicus placed [this] to Minerva for [her] health at his own expense.
EDCS-07900351.summary: This inscription records Venicus dedicating something to Minerva for her well-being, funded by himself, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-07900352.text_edition: \n Deo Invicto Mithrae Lucius Antonius Proculus praefectus cohortis I Batavorum Antoninianae votum solvit libens merito\n
