EDCS-07900352.translation: Lucius Antonius Proculus, prefect of the First Cohort of Batavians Antoniniana, fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly to the Invincible God Mithras.
EDCS-07900352.summary: This is a votive inscription from Lucius Antonius Proculus, a prefect in the Roman army, dedicated to the god Mithras, likely from the 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07900353.text_edition: \n Deo Invicto Mithrae sacrum Aulus Cluentius Habitus praefectus cohortis I Batavorum domu Ultinia sic colonia Septimia Aurelia Larino votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-07900353.translation: Aulus Cluentius Habitus, prefect of the First Cohort of Batavians, of the Ultinian tribe, from the colony of Septimia Aurelia Larino, fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly to the Invincible God Mithras.
EDCS-07900353.summary: This votive inscription records Aulus Cluentius Habitus, a prefect of the First Cohort of Batavians, from Larino, dedicating an offering to the god Mithras, likely from the 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07900354.text_edition: \n Deo Invicto Mithrae Marcus Simplicius Simplex praefectus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-07900354.translation: Marcus Simplicius Simplex, prefect, fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly to the Invincible God Mithras.
EDCS-07900354.summary: This votive inscription records Marcus Simplicius Simplex, a prefect, fulfilling a vow to the god Mithras, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-07900356.text_edition: \n Deo Veteri votum Uccus vovit libens\n
EDCS-07900356.translation: Uccus vowed to the Old God willingly.
EDCS-07900356.summary: This is a votive inscription from Uccus to an Old God, likely from a provincial context and possibly pre-Roman or early Roman period.

EDCS-07900363.translation: From the centuria of Antonius Rusticus.
EDCS-07900363.summary: This inscription simply identifies a centuria belonging to Antonius Rusticus, likely marking land or a military division.

EDCS-07900366.text_edition: \n paudi Regulo vixit annis XXXIIII et atencte coniugi piae vixit annis XXX et iane filiae eorum vixit diebus filio eorum udivaiti filio PAHICE \n
EDCS-07900366.translation: Paudis lived 34 years, and to Atencte, his pious wife, who lived 30 years, and to Jane, their daughter, who lived [?] days, and to their son Udivaitus, son of Pahice.
EDCS-07900366.summary: This epitaph records the deaths of members of a family, including Paudis, his wife Atencte, and their children Jane and Udivaitus, with ages provided for the deceased.

EDCS-07900369.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aeliae Comindo annorum XXXII Nobilianus decurio coniugi carissimae posuit\n
EDCS-07900369.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Nobilianus, a decurion, erected this for Aelia Comindo, his dearest wife, aged 32.
EDCS-07900369.summary: This funerary inscription records the dedication of a monument by Nobilianus to his wife Aelia Comindo.

EDCS-07900370.text_edition: \n cohortis I Batavorum et Hilario heredes faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-07900370.translation: The heirs of the first Cohort of Batavians and of Hilarus saw to its construction.
EDCS-07900370.summary: This inscription indicates that the heirs of a military unit and an individual named Hilarus commissioned the construction of this monument.

EDCS-07900371.translation: [text lost] Ulpi [text lost] / [text lost] Sabin[text lost]
EDCS-07900371.summary: This fragmentary inscription contains the names Ulpius and Sabinus, likely part of a dedication or biographical record.

EDCS-07900375.summary: This inscription identifies a military unit, specifically the century belonging to Avidius Rufus.

EDCS-07900378.translation: The century of Caecilius Proclus of the Fifth Cohort.
EDCS-07900378.summary: This inscription marks the military unit as the century of Caecilius Proclus within the Fifth Cohort.

EDCS-07900380.summary: This inscription identifies a military unit, specifically the century belonging to Gellius Philippus.

EDCS-07900384.text_edition: \n Deabus Alaisiagis Baudihillie et Friagabi et numini Augusti numerus Hnaudifridi votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-07900384.translation: To the goddesses Alaisiagis and Baudihillie, and Friagabis, and to the divine spirit of the Emperor, the unit of Hnaudifridus gladly fulfilled his vow as deserved.
EDCS-07900384.summary: This votive inscription records the fulfillment of a vow to local goddesses and the emperor's numen by the unit of Hnaudifridus.

EDCS-07900385.text_edition: \n Cocidio et genio praesidi Valerius miles legionis VI victricis piae fidelis voto posuit\n
EDCS-07900385.translation: To Cocidius and the Genius of the shrine, Valerius, a soldier of the Sixth Legion Victrix, pious and loyal, erected this by vow.
EDCS-07900385.summary: This votive inscription records a soldier named Valerius dedicating a monument to the deity Cocidius and a local Genius.

EDCS-07900386.text_edition: \n Deo Silvano Cocidio Quintus Florius Maternus praefectus cohortis I Tungrorum votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-07900386.translation: To the god Silvanus Cocidius, Quintus Florius Maternus, prefect of the First Cohort of Tungrians, gladly fulfilled his vow as deserved.
EDCS-07900386.summary: This votive inscription records Quintus Florius Maternus, prefect of the First Cohort of Tungrians, fulfilling a vow to the god Silvanus Cocidius.

EDCS-07900388.text_edition: \n Herculi cohors I Tungrorum milliaria cui praeest Publius Aelius Modestus praefectus\n
EDCS-07900388.translation: To Hercules, the first cohort of Tungrians, a thousand strong, commanded by Publius Aelius Modestus, prefect.
EDCS-07900388.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the god Hercules by the first cohort of Tungrians under their prefect, Publius Aelius Modestus.

EDCS-07900391.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo et deo Cocidio Genioque huius loci milites legionis II Augustae agentes in praesidio votum solverunt libentes merito\n
EDCS-07900391.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, and to the god Cocidius, and to the genius of this place, the soldiers of the Second Augustan Legion, serving on guard duty, willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-07900391.summary: This inscription records soldiers of the Second Augustan Legion fulfilling a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and local deities while on duty.

EDCS-07900392.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo et Numinibus Augustorum cohors I Tungrorum cui praeest Quintus Iulius Maximus praefectus\n
EDCS-07900392.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, and to the Divine Augusti, the first cohort of Tungrians, commanded by Quintus Julius Maximus, prefect.
EDCS-07900392.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the Divine Augusti by the first cohort of Tungrians under their prefect, Quintus Julius Maximus.

EDCS-07900393.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo et Numinibus Augustorum cohors I Tungrorum cui praeest Quintus Iulius sus praefectus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-07900393.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, and to the Divine Augusti, the first cohort of Tungrians, commanded by Quintus Julius Sus, prefect, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07900393.summary: This inscription records the prefect Quintus Julius Sus fulfilling a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the Divine Augusti on behalf of the first cohort of Tungrians.

EDCS-07900394.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo et Numinibus Augustorum cohors I Tungrorum milliaria cui praeest Quintus Verius Superstis praefectus\n
EDCS-07900394.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, and to the Divine Augusti, the first cohort of Tungrians, a thousand strong, commanded by Quintus Verius Superstis, prefect.
EDCS-07900394.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the Divine Augusti by the first cohort of Tungrians under their prefect, Quintus Verius Superstis.

EDCS-07900397.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salute Desidieni Aemiliani praefecti et sua suorum posuit votumque solvit libens Tusco et Basso co(n)sulibus\n
EDCS-07900397.translation: For Jupiter Optimus Maximus, for the safety of the prefect Desidienus Aemilianus and himself and his family, he set up this offering and willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow, when Tuscus and Bassus were consuls.
EDCS-07900397.summary: This inscription records a vow made to Jupiter Optimus Maximus for the safety of the prefect Desidienus Aemilianus and his family, dated by the consuls Tuscus and Bassus.

EDCS-07900399.text_edition: \n Deo Marti Quintus Florius Maternus praefectus cohortis I Tungrorum votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-07900399.translation: To the god Mars, Quintus Florius Maternus, prefect of the first cohort of Tungrians, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07900399.summary: This inscription records Quintus Florius Maternus, prefect of the first cohort of Tungrians, fulfilling a vow to the god Mars.

EDCS-07900400.text_edition: \n Deo sancto Marti votum possivit Vianus\n
EDCS-07900400.translation: To the holy god Mars, Vianus set up this offering.
EDCS-07900400.summary: This inscription records Vianus dedicating an offering to the holy god Mars.

EDCS-07900401.text_edition: \n Deo Marti Thincso et duabus Alaisiagis Bede et Fimmilene et Numini Augusti Germani cives Tuihanti votum solverunt libentes merito\n
EDCS-07900401.translation: To the god Mars Thincsus, and to the two Alaisiagae Bede and Fimmilene, and to the Divine Augustus, the citizens of the Tuihanti willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-07900401.summary: This inscription records citizens of the Tuihanti tribe fulfilling a vow to the god Mars Thincsus, the goddesses Alaisiagae, and the Divine Augustus.

EDCS-07900402.text_edition: \n Deo Marti et duabus Alaisiagis et Numini Augusti Germani cives Tuihanti cunei Frisiorum Vercovicianorum Severiani Alexandriani votum solverunt libentes merito\n
EDCS-07900402.translation: To the god Mars, and to the two Alaisiagae, and to the Divine Augustus, the citizens of the Tuihanti, from the wedge of Frisii Vercovicani Severiani Alexandriani, willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-07900402.summary: This inscription records citizens from a specific unit of Frisii (Vercovicani Severiani Alexandriani) fulfilling a vow to the god Mars, the goddesses Alaisiagae, and the Divine Augustus.

EDCS-07900403.summary: This is a dedication to the gods Mars and Victoria.

EDCS-07900404.text_edition: \n Deo Marti et Victoriae et Numinibus Augustorum SVB CVRALIC - VI - IVIC --- II - -- V - IS VALLVTI ALPIBAIIRISI - I - I --- SIC -- VS --- VIVIOB --- NDICII --- custos armorum -- SD -- T\n
EDCS-07900404.translation: To the god Mars and Victoria and the Divine Powers of the Emperors, under the supervision of [---] Licinius Valerius Alpibus (or similar name), warden of arms, [---]...
EDCS-07900404.summary: This is a fragmentary dedication to Mars, Victoria, and the Imperial Divine Powers, possibly set up by a warden of arms.

EDCS-07900405.translation: To the god Mars the Unconquered, Germanus.
EDCS-07900405.summary: This is a dedication to the god Mars the Unconquered, perhaps by a person named Germanus.

EDCS-07900407.text_edition: \n Deo Soli Invicto Mytrae Saeculari Litorius Pacatianus beneficiarius consularis pro se et suis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-07900407.translation: To the Unconquered Sun God Mithras, the Secular, Litorius Pacatianus, a freedman of a consular legate, fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly for himself and his family.
EDCS-07900407.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Litorius Pacatianus to the god Sol Invictus Mithras, dedicated by a freedman of a consular legate.

EDCS-07900408.text_edition: \n Deo Deo Soli Invicto Mitrae Saeculari Publicius Proculinus pro se et Proculo filio suo votum solvit libens merito Dominis Nostris Gallo et Volusiano consulibus\n
EDCS-07900408.translation: To the Unconquered Sun God Mithras, the Secular, Publicius Proculinus fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly for himself and his son Proculus, during the consulship of our Lords Gallus and Volusianus.
EDCS-07900408.summary: This is a vow fulfilled by Publicius Proculinus to the god Sol Invictus Mithras for himself and his son during the consulship of Gallus and Volusianus (mid-3rd century CE).

EDCS-07900409.text_edition: \n Deo Soli Herion votum libens merito\n
EDCS-07900409.translation: To the god Sol, Herion willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07900409.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Herion to the god Sol.

EDCS-07900410.text_edition: \n Deo Hueteri Superstes et Regulus votum solverunt libentes merito\n
EDCS-07900410.translation: To the gods Hueterius Superstes and Regulus willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-07900410.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Hueterius Superstes and Regulus to deities.

EDCS-07900411.text_edition: \n Deo Huitri Aspuanis pro et suis votum solvit\n
EDCS-07900411.translation: To the god Huitrius Aspuanis fulfilled his vow for himself and his family.
EDCS-07900411.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Huitrius Aspuanis to a deity.

EDCS-07900414.text_edition: \n Veteribus posuuit sic Aurelius Victor voto\n
EDCS-07900414.translation: Aurelius Victor placed this in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-07900414.summary: This is a dedication by Aurelius Victor, placed in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-07900417.text_edition: \n legionis VI victricis piae fidelis votum solvit libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-07900417.translation: The Sixth Legion Victrix, dutiful and loyal, fulfilled its vow willingly, joyfully, and deservedly.
EDCS-07900417.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by the Sixth Legion Victrix.

EDCS-07900418.summary: This inscription identifies Lia, a freedwoman of Emperor Augustus.

EDCS-07900422.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription that begins with the title of an emperor.

EDCS-07900424.translation: Julius S, by decree of the villagers.
EDCS-07900424.summary: This inscription records that Julius S acted by decree of the local villagers.

EDCS-07900426.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Anicio Ingenuo medico ordinario cohortis I Tungrorum vixit annos XXV\n
EDCS-07900426.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Anicius Ingenuus, ordinary doctor of the 1st Cohort of Tungri, lived 25 years.
EDCS-07900426.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Anicius Ingenuus, a doctor in the 1st Cohort of Tungri, who died at the age of 25.

EDCS-07900427.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Hurmio Leubasni militi cohortis I Tungrorum beneficiario praefecti Calpurnius heres faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-07900427.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Calpurnius, the heir, had this made for Hurmius Leubasnus, a soldier of the 1st Cohort of Tungri, a beneficiary of the prefect.
EDCS-07900427.summary: This tombstone records that Calpurnius erected this monument for his relative Hurmius Leubasnus, a soldier and beneficiary of a prefect in the 1st Cohort of Tungri.

EDCS-07900428.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus P ANL MPR E enioni Venocari Grato Fersionis Romulo Alimahi Simili Daili Mansuetio Senicionis Pervince Quartionis heres procuravit Delfinus Rautionis ex Germania Superiore\n
EDCS-07900428.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. This was arranged by Delfinus of Rautionis, from Germania Superior, heir of P. ANLMPR E enioni Venocari Grato, Fersionis Romulo, Alimahi Simili, Daili Mansuetio, Senicionis Pervince, Quartionis.
EDCS-07900428.summary: This tombstone records that Delfinus, from Germania Superior, arranged the monument for multiple individuals, including P. ANLMPR E enioni Venocari Grato.

EDCS-07900429.text_edition: \n Au Meni filiae ni coniugi Marcus Aurelius C vicxit annos XXXVII\n
EDCS-07900429.translation: To Au... and his wife and companion, Marcus Aurelius C... lived 37 years.
EDCS-07900429.summary: This tombstone records the life span of Marcus Aurelius C, and mentions his wife and companion.

EDCS-07900430.summary: This is a tombstone inscription recording the lifespan of an individual identified as an heir.

EDCS-07900433.summary: This inscription states that Aurelius was the sculptor of the monument.

EDCS-07900440.translation: The century of Julius Candidus made this.
EDCS-07900440.summary: This inscription records that the century of Julius Candidus was responsible for creating this monument.

EDCS-07900441.text_edition: \n Deo Cocidio Vabrius votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-07900441.translation: To the god Cocidius, Vabrius gladly paid his vow, as was deserved.
EDCS-07900441.summary: This is a votive inscription by Vabrius to the god Cocidius, likely from Roman Britain, recording the fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-07900445.text_edition: \n Imperatoris Gaesaris Traiani Hadriani Augusti legio II Augusta Aulo Platorio Nepote legato pro praetore\n
EDCS-07900445.translation: To Emperor Caesar Trajan Hadrian Augustus, from Legio II Augusta, under Aulus Platorius Nepos as legate pro praetore.
EDCS-07900445.summary: This is an inscription from Legio II Augusta, dedicated to Emperor Hadrian, and indicating Aulus Platorius Nepos as the legate pro praetore, likely from the period of Hadrian's reign (117-138 AD).

EDCS-07900446.text_edition: \n Imperatoris Caesaris Traiani Hadriani Augusti legio II Augusta Aulo Platorio Nepote legato pro praetore\n
EDCS-07900446.translation: To Emperor Caesar Trajan Hadrian Augustus, from Legio II Augusta, under Aulus Platorius Nepos as legate pro praetore.
EDCS-07900446.summary: This is an inscription from Legio II Augusta, dedicated to Emperor Hadrian, and indicating Aulus Platorius Nepos as the legate pro praetore, likely from the period of Hadrian's reign (117-138 AD).

EDCS-07900454.translation: Of the First Cohort, the century of Iulius Candidus.
EDCS-07900454.summary: This inscription records a centuria (a subdivision of a Roman legion or cohort) belonging to Iulius Candidus within the First Cohort.

EDCS-07900455.translation: Of the First Cohort, the century of Olc Libo.
EDCS-07900455.summary: This inscription records a centuria belonging to Olc Libo within the First Cohort.

EDCS-07900456.translation: Of the Fourth Cohort, the century of Claudius Cleonicus.
EDCS-07900456.summary: This inscription records a centuria belonging to Claudius Cleonicus within the Fourth Cohort.

EDCS-07900457.translation: Of the Seventh Cohort, the century of Pompeius.
EDCS-07900457.summary: This inscription records a centuria belonging to Pompeius within the Seventh Cohort.

EDCS-07900459.translation: Of the Eighth Cohort, the century of Postumius.
EDCS-07900459.summary: This inscription records a centuria belonging to Postumius within the Eighth Cohort.

EDCS-07900462.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription identifying a centuria belonging to Florus.

EDCS-07900463.summary: This inscription records a centuria belonging to Kalpurnianus.

EDCS-07900464.summary: This inscription identifies a Roman military century belonging to Maritimus.

EDCS-07900465.summary: This inscription identifies a Roman military century belonging to Caecilius Monimus.

EDCS-07900466.summary: This inscription identifies a Roman military century belonging to Iulius Val[...].

EDCS-07900467.summary: This inscription identifies a Roman military century belonging to Rufus Sabinus.

EDCS-07900469.summary: This inscription identifies a Roman military century belonging to Verus.

EDCS-07900470.summary: This inscription identifies a Roman military century belonging to Verus.

EDCS-07900471.summary: This inscription identifies a Roman military century belonging to Venulus.

EDCS-07900472.translation: The ninth cohort, the century of Flavius Noricus.
EDCS-07900472.summary: This inscription identifies the century of Flavius Noricus within the ninth cohort of a Roman military unit.

EDCS-07900473.text_edition: \n Deo Apollini Melonius Senilis duplicarius Germania Superiore susceptum solvit libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-07900473.translation: To the god Apollo, Melonius Senilis, a duplicarius from Germania Superior, paid his vow willingly, gladly, and deservedly.
EDCS-07900473.summary: This is a votive inscription recording a soldier named Melonius Senilis from Germania Superior dedicating a vow to Apollo.

EDCS-07900476.summary: This inscription identifies a Roman military century belonging to Gellius Philippus.

EDCS-07900478.summary: This inscription identifies the century of Romuleius Iustus, likely a military unit designation.

EDCS-07900479.summary: This inscription identifies the century of Victorinus, likely a military unit designation.

EDCS-07900482.translation: Century of Candidus of the 1st Cohort
EDCS-07900482.summary: This inscription identifies the century of Candidus of the 1st Cohort, indicating a military unit designation.

EDCS-07900483.summary: This inscription identifies the Socelliana Century of the 3rd Cohort, indicating a military unit designation.

EDCS-07900484.summary: This inscription identifies the Ostorian Century of the 5th Cohort, indicating a military unit designation.

EDCS-07900485.summary: This inscription identifies the century of Julius Florentinus, likely a military unit designation.

EDCS-07900486.summary: This inscription identifies the Liberalis Century of the 6th Cohort, indicating a military unit designation.

EDCS-07900487.text_edition: \n Cohors cohortis VI centuria Caledoni Secundi\n
EDCS-07900487.translation: Century of Caledon Secundus of the 6th Cohort
EDCS-07900487.summary: This inscription identifies the century of Caledon Secundus of the 6th Cohort, indicating a military unit designation.

EDCS-07900489.summary: This inscription identifies the century of Lousius Suavis, likely a military unit designation.

EDCS-07900490.summary: This inscription identifies the century of Valerius Maximus, likely a military unit designation.

EDCS-07900491.text_edition: \n Deo Cocidio Decimus Caerellius Victor praefectus cohortis II Nerviorum votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-07900491.translation: To the god Cocidius, Decimus Caerellius Victor, prefect of the Second Cohort of Nervians, willingly paid his vow deservedly.
EDCS-07900491.summary: A vow paid to the god Cocidius by Decimus Caerellius Victor, prefect of the Second Cohort of Nervians, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07900492.text_edition: \n Fortunae populi Romani Gaius Iulius Raeticus |(centurio) legionis VI Victricis\n
EDCS-07900492.translation: To Fortuna of the Roman People, Gaius Iulius Raeticus, centurion of the Sixth Legion Victrix.
EDCS-07900492.summary: An inscription honoring Fortuna of the Roman People, dedicated by Gaius Iulius Raeticus, a centurion of the Sixth Legion Victrix, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07900493.text_edition: \n Genio praetori sacrum Pituanius Secundus praefectus cohortis IIII Gallorum\n
EDCS-07900493.translation: Sacred to the genius of the praetor, Pituanius Secundus, prefect of the Fourth Cohort of Gauls.
EDCS-07900493.summary: An inscription dedicating something sacred to the genius of the praetor by Pituanius Secundus, prefect of the Fourth Cohort of Gauls, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07900494.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo ceterisque diis immortalibus et Genio praetori Quintus Petronius Quinti filius Fabia Urbicus praefectus cohortis IIII Gallorum [] ex Italia domo Brixia votum solvit pro se ac suis\n
EDCS-07900494.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, to all the immortal gods, and to the genius of the praetor, Quintus Petronius Urbicus, son of Quintus, of the Fabia tribe, from Brixia in Italy, prefect of the Fourth Cohort of Gauls, paid his vow for himself and his family.
EDCS-07900494.summary: A vow paid to Jupiter and other gods by Quintus Petronius Urbicus, prefect of the Fourth Cohort of Gauls from Brixia, Italy, likely during the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07900496.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo cohors IIII Gallorum cui praeest Sulpicius Pudens praefectus aram posuit votum libens merito solvit\n
EDCS-07900496.translation: The Fourth Cohort of Gauls, commanded by Sulpicius Pudens, its prefect, set up this altar and willingly paid its vow deservedly.
EDCS-07900496.summary: An altar set up by the Fourth Cohort of Gauls, dedicated by its prefect Sulpicius Pudens, likely during the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07900507.translation: The Senate set up [this] for the Veterans.
EDCS-07900507.summary: An inscription recording a dedication to veterans by the Senate, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07900519.summary: An inscription simply naming the century of Iulius Primus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07900521.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Cornelius Victor singularis consularis militavit nnos XXVI civis nnonius filius Saturnini primi pilaris vixit annos LV dies XI coniux procuravit\n
EDCS-07900521.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, Cornelius Victor, a singularis of a consular governor, served for 26 years, a citizen, son of Saturninus, a primipilaris, lived for 55 years and 11 days; his wife provided this.
EDCS-07900521.summary: A tombstone inscription for Cornelius Victor, who served 26 years as a singularis, and lived 55 years and 11 days, provided by his wife, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07900522.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Ingenui vixit annis XXIIII memses IIII et dies VII\n
EDCS-07900522.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, Ingenuus lived 24 years, 4 months, and 7 days.
EDCS-07900522.summary: A tombstone inscription for Ingenuus, who died at the age of 24 years, 4 months, and 7 days, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07900524.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Flavia Emerita Flavius In N\n
EDCS-07900524.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, Flavia Emerita [and] Flavius In N...
EDCS-07900524.summary: A partial tombstone inscription for Flavia Emerita and Flavius In N..., likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07900533.summary: This inscription is noted as having no recorded text.

EDCS-07900535.text_edition: \n D(e)ae Fortunae vexsillatio Gaesatorum Raetorum quorum curam agit Tabellius Victor |(centurio)\n
EDCS-07900535.translation: To the Goddess Fortuna, from the detachment of Raetian slingers, under the charge of Tabellius Victor, centurion.
EDCS-07900535.summary: A dedication to the goddess Fortuna by a detachment of Raetian slingers, supervised by a centurion, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-07900536.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno Lucius Maximius Gaetulicus |(centurio) legionis XX Valeriae victricis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-07900536.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus, Lucius Maximius Gaetulicus, centurion of the Twentieth Legion Valeria Victrix, willingly and deservedly paid his vow.
EDCS-07900536.summary: A soldier, Lucius Maximius Gaetulicus, a centurion of the Twentieth Legion, dedicates a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus, likely in the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-07900537.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno Sabini filius ina Regulus Publi\n
EDCS-07900537.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus, Publius Regulus, son of Sabinus, [dedicated this].
EDCS-07900537.summary: A dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus by Publius Regulus, son of Sabinus, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-07900540.translation: To the Old Gods, Roman [power] will have [strength].
EDCS-07900540.summary: A brief and archaic dedication to the 'Old Gods', potentially related to Roman military or state power.

EDCS-07900542.text_edition: \n Victoriae Augusti cohors VI Nerviorum cui praeest Gaius Iulius Barbarus praefectus votum solvit laeta libens merito\n
EDCS-07900542.translation: To Victory of the Emperor, the Sixth Cohort of Nervians, commanded by Gaius Iulius Barbarus, prefect, willingly and deservedly paid its vow joyfully.
EDCS-07900542.summary: The Sixth Cohort of Nervians, led by Prefect Gaius Iulius Barbarus, dedicates a vow to the Victory of the Emperor, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-07900544.text_edition: \n nus |(centurio) legionis agens curam aram dicavit votum solvens libens merito\n
EDCS-07900544.translation: [He], centurion of the ... legion, while in charge, dedicated the altar, paying his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-07900544.summary: An unidentified centurion of a Roman legion dedicated an altar and paid a vow, likely during the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-07900547.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Traiano Hadriano Augusto patri patriae\n
EDCS-07900547.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Trajan Hadrian Augustus, father of the country.
EDCS-07900547.summary: An inscription honoring Emperor Hadrian, likely dating to his reign (117-138 AD).

EDCS-07900549.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus horreum vetustate conlabsum sic militibus cohortis II Asturum Severianae Alexandrianae a solo restitverunt provinciam regente Maximo legato Augusti pro praetore curante Valerio Martiale legionis Fusco II et Dextro consulibus\n
EDCS-07900549.translation: Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus. The soldiers of the Second Severan Cohort of Asturians rebuilt the granary, which had collapsed from age, from its foundations, while Maximus was governing the province as Legate of the Emperor, under the supervision of Valerius Martialis of the Second Legion Fuscus and with Fuscus and Dexter as consuls.
EDCS-07900549.summary: This inscription records the rebuilding of a collapsed granary by soldiers of the Second Cohort of Asturians during the reign of Emperor Severus Alexander, with specific officials named, dating to the early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07900553.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aelio Mercuriali corniculario Vacia soror fecit\n
EDCS-07900553.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Vacia, his sister, made [this] for Aelius Mercurialis, a cornicularius.
EDCS-07900553.summary: A funerary dedication to Aelius Mercurialis, a cornicularius (a type of military clerk or messenger), made by his sister Vacia, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-07900554.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Lucius Novellius Lanuccus civis Romanus annorum LXX Novellia Iustina filia faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-07900554.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Lucius Novellius Lanuccus, a Roman citizen aged 70, had this made. His daughter Novellia Iustina took care of it.
EDCS-07900554.summary: A funerary monument for Lucius Novellius Lanuccus, erected by his daughter, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-07900556.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aureliae Caulae Aurelia Silla sorori carissime vixit annos XV menses IIII\n
EDCS-07900556.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. To Aurelia Caula, his dearest sister. She lived 15 years and 4 months.
EDCS-07900556.summary: A funerary epitaph for Aurelia Caula, erected by her sister, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-07900557.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Nigrinae vixit annos XXXX Aurelius Casitto |(centurio) legionis VI victricis piae fidelis curavit\n
EDCS-07900557.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, sacred. Nigrina lived 40 years. Aurelius Casitto, a centurion of the VI Victrix legion, pious and loyal, took care of this.
EDCS-07900557.summary: A funerary monument for Nigrina, erected by Aurelius Casitto, a centurion of VI Victrix, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-07900558.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Pervica, my daughter.
EDCS-07900558.summary: A funerary dedication for Pervica, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-07900565.translation: Of the 5th Cohort, the century of Sextus Proculus.
EDCS-07900565.summary: An inscription likely marking a section of a military camp or a tomb belonging to the 5th Cohort, specifically the century of Sextus Proculus.

EDCS-07900571.summary: An inscription identifying a military unit, likely a century belonging to Marius Dexter.

EDCS-07900572.summary: An inscription identifying a military unit, likely a century belonging to Valerius Verus.

EDCS-07900574.translation: Of the 1st Cohort, the century of Flavius Crescentis.
EDCS-07900574.summary: An inscription likely marking a section of a military camp or a tomb belonging to the 1st Cohort, specifically the century of Flavius Crescentis.

EDCS-07900576.summary: An inscription identifying a military unit, likely a century belonging to Munatius Maximus.

EDCS-07900577.translation: Of the 5th Cohort, the century of Valerius Maximus.
EDCS-07900577.summary: An inscription likely marking a section of a military camp or a tomb belonging to the 5th Cohort, specifically the century of Valerius Maximus.

EDCS-07900579.translation: Of the third cohort, the Socelliana century.
EDCS-07900579.summary: This inscription records a military unit, specifically a century belonging to the third cohort, likely dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-07900580.translation: Of the third cohort, the century of Ferronius Vegetus.
EDCS-07900580.summary: This inscription records a military unit, specifically a century of the third cohort named after Ferronius Vegetus, likely dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-07900581.summary: This inscription records a military unit, specifically a century named after Claudius Augustanus, likely dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-07900582.summary: This inscription records a military unit, specifically a century named after Marius Dexter, likely dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-07900583.translation: Of the sixth cohort, the Lepidiana century.
EDCS-07900583.summary: This inscription records a military unit, specifically a century belonging to the sixth cohort, likely dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-07900584.summary: This inscription records a military unit, specifically a century belonging to the third cohort, with an illegible addition, likely dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-07900585.translation: Of the fifth cohort, the century of Lucius Valens.
EDCS-07900585.summary: This inscription records a military unit, specifically a century of the fifth cohort named after Lucius Valens, likely dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-07900588.translation: To the goddess Epona, P. So [fulfills vow?].
EDCS-07900588.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the goddess Epona by P. So, possibly fulfilling a vow, likely dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-07900589.text_edition: \n Fortunae Augustae pro salute Luci Aeli Caesaris ex visu Titus Flavius Secundus praefectus cohortis I Hamiorum Sagittariorum votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-07900589.translation: To Fortune the August, Titus Flavius Secundus, prefect of the first cohort of the Hamians and Archers, happily and deservedly fulfilled a vow in accordance with a vision for the safety of Caesar Lucius Aelius.
EDCS-07900589.summary: This is a votive inscription by Titus Flavius Secundus, prefect of an auxiliary cohort, dedicated to Fortuna Augusta for the health of Lucius Aelius Caesar, likely dating to the mid-2nd century AD.

EDCS-07900590.text_edition: \n Fortunae Audacilius Romanus |(centurio) legionum VI XX Augustae\n
EDCS-07900590.translation: To Fortune the Bold, Romanus, a centurion of the sixth and twentieth legions Augustan, [fulfilled a vow?].
EDCS-07900590.summary: This is a votive inscription to Fortuna Audacilius by Romanus, a centurion serving in two legions, likely dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-07900591.translation: To the goddess Hamia, Sabinus made [this offering].
EDCS-07900591.summary: A dedication to the goddess Hamia by Sabinus, likely on a shrine or altar, probably from the imperial period.

EDCS-07900594.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Heliopolitano Iulius Pollio \n
EDCS-07900594.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Heliopolitanus, Iulius Pollio [dedicated this].
EDCS-07900594.summary: A dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Heliopolitanus by Iulius Pollio, likely a military dedication or personal vow, from the imperial period.

EDCS-07900600.text_edition: \n Deabus Nymphis Vettia Mansueta et Claudia Turianilla filia votum solverunt libentes merito\n
EDCS-07900600.translation: To the Nymphs, Vettia Mansueta and her daughter Claudia Turianilla gladly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-07900600.summary: A votive offering to the Nymphs by Vettia Mansueta and Claudia Turianilla, indicating a fulfillment of a vow, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-07900601.summary: A dedication to the god Silvanus by Vellaeus, probably on a small shrine or altar, from the imperial period.

EDCS-07900602.text_edition: \n Imminet Leoni Virgo caelesti situ spicifera iusti inventrix urbium conditrix ex quis muneribus nosse contigit deos ergo eadem mater divum Pax Virtus Ceres Dea Syria lance vitam et iura pensitans in caelo visum Syria sidus edidit Libyae colendum inde cuncti didicimus ita intellexit numine inductus tuo Marcus Caecilius Donatianus militans tribunus in praefecto dono principis\n
EDCS-07900602.translation: The heavenly Virgin, bearing grain, inventor of justice, founder of cities, overshadows the Lion; from which gifts it has been granted to know the gods. Therefore, the same mother of gods, Peace, Virtue, Ceres, the Syrian Goddess, weighing life and rights on a scale, revealed the Syrian star to be seen in heaven, to be worshipped in Libya; from there we all learned. Thus, Marcus Caecilius Donatianus, serving as tribune under the prefect, understood by your divine will, [dedicates this] as a gift of the emperor.
EDCS-07900602.summary: A complex theological inscription that identifies the Virgin (likely a representation of Isis or a similar goddess) with multiple divine attributes and connects her to the stars and Roman authority, dedicated by Marcus Caecilius Donatianus, a military tribune, likely from the late imperial period.

EDCS-07900606.text_edition: \n Deo sancto Veteri Iulius Pastor imaginifer cohortis II Delmatarum votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-07900606.translation: To the holy god Veter, Iulius Pastor, standard-bearer of the II Cohort of Dalmatians, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07900606.summary: A military dedication by Iulius Pastor, a standard-bearer in the II Cohort of Dalmatians, to the god Veter, indicating the fulfillment of a vow, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-07900610.text_edition: \n Deo Vitiri Menius Dada votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-07900610.translation: To the god Vitiris, Menius Dada gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07900610.summary: A votive offering to the god Vitiris by Menius Dada, indicating the fulfillment of a vow, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-07900611.text_edition: \n Deo Vitiri Milus et Aurides votum solverunt libentes merito\n
EDCS-07900611.translation: To the god Vitiris, Milus and Aurides gladly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-07900611.summary: A votive offering to the god Vitiris by Milus and Aurides, indicating the fulfillment of a vow, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-07900616.text_edition: \n Dibus Vitiribus Deccius votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-07900616.translation: To the goddesses Vitirides, Deccius gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07900616.summary: A votive offering to the goddesses Vitirides by Deccius, indicating the fulfillment of a vow, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-07900617.text_edition: \n Deo armilum Binius votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-07900617.translation: To the god Armilus, Binius gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07900617.summary: A votive offering to the god Armilus by Binius, indicating the fulfillment of a vow, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-07900620.text_edition: \n sub Calpurnio Agricola consulari Licinius Clemens praefectus\n
EDCS-07900620.translation: Under the consulship of Calpurnius Agricola, Licinius Clemens was prefect.
EDCS-07900620.summary: This inscription records an administrative detail, mentioning the consul Calpurnius Agricola and the prefect Licinius Clemens, likely from the mid-2nd century CE.

EDCS-07900622.translation: From the century of Claudius Augustanus of the Third Cohort.
EDCS-07900622.summary: This inscription identifies a military unit, the Third Cohort, and likely refers to a centurion or a century named after Claudius Augustanus.

EDCS-07900625.translation: The century of Felix made 20 feet.
EDCS-07900625.summary: This inscription records a construction or measurement, stating that the century of Felix completed 20 feet of work.

EDCS-07900628.text_edition: \n centuria Martialis Antonius Viator ex Germania Superiore fecit\n
EDCS-07900628.translation: Antonius Viator from Germania Superior made this century.
EDCS-07900628.summary: This inscription records the construction of a military work, attributing it to Antonius Viator, who came from Germania Superior, likely during the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-07900629.text_edition: \n centuria Primi vallavit pedes CXII sub Flavio Secundo praefecto\n
EDCS-07900629.translation: The century of Primus fortified 112 feet under Prefect Flavius Secundus.
EDCS-07900629.summary: This inscription records a military construction project, detailing that the century of Primus built 112 feet of fortifications under the command of Prefect Flavius Secundus.

EDCS-07900631.text_edition: \n centuria Silvani vallavit pedes CXII sub Flavio Secundo praefecto\n
EDCS-07900631.translation: The century of Silvanus fortified 112 feet under Prefect Flavius Secundus.
EDCS-07900631.summary: This inscription records a military construction project, stating that the century of Silvanus built 112 feet of fortifications under Prefect Flavius Secundus.

EDCS-07900636.text_edition: \n Gaius Valerius Gai Voltinia Iullus Vianna miles legionis XX Valeriae victricis\n
EDCS-07900636.translation: Gaius Valerius Iullus, son of Voltinia, from Vianna, a soldier of the 20th Legion Valeria Victrix.
EDCS-07900636.summary: This inscription records the identity of a soldier, Gaius Valerius Iullus, identifying his legion (Legio XX Valeria Victrix) and origin.

EDCS-07900638.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aureliae Titi filiae Aiae domo Salonas Aurelius Marcus centuria Obsequentis coniugi sanctissimae quae vixit annis XXXIII sine ulla macula\n
EDCS-07900638.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, for Aurelia Aya, daughter of Titus, from Salona. Aurelius Marcus, century of Obsequens, [erected this] for his most holy wife, who lived 33 years without any blemish.
EDCS-07900638.summary: This is a funerary inscription honoring Aurelia Aya, who died at 33, erected by her husband Aurelius Marcus, a centurion, from Salona.

EDCS-07900639.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelia VBE OVC MA vixit nnos Aurelia Pusinna sorori pientissime et desideratissime \n
EDCS-07900639.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, for Aurelia VBE OVC MA, who lived [X] years. Aurelia Pusinna [erected this] for her most devoted and beloved sister.
EDCS-07900639.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Aurelia VBE OVC MA, erected by her sister Aurelia Pusinna.

EDCS-07900640.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Lifana Bci filia vixit nnos Lucius Senovalus avunculus fecit\n
EDCS-07900640.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, for Lifana, daughter of Bcius, who lived [X] years. Lucius Senovalus, her uncle, erected this.
EDCS-07900640.summary: This is a funerary inscription honoring Lifana, daughter of Bcius, erected by her uncle Lucius Senovalus.

EDCS-07900644.summary: This is likely the dedication or ownership inscription of a monument, possibly a tomb, belonging to Titus Julius and his siblings.

EDCS-07900655.translation: The century of the Second Cohort under the command of Pon(tius) Magnus.
EDCS-07900655.summary: This inscription records a military unit, specifically a century of the Second Cohort, identified by its commander, Pontius Magnus.

EDCS-07900656.summary: This is a military inscription identifying a century by the name of its commander, Munatius Maxumus.

EDCS-07900663.summary: This inscription records a military unit, identifying a century by its commander, Valerius Verus.

EDCS-07900664.translation: The century of the Sixth Cohort under the command of Caledonius Secundus.
EDCS-07900664.summary: This inscription identifies a military unit, a century of the Sixth Cohort, led by Caledonius Secundus.

EDCS-07900665.translation: The century of the Third Cohort under the command of Claudius Augustanus.
EDCS-07900665.summary: This military inscription names a century belonging to the Third Cohort, commanded by Claudius Augustanus.

EDCS-07900668.summary: This inscription records a military unit, identifying a century by its commander, Vesius Rufus.

EDCS-07900669.translation: The Sixth Cohort, century of Lucius Suavis.
EDCS-07900669.summary: This military inscription identifies a century within the Sixth Cohort, named after its commander, Lucius Suavis.

EDCS-07900670.summary: This inscription records a military unit, identifying a century by its commander, Cocceius Regulus.

EDCS-07900671.translation: The Sixth Cohort, century of Lucius Suavis.
EDCS-07900671.summary: This military inscription identifies a century within the Sixth Cohort, named after its commander, Lucius Suavis.

EDCS-07900672.summary: This inscription identifies a military unit, specifically the century belonging to Cocceius Regulus, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-07900676.translation: The century of Ferronius Vegetus of the 3rd cohort.
EDCS-07900676.summary: This inscription identifies a military unit, specifically the century of Ferronius Vegetus within the 3rd cohort, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-07900677.summary: This inscription identifies a military unit, specifically the Probian century, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-07900678.translation: The century of Cassius Priscus of the 6th cohort.
EDCS-07900678.summary: This inscription identifies a military unit, specifically the century of Cassius Priscus within the 6th cohort, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-07900679.text_edition: \n Deo Sancto Silvano Flavius Marcellinus decurio votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-07900679.translation: Flavius Marcellinus, a decurion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to the Holy God Silvanus.
EDCS-07900679.summary: This is a votive inscription by Flavius Marcellinus, a decurion, dedicating a vow to the god Silvanus, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-07900680.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Serenus vixit annis II mensibus II diebus III\n
EDCS-07900680.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Serenus lived for 2 years, 2 months, and 3 days.
EDCS-07900680.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the short lifespan of Serenus, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-07900683.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo cohors I Aelia Dacorum quibus praeest Ammonius Victorinus tribunus\n
EDCS-07900683.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest. The 1st Aelian Cohort of Dacians, commanded by Ammonius Victorinus, Tribune.
EDCS-07900683.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by the 1st Aelian Cohort of Dacians, under the command of Tribune Ammonius Victorinus, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-07900684.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo cohors I Aelia Dacorum cui praeest Aurelius Faustus tribunus Perpetuo consule\n
EDCS-07900684.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest. The 1st Aelian Cohort of Dacians, commanded by Tribune Aurelius Faustus, under the consulship of Perpetuus.
EDCS-07900684.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by the 1st Aelian Cohort of Dacians, under Tribune Aurelius Faustus, and is dated to the consulship of Perpetuus, likely in the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-07900685.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo cohors I Aelia Dacorum cuius curam agit Aurelius Saturninus\n
EDCS-07900685.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest. The 1st Aelian Cohort of Dacians, whose care is managed by Aurelius Saturninus.
EDCS-07900685.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by the 1st Aelian Cohort of Dacians, noting Aurelius Saturninus as being in charge of its affairs, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-07900686.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo cohors I Aelia Dacorum Augusta cui praeest Aurelius \n
EDCS-07900686.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest. The 1st Aelian Cohort of Dacians Augusta, commanded by Aurelius...
EDCS-07900686.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by the 1st Aelian Cohort of Dacians Augusta, noting its commander as Aurelius, with the name incomplete, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-07900689.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo cohors I Aelia Dacorum cuius curam agit Iulius Marcellinus legionis II Augustae\n
EDCS-07900689.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, the First Cohort of Aelii Dacians, under the care of Julius Marcellinus of the Second Augustan Legion.
EDCS-07900689.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter by the First Cohort of Aelii Dacians, supervised by an officer of the Second Augustan Legion, likely from the Roman military presence in Britannia.

EDCS-07900690.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo cohors I Aelia Dacorum cui praeest Iulius Saturninus tribunus\n
EDCS-07900690.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, the First Cohort of Aelii Dacians, commanded by the tribune Julius Saturninus.
EDCS-07900690.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter by the First Cohort of Aelii Dacians, led by the tribune Julius Saturninus, indicating military activity.

EDCS-07900691.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo et Numini Augusti cohors I Aelia Dacorum cui praeest Marcius Gallicus tribunus\n
EDCS-07900691.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest and to the divine will of the Emperor, the First Cohort of Aelii Dacians, commanded by the tribune Marcius Gallicus.
EDCS-07900691.summary: This dedication to Jupiter and the Imperial divinity was made by the First Cohort of Aelii Dacians under the command of tribune Marcius Gallicus, likely during the Imperial period.

EDCS-07900692.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo cohors I Aelia Dacorum Postumiana cui praeest Marcius Gallicus tribunus\n
EDCS-07900692.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, the First Cohort of Aelii Dacians Postumiana, commanded by the tribune Marcius Gallicus.
EDCS-07900692.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter by the First Cohort of Aelii Dacians Postumiana, led by tribune Marcius Gallicus, indicating a specific unit designation.

EDCS-07900694.text_edition: \n Deo Cocidio Iovi Optimo Maximo cohors I Aelia Dacorum Tetricianorum cui praeest Pomponius Desideratus tribunus\n
EDCS-07900694.translation: To the god Cocidius, to Jupiter Best and Greatest, the First Cohort of Aelii Dacians Tetricianorum, commanded by the tribune Pomponius Desideratus.
EDCS-07900694.summary: This dedication combines local deity worship (Cocidius) with Jupiter Optimus Maximus by the First Cohort of Aelii Dacians Tetricianorum, under tribune Pomponius Desideratus, likely in Britannia.

EDCS-07900695.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo cohors I Aelia Dacorum Postumiana cui praeest Probius Augendus tribunus\n
EDCS-07900695.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, the First Cohort of Aelii Dacians Postumiana, commanded by the tribune Probius Augendus.
EDCS-07900695.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter by the First Cohort of Aelii Dacians Postumiana, commanded by tribune Probius Augendus, suggesting military administration.

EDCS-07900696.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo cohors I Aelia Dacorum cui praeest Statius Longinus tribunus\n
EDCS-07900696.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, the First Cohort of Aelii Dacians, commanded by the tribune Statius Longinus.
EDCS-07900696.summary: This dedication to Jupiter was made by the First Cohort of Aelii Dacians under the command of tribune Statius Longinus, reflecting military religious practices.

EDCS-07900698.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo cohors I Aelia Dacorum cui praeest \n
EDCS-07900698.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, the First Cohort of Aelii Dacians, commanded by [---].
EDCS-07900698.summary: This incomplete inscription records a dedication to Jupiter by the First Cohort of Aelii Dacians, with the commanding officer's name missing.

EDCS-07900699.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo cohors I Aelia Dacorum cui praeest \n
EDCS-07900699.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, the First Cohort of Aelii Dacians, commanded by [---].
EDCS-07900699.summary: This incomplete inscription is a dedication to Jupiter by the First Cohort of Aelii Dacians, where the name of the commanding officer is lost.

EDCS-07900700.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo cohors I Aelia Dacorum cui praeest \n
EDCS-07900700.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, the First Cohort of Aelii Dacians, commanded by [---].
EDCS-07900700.summary: This fragmentary inscription records a dedication to Jupiter by the First Cohort of Aelii Dacians, with the commander's name illegible.

EDCS-07900701.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo cohors I Aelia Dacorum Antoniniana \n
EDCS-07900701.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, the First Cohort of Aelia Dacorum, bearing the Antonine title.
EDCS-07900701.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter by the First Cohort of Aelia Dacorum, likely during the Antonine period.

EDCS-07900702.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo cohors I Aelia Dacorum Gordiana cui praeest \n
EDCS-07900702.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, the First Cohort of Aelia Dacorum, bearing the Gordian title, under the command of...
EDCS-07900702.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter by the First Cohort of Aelia Dacorum, identified with the Gordian title, and is incomplete.

EDCS-07900703.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo cohors I Aelia Dacorum M NV S rin beneficiar---) votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-07900703.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, the First Cohort of Aelia Dacorum [under the command of...] paid their vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-07900703.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by the First Cohort of Aelia Dacorum, with partial restorations of the commanding officer's name.

EDCS-07900708.text_edition: \n Deo Marti et Victoriae Aurelius Maximus sacrum sumptu suo votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-07900708.translation: To the god Mars and Victory, Aurelius Maximus dedicated this, fulfilling his vow willingly and deservedly at his own expense.
EDCS-07900708.summary: This inscription records a personal dedication to Mars and Victory by Aurelius Maximus, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-07900715.text_edition: \n to cohors I quibus praeest Ammonius Victorinus tribunus\n
EDCS-07900715.translation: ...the First Cohort, commanded by Ammonius Victorinus, tribune.
EDCS-07900715.summary: This inscription records the commander of the First Cohort, Ammonius Victorinus, a tribune.

EDCS-07900716.text_edition: \n V C Lucius Vereius Fortunatus |(centurio) legionis VI\n
EDCS-07900716.translation: Lucius Vereius Fortunatus, former consular legate and centurion of the Sixth Legion.
EDCS-07900716.summary: This inscription identifies Lucius Vereius Fortunatus, a former consular legate and centurion of the Sixth Legion.

EDCS-07900718.text_edition: \n Impperatoribus Caessaribus Lucio Septimio Severo Pio Pertinaci et Marco Aurelio Antonino Auggustis [et Publio Septimio] [Getae nobilissimo Caesari] horreum fecerunt cohortes I Aelia Dacorum et I Thracum civium Romanorum sub Alfeno Senecione consulari per Aurelium Iulianum tribunum\n
EDCS-07900718.translation: To the Emperors Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus and Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus [and to Publius Septimius Geta Nobilissimus Caesar], the First Cohort of Aelia Dacorum and the First Cohort of Thracian Roman Citizens built a granary, under the consular command of Alfennus Senecio, through Aurelius Iulianus, tribune.
EDCS-07900718.summary: This inscription records the construction of a granary by two cohorts under the emperors Septimius Severus and Caracalla, during the consulship of Alfennus Senecio, likely in the early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-07900720.text_edition: \n Pro salute domini nostri maximi ac fortissimi imperatoris Caesaris Marci Aureli Antonini Pii Felicis Augusti OC VVST O aedificavit\n
EDCS-07900720.translation: For the safety of our lord, the greatest and bravest Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, [....] built.
EDCS-07900720.summary: This inscription records a building project undertaken for the safety of Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus (likely Caracalla), with some text missing.

EDCS-07900721.text_edition: \n DDominis nnostris Diocletiano et Maximiano invictis Auggustis et Constantio et Maximiano nnobilissimis CCaesaribus sub viro perfectissimo Aurelio Arpagio praeside praetorium quod erat humo copertum et in labem conlapsum et principia et balneum restituit curante Flavio Martino centurione praeposito cohors \n
EDCS-07900721.translation: For our lords Diocletian and Maximian, unconquered Augusti, and Constantius and Maximian, most noble Caesars, under the most perfect man Aurelius Arpagius, the governor, the praetorium which was covered with earth and had collapsed into ruin, and the principia and the bathhouse were restored, under the care of Flavius Martinus, centurion in command, of the cohort.
EDCS-07900721.summary: This inscription records the restoration of a praetorium, principia, and bathhouse by Aurelius Arpagius, governor, and Flavius Martinus, centurion, for the emperors Diocletian, Maximian, Constantius, and Maximian, likely in the late 3rd or early 4th century CE.

EDCS-07900723.text_edition: \n Sub Modio Iulio legato Augusti pro praetore cohors I Aelia Dacorum cui praeest Marcus Claudius Menander tribunus\n
EDCS-07900723.translation: Under Modius Iulius, imperial legate pro praetore, the First Cohort of Aelia Dacorum, commanded by Marcus Claudius Menander, tribune.
EDCS-07900723.summary: This inscription records the command of the First Cohort of Aelia Dacorum by Marcus Claudius Menander, tribune, under the governorship of Modius Iulius.

EDCS-07900728.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aureli Concordi vixit nnum unum dies V filius Aureli Iuliani tribuni\n
EDCS-07900728.translation: To the Divine Shades. Aurelius Concordius lived for one year and five days, son of Aurelius Iulianus, a tribune.
EDCS-07900728.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the short life of Aurelius Concordius, son of a tribune, likely from the 2nd-3rd century CE.

EDCS-07900730.text_edition: \n spa Septimo vixit nnos XXXX militavit XVIII cohorte I Aelia Dacorum heres faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-07900730.translation: Septimus lived for 40 years, served for 18 years in the Cohors I Aelia Dacorum. The heir arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-07900730.summary: A funerary epitaph for Septimus, a soldier of Cohors I Aelia Dacorum, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07900745.summary: A brief inscription likely marking a military boundary or possession belonging to the century of Carus Scipio.

EDCS-07900746.text_edition: \n centuria Floriani cohortis VIII legionis VI\n
EDCS-07900746.translation: The century of Florianus of Cohors VIII of Legio VI.
EDCS-07900746.summary: A military inscription identifying a specific century within Cohors VIII of Legio VI.

EDCS-07900755.translation: Securus of the century of Ap(---) Iustus.
EDCS-07900755.summary: A brief military inscription naming Securus and possibly a centurion, Ap(---) Iustus.

EDCS-07900761.translation: Apollonius Daminius, I did not want to give S(---).
EDCS-07900761.summary: A short inscription possibly recording a dispute or a refusal related to a debt or gift, attributed to Apollonius Daminius.

EDCS-07900765.text_edition: \n Deo Cocidio milites legionis XX Valeriae victricis votum solverunt libentes merito Apr et Ruf consulibus\n
EDCS-07900765.translation: Soldiers of Legio XX Valeria Victrix have gladly and deservedly paid their vow to the god Cocidius, under the consulship of Apr(---) and Ruf(---).
EDCS-07900765.summary: A votive inscription by soldiers of Legio XX Valeria Victrix to the god Cocidius, dated by consuls to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07900779.translation: The century of Iulius Tertullianus of Cohors VIII.
EDCS-07900779.summary: A military inscription identifying the century of Iulius Tertullianus within Cohors VIII.

EDCS-07900785.translation: An altar to the god Belatugra(grus), dedicated by Minervalis.
EDCS-07900785.summary: A dedication of an altar to the god Belatugra(grus) by Minervalis, likely from Roman Britain.

EDCS-07900786.text_edition: \n Deo sancto Belatucadro arulam donavit ullinus voto soluto\n
EDCS-07900786.translation: Ullinus, his vow fulfilled, dedicated this small altar to the holy god Belatucadrus.
EDCS-07900786.summary: A votive inscription recording Ullinus fulfilling a vow by dedicating a small altar to the god Belatucadrus, likely from Roman Britain.

EDCS-07900788.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo cohors IIII Gallorum equitata cui praeest Cassius Ir \n
EDCS-07900788.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, the Fourth Cohort of Gauls, cavalry, commanded by Cassius Ir.
EDCS-07900788.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter by the Fourth Cohort of Gauls, commanded by Cassius Ir, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-07900789.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo cohors IIII Gallorum cui praeest Volcacius Hospes praefectus equitum\n
EDCS-07900789.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, the Fourth Cohort of Gauls, commanded by Volcacius Hospes, prefect of cavalry.
EDCS-07900789.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter by the Fourth Cohort of Gauls, commanded by Volcacius Hospes, prefect of cavalry, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-07900790.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo cohors II Tungrorum |(milliaria) equitata coram laudata cui praeest Albius Severus praefectus Tungrorum instante Vic Severo principi\n
EDCS-07900790.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, the Second Cohort of Tungri, milliary and cavalry, praised in assembly, commanded by Albius Severus, prefect of the Tungri, at the urging of Vic Severus, principalis.
EDCS-07900790.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter by the Second Cohort of Tungri, a praised milliary and cavalry unit, commanded by Albius Severus, at the urging of Vic Severus, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-07900791.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo cohors II Tungrorum milliaria equitata civium liberorum cui praeest Aurelius Optatus praefectus Tungrorum instante Messio Opsequente sic principe\n
EDCS-07900791.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, the Second Cohort of Tungri, milliary and cavalry, of freeborn citizens, commanded by Aurelius Optatus, prefect of the Tungri, at the urging of Messius Opsequens, principalis.
EDCS-07900791.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter by the Second Cohort of Tungri, a milliary and cavalry unit of freeborn citizens, commanded by Aurelius Optatus, at the urging of Messius Opsequens, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-07900793.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo et Genio loci Gaius Verecundius Severus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-07900793.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest and the Spirit of the Place, Gaius Verecundius Severus fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-07900793.summary: This inscription records Gaius Verecundius Severus fulfilling a vow to Jupiter and the Genius of the place, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-07900795.text_edition: \n Deo San<c=G>(to) Marti Venustinius Lupus votum solvit libens merito \n
EDCS-07900795.translation: To the Sacred God Mars, Venustinius Lupus fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-07900795.summary: This inscription records Venustinius Lupus fulfilling a vow to the god Mars, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-07900797.text_edition: \n Deabus Matribus omnium gentium templum olim vetustate conlabsum sic Gaius Iulius Cupitianus |(centurio) praepositus restituit\n
EDCS-07900797.translation: To the Mother Goddesses of all peoples, Gaius Julius Cupitianus, centurion and prefect, restored the temple, which had long since collapsed from age.
EDCS-07900797.summary: This inscription records the restoration of a temple to the Mother Goddesses by Gaius Julius Cupitianus, centurion and prefect, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-07900800.text_edition: \n Numini Augusti deo Vanaunti Aurelius Armiger decurio princeps\n
EDCS-07900800.translation: To the Divine Augustus, to the god Vanauntius, Aurelius Armiger, decurion and princeps.
EDCS-07900800.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the Divine Augustus and the god Vanauntius by Aurelius Armiger, a decurion and princeps, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-07900801.text_edition: \n Deo Soli Invicto Sextus Severius Salvator praefectus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-07900801.translation: To the Invincible Sun God, Sextus Severius Salvator, prefect, fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-07900801.summary: This inscription records Sextus Severius Salvator, a prefect, fulfilling a vow to the unconquered Sun god, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-07900812.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Gemelli custodis armorum Flavius Hilario heres faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-07900812.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld, Flavius Hilario, the heir, arranged for this to be made for Gemellus, the keeper of arms.
EDCS-07900812.summary: This inscription records Flavius Hilario arranging for a funerary monument for Gemellus, keeper of arms, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-07900819.summary: This inscription records the century of a soldier named Romanus, likely serving as a boundary or ownership marker.

EDCS-07900822.summary: This inscription records the century of a soldier named Iulius Subso, likely serving as a boundary or ownership marker.

EDCS-07900825.text_edition: \n Legionis II Augustae centuria Iuli Tertulliani\n
EDCS-07900825.translation: The century of Iulius Tertullianus of the Second Augustan Legion.
EDCS-07900825.summary: This inscription records the century of Iulius Tertullianus from the Second Augustan Legion, likely serving as a boundary or ownership marker.

EDCS-07900828.summary: This inscription records the century of Trebonius Nepos, likely serving as a boundary or ownership marker.

EDCS-07900829.text_edition: \n Deo Cocidio milites legionis VI victricis piae fidelis\n
EDCS-07900829.translation: Soldiers of the Sixth Victorious, Pious, and Loyal Legion, to the god Cocidius.
EDCS-07900829.summary: This inscription records a dedication by soldiers of the Sixth Victorious, Pious, and Loyal Legion to the deity Cocidius.

EDCS-07900832.text_edition: \n Cohortis IIII principis posterioris centuria Iuli Vitalis\n
EDCS-07900832.translation: The century of Iulius Vitalis of the Fourth Cohort, Princeps Posterior.
EDCS-07900832.summary: This inscription records the century of Iulius Vitalis from the Fourth Cohort, Princeps Posterior, likely serving as a boundary or ownership marker.

EDCS-07900833.text_edition: \n Marti Cocidio milites legionis II Augustae centuria Sanctiana centuria Secundini dono solverunt sub cura Aeliani curavit Oppius Felix optio\n
EDCS-07900833.translation: Soldiers of the Second Augustan Legion, the Sanctian century, and the century of Secundinus, gave this offering to Mars Cocidius, and fulfilled their vow under the supervision of Aelianus; Oppius Felix, optio, saw to its preparation.
EDCS-07900833.summary: This inscription records soldiers from two centuries of the Second Augustan Legion dedicating an offering to Mars Cocidius, with supervision and completion details.

EDCS-07900838.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marci Troiani Augustini titulum faciendum curavit Aelia Ammillusima coniux karissima\n
EDCS-07900838.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. His dearest wife, Aelia Ammillusima, arranged for this monument to be made for Marcus Troianus Augustinus.
EDCS-07900838.summary: This funerary inscription records Aelia Ammillusima commissioning a monument for her deceased husband, Marcus Troianus Augustinus.

EDCS-07900848.text_edition: \n Deo Belatocadro Antronius Aufidianus posuit aram pro se et suis\n
EDCS-07900848.translation: Antronius Aufidianus set up this altar to the god Belatocadros for himself and his family.
EDCS-07900848.summary: This inscription records Antronius Aufidianus dedicating an altar to the god Belatocadros for his family's well-being.

EDCS-07900850.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo cohors I Nervana Germanorum milliaria equitata cui praeest Publius Tuscilius CLND asinianus tribunus \n
EDCS-07900850.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest. The First Cohort of Nervani Germans, milliary and cavalry, which is commanded by Publius Tuscilius Asinianus, tribunus (tribune) of the CLND (cohort), has dedicated this.
EDCS-07900850.summary: This inscription records the First Cohort of Nervani Germans dedicating something to Jupiter, identifying their commander, Publius Tuscilius Asinianus.

EDCS-07900851.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo et numinibus Auggustorum genio numeri Maurorum Aurelianorum Valeriani Gallienique Caelius Vibianus tribunus cohortis praepositus numeri supra scripti instante Iulio Rufino principe\n
EDCS-07900851.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, and to the divine powers of the Emperors, and to the genius of the unit of the Maurei of Aurelianus, Valerianus, and Gallienus, Caelius Vibianus, tribune of the cohort and prefect of the aforementioned unit, dedicated this, at the urging of Iulius Rufinus, princeps.
EDCS-07900851.summary: This inscription records a dedication by a military officer to Jupiter and the emperors' divine powers on behalf of his unit.

EDCS-07900852.translation: Lucius Ursei to the goddess Latia.
EDCS-07900852.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Lucius Ursei to the goddess Latia.

EDCS-07900854.text_edition: \n Balatucadro sancto Censorinus pro salute sua et suorum posuit\n
EDCS-07900854.translation: Censorinus dedicated this for the health of himself and his family to Balatucadro.
EDCS-07900854.summary: This inscription records a votive dedication by Censorinus to the deity Balatucadro for his well-being and that of his household.

EDCS-07900855.summary: This inscription simply records the name and origin of Iulius Piitinus.

EDCS-07900865.text_edition: \n Deo Belatocairo Peisius miles solvit votum libens merito\n
EDCS-07900865.translation: Peisius the soldier gladly fulfilled his vow to the god Belatocairus, as was fitting.
EDCS-07900865.summary: This inscription records a soldier named Peisius fulfilling a vow to the god Belatocairus.

EDCS-07900866.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salute dominorum nostrorum Galli et Volusiani Augustorum Sulpicius Secundianus tribunus cohortis posuit\n
EDCS-07900866.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, for the health of our lords Gallus and Volusianus Augusti, Sulpicius Secundianus, tribune of the cohort, dedicated this.
EDCS-07900866.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Sulpicius Secundianus, a military tribune, to Jupiter for the health of the emperors Gallus and Volusianus.

EDCS-07900868.text_edition: \n Matribus deabus aedem Antonianus dedico sed date ut fetura quaestus suppleat votis fidem aureis sacrabo carmen mox viritim litteris\n
EDCS-07900868.translation: Antonianus dedicates this temple to the Mother Goddesses, but grant that she may fulfill the abundance of my profits with vows; I shall consecrate it with gold and songs soon, man by man, with letters.
EDCS-07900868.summary: This inscription is a dedication of a temple to the Mother Goddesses by Antonianus, who requests divine favor for his prosperity.

EDCS-07900869.text_edition: \n MARC AVRELLIVS IMPERA TRIVMPH PERSA MARC AVREL PHILO\n
EDCS-07900869.translation: Emperor Marcus Aurelius, victor in Persia; Marcus Aurelius Philo.
EDCS-07900869.summary: This inscription appears to record the titles of Emperor Marcus Aurelius and possibly a person named Marcus Aurelius Philo.

EDCS-07900872.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo cohors IIII Gallorum et Naevius Hilarus praefectus curam agente Firm\n
EDCS-07900872.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, the Fourth Cohort of Gauls, and Naevius Hilarus, prefect, under the supervision of Firmus.
EDCS-07900872.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter by the Fourth Cohort of Gauls and its prefect, Naevius Hilarus.

EDCS-07900873.text_edition: \n Deo Mapono et numini Augusti Durio et Ramio et Trupo et Lurio Germani votum solverunt libentes merito\n
EDCS-07900873.translation: To the god Maponus and the divine power of the Emperor, Durio, Ramio, Trupo, and Lurio, Germans, gladly fulfilled their vow, as was fitting.
EDCS-07900873.summary: This inscription records four German individuals fulfilling a vow to the god Maponus and the emperor's divine power.

EDCS-07900875.translation: Apollonius, the priest, made this for the goddess Nemesis.
EDCS-07900875.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a monument to the goddess Nemesis by Apollonius, a priest.

EDCS-07900876.text_edition: \n Deae Nymphae Brigantiae quod voverat pro salute et incolumitate domini nostri Invicti imperatoris Marci Aureli Severi Antonini Pii Felicis Augusti totiusque domus divinae eius Marcus Cocceius Nigrinus procurator Augusti nostri devotissimus numini maiestatique eius votum solvit libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-07900876.translation: Marcus Cocceius Nigrinus, a devoted procurator of our emperor, willingly, joyfully, and deservedly fulfilled the vow for the safety and well-being of our lord, the invincible emperor Marcus Aurelius Severus Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, and his entire divine family, to the goddess Nympha Brigantia.
EDCS-07900876.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled to the goddess Nympha Brigantia by Marcus Cocceius Nigrinus, a procurator, for the safety of Emperor Marcus Aurelius Severus Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus and his family.

EDCS-07900881.text_edition: \n Sive deo sive deae Claudius Marcianus praefectus \n
EDCS-07900881.translation: Claudius Marcianus, the prefect, for a god or goddess.
EDCS-07900881.summary: This inscription records an offering made by Claudius Marcianus, a prefect, to an unspecified deity.

EDCS-07900887.text_edition: \n Legionis XX Valeriae victricis cohortis IIII Liburni Frontonis Terenti Magni legio XX\n
EDCS-07900887.translation: Of the Fourth Cohort of the Twentieth Legion Valeria Victrix, of Liburnus Frontonius Terentius Magnus, Legion XX.
EDCS-07900887.summary: This inscription likely indicates the origin or belonging of an object or structure to the Fourth Cohort of the Twentieth Legion Valeria Victrix, possibly associated with Liburnus Frontonius Terentius Magnus.

EDCS-07900901.text_edition: \n Brigantiae sacrum Amandus architectus ex imperio imperatum\n
EDCS-07900901.translation: A sacred offering to Brigantia, commissioned by Amandus the architect by order.
EDCS-07900901.summary: This inscription records a sacred offering to the goddess Brigantia, commissioned by Amandus the architect.

EDCS-07900904.text_edition: \n Fortunae pro salute Publi Campani Italici praefecti cohortis II Tungrorum Celer libertus votum solvit libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-07900904.translation: Celer, a freedman, willingly, joyfully, and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Fortuna for the safety of Publius Campanius Italicus, prefect of the Second Cohort of Tungrians.
EDCS-07900904.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled to Fortuna by Celer, a freedman, for the safety of Publius Campanius Italicus, prefect of the Second Cohort of Tungrians.

EDCS-07900906.text_edition: \n Deae Harimellae sacrum Gamidiahus architectus votum solvit libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-07900906.translation: A sacred offering to the goddess Harimella; Gamidiahus the architect willingly, joyfully, and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07900906.summary: This inscription records a sacred offering to the goddess Harimella, with the vow fulfilled by Gamidiahus the architect.

EDCS-07900907.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo cohors I Nervana Germanorum |(milliaria) equitata cui praeest Lucius Faenius Felix tribunus\n
EDCS-07900907.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, the First Cohort of Nervanian Germans, equipped with legionary standards and cavalry, commanded by Lucius Faenius Felix, tribune.
EDCS-07900907.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by the First Cohort of Nervanian Germans (equitata milliaria), commanded by Tribune Lucius Faenius Felix.

EDCS-07900908.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo iainius i fecit pro \n
EDCS-07900908.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Ianius I made this for...
EDCS-07900908.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Ianius I, with the purpose incomplete.

EDCS-07900910.text_edition: \n Marti et Victoriae Augusti cives Raeti militantes in cohorte II Tungrorum cui praeest Silvius Auspex praefectus votum solverunt libentes merito\n
EDCS-07900910.translation: To Mars and Victoria Augusta, the Raetian citizens serving in the Second Cohort of Tungrians, commanded by Silvius Auspex, prefect, fulfilled their vows willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-07900910.summary: This inscription records vows fulfilled to Mars and Victoria Augusta by Raetian soldiers of the Second Cohort of Tungrians, commanded by Prefect Silvius Auspex.

EDCS-07900912.text_edition: \n Deo Mercurio Iulius Crescens sigillum collignio cultorum eius de suo dedit votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-07900912.translation: To the god Mercury, Julius Crescens, a member of the college of his worshippers, dedicated a statue at his own expense, gladly and deservedly fulfilling his vow.
EDCS-07900912.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a statue to the god Mercury by Julius Crescens, a member of a worshipper's college, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-07900913.text_edition: \n Numini Augusti deo Mercurio signum posuerunt cultores colligni eiusdem dei curante Ingenuio Rufo votum solverunt libentes merito\n
EDCS-07900913.translation: The worshippers of the divine Augustus and of Mercury erected a statue, under the care of Ingenuius Rufus, gladly and deservedly fulfilling their vow.
EDCS-07900913.summary: This inscription records the erection of a statue to the divine Augustus and Mercury by his worshippers, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-07900914.text_edition: \n Deae Minervae cohors II Tungrorum milliaria equitata coram laudata cui praeest Gaius Silvius Auspex praefectus\n
EDCS-07900914.translation: To the goddess Minerva, from the Second Cohort of Tungrians, a milliary, equestrian unit, which was praised, under the command of Gaius Silvius Auspex, prefect.
EDCS-07900914.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the goddess Minerva by the Second Cohort of Tungrians, an equestrian unit, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07900915.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription dedicated to the god Neptune by Claudius, probably from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07900917.text_edition: \n Deae Ricagambedae pagus Vellaus militans cohorte II Tungrorum votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-07900917.translation: To the goddess Ricagambeda, the pagus of Vellaus, serving in the Second Cohort of Tungrians, gladly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
